name: Affenpinscher
temperament: Confident, alert, playful, loyal, courageous
description: Small, sturdy toy breed with a distinctive monkey-like expression and shaggy, wiry coat. Known for its confident, terrier-like personality despite its small size.
history: Originating in 17th-century Germany, bred down from larger terriers to be skilled ratters in homes, kitchens and stables. Refined in Munich and Berlin, recognized by AKC in 1936. Name translates to 'monkey-like terrier'.

name: Afghan Hound
temperament: Independent, dignified, aloof, loyal, confident
description: Ancient, elegant sighthound with dramatic, flowing silky coat and aristocratic bearing, bred for hunting large prey in mountainous terrain
history: Ancient sighthound originating in the mountainous regions of Afghanistan, where they were prized for hunting large prey like gazelles and leopards. Valued for their speed, stamina, and problem-solving intelligence over rugged terrain. Introduced to the Western world by British officers in the early 20th century.

name: Africanis
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, alert, adaptable, independent
description: Medium-sized, athletic landrace dog indigenous to Southern Africa with variable appearance and short coat, naturally evolved through centuries of selection by African communities and adapted to harsh environments
history: Ancient landrace breed indigenous to Southern Africa, naturally developed by African communities over thousands of years through natural selection rather than deliberate breeding. Recognized by the Kennel Union of Southern Africa as an emerging breed but not by major international kennel clubs.

name: Aidi
temperament: Alert, protective, independent, loyal, courageous, intelligent, energetic
description: Medium to large, powerful and muscular Moroccan mountain dog with thick, weather-resistant double coat bred as a livestock guardian and protector in the rugged Atlas Mountains
history: Ancient Berber breed from the Atlas Mountains of Morocco, used for centuries by Berber tribes as a livestock guardian protecting herds of sheep and goats from predators. Valued for its courage, adaptability, and keen senses in protecting property and tents.

name: Airedale Terrier
temperament: Confident, intelligent, courageous, alert, energetic, outgoing
description: The largest of all terrier breeds, the Airedale is a large, athletic dog with a distinctive wiry black and tan coat and confident, alert demeanor. Known as the 'King of Terriers', this highly intelligent and versatile breed combines courage with an outgoing temperament.
history: Developed in the Aire Valley of Yorkshire, England, by crossing Otterhounds with various terrier breeds. Originally bred by working-class fanciers to hunt otters and rats, their intelligence and courage led to their use as police dogs, war dogs, and all-around companions.

name: Airedoodle
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, courageous, loyal, friendly, alert
description: Large, intelligent crossbreed resulting from crossing an Airedale Terrier with a Standard Poodle, combining the terrier's confident, courageous nature with the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat qualities
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the US by crossing the versatile, courageous Airedale Terrier with the intelligent Standard Poodle. The goal was to combine the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat with the Airedale's confidence and sturdiness. Not recognized by major kennel clubs

name: Akbash
temperament: Independent, protective, calm, alert, loyal, intelligent
description: Large, powerful, all-white livestock guardian dog from Turkey with lean athletic build, known for its independent nature and strong protective instincts. The distinctive white coat helped shepherds distinguish it from predators at a distance.
history: Ancient breed developed over 3,000 years ago in western Turkey for guarding livestock, particularly in the Akbash region. The white coat helped shepherds distinguish the dogs from predators. Introduced to North America in the 1970s for predator control programs.

name: Akita
temperament: Courageous, loyal, dignified, independent, protective, alert
description: A large, powerful spitz-type dog of Japanese origin with a bear-like head, dense double coat, and full curled tail. Known for its dignified presence, imposing stature, and courageous, loyal temperament.
history: Ancient Japanese breed developed in the mountainous Akita prefecture for hunting large game including wild boar and bear. Revered in Japanese culture as a symbol of health, happiness, and good fortune. The breed was nearly lost after World War II but was preserved by dedicated breeders and designated as a national treasure in Japan.

name: Aksaray Malaklisi
temperament: Protective, loyal, independent, calm, alert, courageous, intelligent
description: A giant and powerful livestock guardian dog from the Aksaray region of Turkey, characterized by its massive frame, muscular build, and pendulous lips from which it gets its name ('Malakli' meaning 'with lips'). Known for exceptional size and protective instincts.
history: Ancient Turkish breed developed as a landrace in the Aksaray region of central Turkey for livestock protection against large predators such as wolves and bears. Closely related to Turkish mastiff and guardian breeds, recognized by Turkish cynology organizations as distinct from the Kangal Shepherd Dog

name: Alano Español
temperament: Loyal, courageous, protective, intelligent, confident
description: A large, athletic Molosser-type dog with a powerful, agile build and brachycephalic head. This robust Spanish breed has a short coat, serious expression, and strong working instincts, historically used as a catch dog for large game and cattle work.
history: Ancient Spanish Molosser breed tied to dogs brought by Alani tribes in 5th century. Traditionally used for hunting large game, cattle herding, and guard work. Nearly extinct but recovered in late 20th century from remaining specimens in rural Spain.

name: Alapaha Blue Blood Bulldog
temperament: Loyal, protective, intelligent, confident, alert
origin: Alapaha River region of southern Georgia, United States
description: A powerful, athletic bulldog breed with distinctive blue merle or patched coat patterns and muscular build. Originally developed as a 'catch dog' for farm work, livestock protection, and family guardian with strong protective instincts.
history: Developed in the Alapaha River region of southern Georgia as a multi-purpose catch dog for working cattle and hogs, and as a family guardian. Descended from Old English Bulldogs and recreated the original plantation bulldogs of the 1800s, maintained by the Lane family.

name: Alaskan Husky
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, work-focused, athletic, friendly, independent
description: Medium-sized athletic sled dog bred for performance rather than appearance, with variable coat colors and exceptional endurance. Built for speed and arctic conditions, this working dog represents a blend of various Northern breeds and pointers optimized for sled racing.
history: Developed in Alaska as a purpose-bred sled dog through selective breeding of various northern breeds (Siberian Husky, Alaskan Malamute, Inuit dogs) with European breeds like Pointers, Greyhounds, and Salukis to create the ideal combination of speed, endurance, and work ethic for sled racing

name: Alaskan Klee Kai
temperament: Alert, intelligent, energetic, loyal, curious, playful
description: Small to medium spitz-type dog resembling a miniature Alaskan Husky with striking masked face, erect ears, curled tail, and lively, alert personality
history: Developed in the 1970s by Linda S. Spurlin in Alaska to create a companion-sized version of the Alaskan Husky. Foundation stock included Siberian and Alaskan Huskies, with potential crosses of Schipperke and American Eskimo Dog to reduce size. Recognized by UKC but not currently AKC recognized.

name: Alaskan Malamute
temperament: Affectionate, friendly, loyal, playful, intelligent, energetic, outgoing
description: An immensely strong, heavy-duty freighting dog of spitz type, the Alaskan Malamute is a large, powerful Arctic sled dog with a thick double coat, deep chest, well-muscled body, wolf-like appearance, and characteristically plumed tail carried over the back
history: One of the oldest Arctic sled dogs, developed by the native Mahlemut Inuit tribe of northwestern Alaska. Essential for survival, used for hauling heavy freight across Arctic terrain, hunting large predators like seals and polar bears

name: Alopekis
temperament: Intelligent, alert, loyal, adaptable, energetic, playful
description: Small, fox-like Greek primitive landrace breed with wedge-shaped head and agile build, historically used as an all-purpose farm dog for companionship, watching, and vermin control
history: Ancient Greek landrace breed with depictions found on ancient pottery, traditionally used as versatile farm companion for vermin control and watchdog duties. Kept for thousands of years in rural Greek communities, with current preservation and standardization efforts ongoing for official recognition.

name: Alpine Dachsbracke
temperament: Intelligent, courageous, loyal, work-focused, energetic
description: Sturdy, short-legged Austrian scent hound with dense double coat, originally bred for tracking wounded game in high-altitude Alpine terrain. Built low-to-the-ground with a robust, weather-resistant build designed for rugged mountainous conditions.
history: Developed in the Austrian Alps during the mid-19th century as a durable scent hound for tracking wounded deer, boar, and hare over rugged terrain. Descended from larger Austrian hounds crossed with Dachshunds, it was a favorite of Crown Prince Rudolf of Habsburg and is officially recognized by the FCI.

name: American Bulldog
temperament: Confident, loyal, protective, energetic, affectionate, courageous
description: A powerful, athletic, short-coated dog of the molosser type with a broad head and muscular build, known for its confidence, strength, and loyalty as a family companion and guardian. Originally bred for farm work and protection.
history: Descended from Old English Bulldogs brought by working-class immigrants to the American South. Developed as versatile all-around farm utility dogs for guarding property, catching livestock, and protection. The breed was preserved from near extinction after WWII by breeders John D. Johnson and Alan Scott.

name: American Bully
temperament: Confident, gentle, loyal, affectionate, outgoing, eager to please
description: A compact, muscular companion breed with a powerfully built body, broad head, and confident demeanor. Known for its gentle, affectionate nature and loyalty to family, the American Bully was developed from American Pit Bull Terriers and other bully breeds to create an excellent companion dog with a stable temperament.
history: Developed in the United States between the 1980s and 1990s as a companion breed through selective breeding of American Pit Bull Terriers, American Staffordshire Terriers, and other bulldog-type breeds to produce a dog with a gentler temperament and more muscular, 'bullier' physique. Recognized by the United Kennel Club in 2013.

name: American Cocker Spaniel
temperament: Gentle, smart, happy, outgoing, affectionate
description: The smallest member of the Sporting Group, the American Cocker Spaniel is a medium-sized sporting dog with a beautifully chiseled head, long flowing silky coat, long ears, and gentle expression
history: Developed in the United States from English Cocker Spaniels brought over in the late 1800s. American breeders selected for smaller size, more refined head, and heavier coat for woodcock hunting, leading to recognition as a separate breed by the AKC in 1946

name: American English Coonhound
temperament: Energetic, friendly, intelligent, alert, work-focused
description: Athletic medium to large scent hound with excellent tracking ability, swift and powerful build, and a hard, protective short coat. Bred for treeing raccoons and fox, known for speed, endurance, and distinctive melodious baying.
history: Descended from English Foxhounds brought to American colonies in the 17th and 18th centuries. Developed by breeders in the American South for hunting raccoons and foxes in rougher terrain, recognized by AKC in 2011

name: American Eskimo Dog
temperament: Intelligent, alert, friendly, playful, energetic, eager to please
description: Compact spitz-type companion dog with distinctive dense white double coat, black points on lips, nose, and eye rims, wedge-shaped head, and plumed tail carried over the back. Available in three sizes (toy, miniature, standard) with an alert expression and striking appearance.
history: Descended from white German Spitz dogs brought to America by German immigrants. Name changed from German Spitz to American Eskimo Dog during World War I due to anti-German sentiment. Became popular circus performers in the 19th and early 20th centuries, recognized by AKC in 1994

name: American Foxhound
temperament: Independent, friendly, gentle, energetic, intelligent
description: A sleek, powerful scenthound with a long, fine-boned body and athletic build, known for incredible stamina, speed, and melodious baying voice. One of the oldest American-bred dogs, developed for endurance and pack hunting.
history: Developed from English Foxhounds imported to Colonial America in the 1650s, refined for American terrain and fox hunting. George Washington was a key figure in the breed's development, breeding them to be lighter, taller, and faster for hunting American red fox over varied terrain.

name: American Hairless Terrier
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, friendly, playful, curious
description: Small to medium-sized, active and inquisitive terrier that comes in both hairless and coated varieties, notable for its alert expression, compact build, and friendly, intelligent nature
history: Originated in Louisiana in 1972 when a hairless puppy named Josephine was born in a litter of Rat Terriers. Developed by Edwin and Willie Scott, recognized by UKC in 1999 and AKC in 2016

name: American Leopard Hound
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, work-focused, protective, athletic
description: Medium to large athletic scent hound known for its distinctive leopard-spotted or patched coat pattern and exceptional tracking ability. A tough, powerful, and driven American tree and trailing hound with strong hunting instincts and versatile hunting capabilities.
history: One of the oldest American tree hound breeds, developed in North Carolina from dogs brought by Spanish conquistadors crossed with other scent hounds. Officially recognized by United Kennel Club in 1960s, bred for exceptional trailing and treeing abilities on variety of game.

name: American Pit Bull Terrier
temperament: Confident, intelligent, loyal, affectionate, eager to please, energetic
description: Medium-sized, muscular terrier with athletic build and short coat, known for its strength, confidence, intelligence, and affectionate nature towards people. Possesses great strength for its size and is both athletic and agile.
history: Developed in the late 19th century from English bull-and-terrier breeds brought to America. Originally bred for bull-baiting and later refined as all-around farm dogs and loyal family companions. First recognized by the United Kennel Club in 1898.

name: American Staffordshire Terrier
temperament: Confident, courageous, loyal, intelligent, smart
description: A muscular, stocky, but agile medium-sized terrier of remarkable strength with a broad head, strong build, smooth coat and confident, good-natured personality
history: Developed in the 19th century from crosses of bulldogs and terriers brought to America in the 1800s. Originally bred for bull-baiting and pit fighting, later refined to be larger than English Staffordshire Bull Terriers and developed into all-purpose farm dogs and family companions. Recognized by the AKC in 1936.

name: American Water Spaniel
temperament: Eager to please, intelligent, energetic, alert, happy
description: Medium-sized versatile sporting dog with distinctive curly or wavy chocolate or liver coat, bred for hunting waterfowl in the icy waters and marshy banks of the Great Lakes region
history: Developed in the 19th century in the Fox and Wolf River valleys of Wisconsin as a compact, versatile hunter that could work from a skiff or boat. Ancestry likely includes Irish Water Spaniel and Curly-Coated Retriever. Named the state dog of Wisconsin in 1985 and foundation breed for the Boykin Spaniel, recognized by AKC in 1940.

name: Andalusian Terrier
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, alert, loyal, courageous, playful
description: A slender, medium-sized Spanish working terrier with a predominantly white coat and characteristic head markings, known for its lively, athletic nature and energetic, alert temperament
history: Developed in the late 19th and early 20th centuries in the Andalusia region of Spain, particularly in the province of Cádiz. Originated from crosses between Fox Terriers brought by English wine merchants and local ratter dogs used to control vermin in wineries (bodegas). Traditional Spanish working terrier developed for vermin control, hunting, and farm work; recognized by regional and national breed clubs in Spain though not by AKC or FCI

name: Anglo-Français de Petite Vénerie
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, alert, loyal, work-focused, devoted
description: Medium-sized, elegant and well-balanced French scenthound with a short tricolor coat, bred for hunting small game in packs with excellent scenting ability and strong pack hunting instincts
history: Developed in France by crossing English Foxhounds and Harriers with various French scenthound breeds like the Poitevin and Porcelaine to create efficient pack hounds suited for 'petite vénerie' - the hunting of small game

name: Appenzeller Sennenhund
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, confident, loyal, alert, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, muscular and agile Swiss mountain dog with distinctive tricolor coat and tightly curled tail carried over the back. Known for their lively, self-assured expression and work-focused temperament, this ancient breed combines herding instincts with guarding abilities.
history: One of the four Swiss Sennenhund breeds, this ancient breed from the Appenzell region was used for centuries as an all-purpose farm dog for herding cattle, guarding property, and pulling carts. Formally established in the late 19th century through the efforts of forester Max Siber and Professor Albert Heim.

name: Argentine Pila
temperament: Affectionate, alert, playful, gentle, intelligent
description: An elegant and slender hairless dog native to Argentina, known for its soft, warm skin and lively, alert temperament. The breed comes in three size varieties and is prized as a companion dog.
history: An ancient hairless breed native to Argentina, believed to have descended from dogs brought by pre-Incan peoples. Related to other South American hairless breeds and traditionally kept as a companion for perceived therapeutic warmth among indigenous peoples.

name: Ariégeois
temperament: Gentle, calm, intelligent, loyal, affectionate, friendly
description: Medium-sized, elegant French scenthound with distinctive tricolor coat, lean body, long ears, and light limbs. Bred for pack-hunting hare and deer, known for keen nose, stamina, and scent-tracking abilities.
history: Developed in the Ariège region of southern France in the early 20th century by crossing local briquet hounds with Grand Bleu de Gascogne and Grand Gascon-Saintongeois to create a versatile and durable pack hound for hunting hare

name: Ariège Pointer
temperament: Intelligent, gentle, docile, loyal, work-focused, adaptable
description: Medium to large, elegant and muscular French pointing dog with distinctive orange and white coat pattern and fine nose, known for its endurance, docile nature, and versatile hunting abilities
history: Originated in the Ariège region of southern France in the 19th century, descended from old French pointing breeds crossed with Spanish Pointer and other southern pointing breeds. Faced near extinction after World War II but was revived by passionate breeders in the 1990s.

name: Armant
temperament: Loyal, courageous, intelligent, protective, alert
description: Medium-sized Egyptian herding dog with a sturdy, well-muscled build and distinctive shaggy, wiry coat. Known for being courageous, protective, and alert with an intelligent expression.
history: Named after the town of Armant along the Nile in Egypt, believed to have descended from European herding dogs, possibly Briards, brought by Napoleon's armies and crossed with native Egyptian dogs. Developed as versatile livestock guardians and herding dogs, officially recognized in Egypt as the national herding breed.

name: Armenian Gampr
temperament: Protective, independent, loyal, calm, intelligent
description: Large, powerful ancient livestock guardian dog from the Armenian Highlands, known for its thick double coat, formidable protective instincts, and independent temperament developed through millennia of natural selection
history: Ancient landrace breed native to the Armenian Highlands, used for millennia as livestock and home guardian protecting against predators and intruders, developed through natural selection rather than selective breeding for appearance

name: Artois Hound
temperament: Energetic, loyal, alert, courageous, calm, affectionate
description: Medium-sized French scent hound with distinctive short, dense tricolor coat and powerful build. Known for its endurance, strong hunting instincts, and calm yet energetic temperament.
history: Ancient French scenthound from the Artois region dating back to the 15th century, descended from the St. Hubert Hound. Used by nobility for hunting, the breed faced near extinction after World War II but was revived in the 1970s by dedicated breeders following historical standards

name: Aussiedoodle
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, friendly, loyal, affectionate, playful
description: Designer crossbreed combining Australian Shepherd and Poodle traits, known for high intelligence, energetic nature, and often a low-shedding coat with varied coat types
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the late 20th century/1990s in the United States by crossing Australian Shepherd and Poodle to create a highly intelligent low-shedding companion dog

name: Australian Cattle Dog
temperament: Intelligent, alert, loyal, energetic, courageous
description: Compact, muscular working dog with distinctive coat markings and exceptional endurance for cattle herding; known as the Heeler
history: Developed in 19th century Australia by crossing British herding dogs including blue merle Collies with native Dingo, with later infusions of Dalmatian and Black and Tan Kelpie to create a hardy cattle herding dog for harsh Australian conditions

name: Australian Kelpie
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, work-focused, alert
description: Medium-sized athletic herding dog with exceptional energy and intelligence, developed for working sheep over vast distances in harsh Australian conditions. Known for their lithe, agile build and inexhaustible work ethic.
history: Developed in Australia during the late 19th century from crosses of imported Scottish Collies with Dingoes and other working dogs to create the ultimate sheep herding dog capable of working independently over vast distances in harsh climates

name: Australian Shepherd
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, work-focused, loyal, devoted, athletic
description: Medium-sized, agile herding dog with striking multicolored coat, expressive eyes, and keen herding instincts. Known for their intense work drive and weather-resistant double coat.
history: Despite its name, the Australian Shepherd was developed in the American West in the 19th century from Basque shepherd dogs brought by immigrants from the Pyrenees region. The breed's intelligence and versatility made it popular on the rodeo circuit and for ranching work.

name: Australian Silky Terrier
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, alert, energetic, confident
description: A small, spirited toy terrier with a refined but substantial build, characterized by its long, straight, glossy blue-and-tan silky coat that is parted down the middle and lively, alert demeanor
history: Developed in Australia in the late 19th century by crossing Yorkshire Terriers with Australian Terriers to create a small companion dog with a long, silky coat, combining hardiness with refined features and vermin hunting ability

name: Australian Stumpy Tail Cattle Dog
temperament: Loyal, alert, energetic, intelligent, courageous, independent, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, rugged working dog with naturally short tail, bred for herding cattle in harsh Australian conditions. This compact, athletic breed combines intelligence, stamina, and exceptional herding prowess.
history: Developed in the early-to-mid 1800s by Australian cattlemen crossing British herding dogs (such as the Smithfield) with native Australian dingo-type dogs. Bred specifically for working ability and natural bob-tail, developing parallel to the Australian Cattle Dog as an independent herding breed.

name: Australian Terrier
temperament: Courageous, alert, friendly, loyal, intelligent
description: Small, sturdy terrier with harsh double coat and distinctive topknot, known for its spirited, alert expression and courageous personality
history: Developed in Australia during the 19th century from British terrier breeds including Cairn, Skye, and Yorkshire Terriers. Bred as all-purpose exterminators of rodents and snakes, as well as loyal watchdogs and companions. Recognized by AKC in 1960.

name: Austrian Black and Tan Hound
temperament: Intelligent, friendly, energetic, work-focused, independent, loyal
description: Medium-sized, athletic scent hound with a smooth black coat and distinctive rich tan markings, renowned for its stamina, keen sense of smell, and elegant build. Developed specifically for tracking game in mountainous Alpine terrain.
history: Descended from ancient Celtic Hounds (Keltenbracke), developed over centuries in Austria from Celtic Hounds and local hunting breeds. Modern form established in mid-19th century as a versatile and durable scenthound for tracking wounded game, primarily hare and fox, in high-altitude Alpine terrain.

name: Austrian Pinscher
temperament: Alert, protective, loyal, energetic, confident, intelligent
description: Medium-sized, sturdy farm dog with robust build, distinctive pear-shaped head, and dense double coat, originally developed as a versatile guardian and ratter with strong protective instincts
history: Descended from old Austrian farm dogs of the 18th century, developed as versatile all-purpose farm dog for guarding livestock and controlling vermin. Systematic breeding began in 1921 with official recognition in 1928. Nearly extinct post-WWII and by the 1970s, saved through dedicated breeding programs using remaining bloodlines.

name: Azawakh
temperament: Independent, loyal, alert, protective, affectionate, aloof
origin: West Africa (Sahel region: Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso)
description: Elegant, tall sighthound from West Africa with a lean, muscular build, fine coat, and long legs built for speed and endurance in desert conditions of the Sahel region
history: Ancient sighthound breed developed by nomadic Tuareg peoples of the Sahel region for hunting gazelle and other game, protecting livestock, and serving as companions. Bred for speed and stamina in Saharan and Sahelian conditions

name: Bắc Hà
temperament: Alert, loyal, intelligent, protective, independent
origin: Bắc Hà District, Lào Cai Province, northern Vietnam
description: Medium-sized spitz-type dog from the mountainous regions of northern Vietnam, known for its dense double coat, wedge-shaped head, and distinctive lion-like mane. Alert and loyal breed adapted to cold mountain climates.
history: Indigenous spitz-type breed developed by ethnic minority groups, particularly the Hmong people, in the mountainous Bắc Hà region of northern Vietnam. Traditionally used for hunting, guarding, and companionship. Officially recognized by the Vietnam Kennel Association in 2019.

name: Bakharwal Dog
temperament: Loyal, protective, intelligent, courageous, independent
description: Large, powerful mountain dog bred for guarding livestock in the Himalayas, featuring a dense double coat and strong protective instincts
history: Ancient livestock guardian of the Bakharwal and Gujjar tribes, used for centuries in the Himalayas for flock protection

name: Banjara Hound
temperament: Independent, loyal, alert, intelligent, courageous
description: Ancient, lean athletic sighthound landrace native to India, traditionally used by nomadic Banjara people for hunting game over harsh terrain. Known for endurance, loyalty, and speed, bred for function over form.
history: Ancient landrace sighthound developed by the Banjara nomadic tribes of India, primarily in the Maharashtra region. For centuries bred for hunting deer, hare, and medium game over open terrain, also serving as flock guardian and family protector. Not recognized by major international kennel clubs but established in regional registries.

name: Bankhar Dog
temperament: Protective, independent, loyal, calm, alert, intelligent
description: Large, powerful ancient livestock guardian dog from Mongolia with thick double coat and independent, protective temperament. This landrace breed is adapted to survive and work in harsh climates.
history: Ancient landrace livestock guardian breed developed by nomadic Mongolian herders for over 15,000 years to protect livestock from wolves snow leopards and other predators. Modern conservation efforts by organizations like the Mongolian Bankhar Dog Project work to preserve and reintroduce the breed to traditional working roles.

name: Barak Hound
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, alert, independent, courageous
description: Medium-sized rough-coated scent hound with distinctive tricolor coat, bred for hunting in mountainous terrain across the Balkans
history: Descended from local hounds in the Balkans, developed for hunting wild boar and game in mountainous terrain. Recognized by FCI as the Bosnian Coarse-haired Hound (Bosanski Oštrodlaki Gonič – Barak)

name: Barbado da Terceira
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, docile, playful, courageous, alert
description: Medium-sized, rugged Portuguese herding dog from the Azores with a distinctive long wavy coat and beard, known for its intelligence, docile nature, and lively disposition developed for cattle herding
history: Ancient indigenous breed developed on Terceira Island in the Azores by Portuguese settlers for herding and guarding cattle. Nearly faced extinction in the 20th century before conservation and revival efforts in the 1990s led to its preservation and recognition

name: Barbet
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, loyal, playful, athletic, devoted
description: Medium-sized French water dog with distinctive long, dense, curly coat and webbed feet, bred for waterfowl retrieval. Known for its rustic, athletic build and joyful, intelligent disposition.
history: Ancient French water dog dating to 16th century, used for centuries to locate, flush and retrieve waterfowl in marshes and rivers. The breed's name derives from 'barbe' meaning beard. Nearly extinct after WWI but revived through dedicated breeding programs. Considered ancestor to several modern breeds including the Poodle and Briard. Officially recognized by the AKC in 2020.

name: Basenji
temperament: Independent, intelligent, alert, energetic, curious, affectionate
description: Small, elegant sighthound from Central Africa with a tightly curled tail, wrinkled forehead, and smooth coat. Known as the 'barkless dog' for its unique yodel-like vocalizations instead of traditional barking.
history: Ancient breed originating in Central Africa, depicted in Egyptian and Babylonian art. Prized by Pygmy tribes and Congo region tribes as versatile hunters for their speed and silence. Brought to England in the 1930s and established in the West.

name: Basque Shepherd Dog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, alert, work-focused, devoted
description: An ancient medium-sized herding dog from the Basque Country, known for its rustic double coat, agility, and intelligence. Comes in two coat varieties: the rough-coated Iletsua and the smoother-coated Gorbeikoa, bred for working livestock in mountainous terrain.
history: An ancient landrace breed developed by shepherds in the Basque region for herding and protecting livestock in mountainous terrain. Faced near extinction in the 20th century but was revived by dedicated breeders, leading to official recognition by the Real Sociedad Canina de España in 1995 and inclusion in AKC's Foundation Stock Service.

name: Bassador
temperament: Friendly, loyal, gentle, intelligent, outgoing
description: Medium-to-large crossbreed with a long, sturdy body, short legs, and distinctive droopy ears of the Basset Hound combined with the friendly face and temperament of a Labrador Retriever
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in recent decades in North America by crossing Basset Hound and Labrador Retriever to combine friendly family-oriented traits with unique appearance and scenting abilities

name: Basset Artésien Normand
temperament: Affectionate, calm, gentle, intelligent, friendly
description: A French short-legged scenthound with a long back, noble head, very long ears, and tricolour or bicolour coat, known for its tenacity on the trail and affectionate nature at home
history: Developed in France from the older Basset d'Artois and Basset Normand breeds, standardized around 1924 by Léon Verrier for hunting small game like rabbit and hare either alone or in packs

name: Basset Bleu de Gascogne
temperament: Gentle, friendly, alert, loyal, independent
description: Medium-sized, low-slung French scent hound with distinctive blue-mottled coat, long drooping ears, and exceptional tracking abilities. Known for its noble expression and remarkable sense of smell.
history: Descended from the larger Grand Bleu de Gascogne developed in medieval Gascony region for hunting small game. Nearly extinct but revived in early 20th century by breeder Alain Bourbon

name: Basset Fauve de Bretagne
temperament: Friendly, energetic, intelligent, courageous, alert, happy, outgoing, loyal, affectionate
description: Small, compact scent hound with harsh, wiry fawn to golden-red coat and sturdy build. Known for its energetic, happy disposition and lively temperament, originally bred for hunting small game in Brittany.
history: Developed in Brittany, France as a downsized version of the larger Grand Fauve de Bretagne (Griffon Fauve de Bretagne). Used for hunting small game like rabbit and hare. Nearly extinct after WWII but restored by crossing remaining dogs with breeds like Basset Griffon Vendeen.

name: Basset Hound
temperament: Friendly, calm, gentle, loyal, independent, devoted
description: Low-slung, heavy-boned scent hound with distinctive long, velvety ears, mournful expression, and loose skin. Known for powerful tracking ability and calm demeanor.
history: Developed by French aristocrats from hounds bred at the Abbey of St. Hubert for hunting small game in dense cover. Low-slung body perfected for trailing rabbits and hare, later refined in England for pack hunting.

name: Bassettoodle
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, gentle, playful, loyal
description: Medium-sized mixed breed with long ears, short legs, and a wavy or curly coat, combining the Basset Hound's laid-back nature with Poodle intelligence and low-shedding coat
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States by combining Basset Hound and Standard Poodle, aiming to create a companion dog with the Poodle's intelligence and hypoallergenic coat combined with the Basset's friendly, easygoing temperament

name: Bavarian Mountain Hound
temperament: Calm, loyal, intelligent, devoted, courageous, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, lean and muscular scent hound with exceptional tracking abilities and endurance, developed for following wounded game in mountainous terrain. Known for its calm and poised demeanor combined with courage and devotion.
history: Developed in 19th century Bavaria by crossing Hanoverian Scenthounds with Alpine hunting dogs and local mountain dogs to create a lighter, more agile dog capable of tracking wounded game on cold trails through difficult mountainous terrain

name: Beagle
temperament: Friendly, gentle, alert, outgoing, curious, energetic
description: Compact, merry scent hound with sturdy build, distinctive tri-color or bi-color coat pattern, and cheerful disposition. Originally bred for hunting hare in packs with a distinctive baying voice.
history: The modern Beagle was developed in Great Britain around the 1830s from various English hounds. Bred as 'foot hounds' for hare hunting, with hunters following on foot rather than horseback.

name: Beagle-Harrier
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, friendly, courageous, devoted
description: Medium-sized, sturdily built French scent hound created by crossing Beagles and Harriers, known for its balanced build, short dense coat, and energetic disposition with a passion for hunting
history: Developed in 19th century France by Baron Gérard, crossing English Beagles with French Harriers to create a medium-sized pack hound for hunting hare and other small game. Recognized by the FCI.

name: Bearded Collie
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, playful, affectionate, alert, confident, outgoing
description: Medium-sized herding dog with long, shaggy double coat and animated expression, known for its boisterous, charismatic personality and distinctive bouncing gait
history: Ancient Scottish herding breed developed to drive sheep and cattle in harsh Highland conditions, bred for hardiness, intelligence, and ability to work independently. Nearly extinct after World War II but revived through careful breeding programs. Recognized by Kennel Club in 1959 and AKC in 1977.

name: Beauceron
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, protective, courageous, confident
description: Large, powerful French herding dog with distinctive double dewclaws, short double coat, and black and tan markings. Known for its self-assured demeanor and versatility as both guardian and companion.
history: Ancient French herding breed also known as Berger de Beauce, developed in plains surrounding Paris for herding and protecting sheep and cattle. Shares ancestry with the Briard, nearly extinct after World War II but saved by dedicated breeders. Also used for military and police work.

name: Bedlington Terrier
temperament: Intelligent, affectionate, energetic, playful, courageous, alert
description: Distinctive lamb-like terrier with graceful, lithe build, arched back, pear-shaped head, and crisp curly coat that gives it a unique appearance among terrier breeds
history: Developed in Bedlington, Northumberland in the early 19th century for hunting vermin in mines and farms. Known as the 'Gypsy Dog' due to use by poachers. Lineage includes Dandie Dinmont Terrier, Whippet, and Otterhound crosses. Recognized by The Kennel Club in 1877 and AKC in 1886.

name: Belgian Laekenois
temperament: Alert, intelligent, protective, loyal, energetic, affectionate
description: Medium-sized herding dog with distinctive rough, wiry, tousled coat and alert, intelligent expression. The oldest and rarest of the four Belgian Shepherd varieties, known for its unique fawn-colored wire coat.
history: One of four Belgian Shepherd varieties and the rarest, developed in late 1800s around Laeken near the Royal Castle of Laeken. Originally bred to guard flocks and the drying linen fields near Antwerp. Nearly extinct after WWII but preserved by dedicated breeders.

name: Belgian Malinois
temperament: Intelligent, confident, energetic, loyal, alert, work-focused, protective
description: Medium-sized, athletic herding dog that is squarely built with a proud carriage. Known for its intense intelligence and strong work ethic, particularly suited for police and military roles. Features a short fawn-to-mahogany coat with distinctive black mask and ears.
history: Developed in the area around Malines, Belgium, in the late 19th century as one of four varieties of Belgian Shepherd dogs. Originally bred for herding ability and strong work ethic, quickly adopted by police forces and militaries worldwide. AKC recognized as distinct breed in 1959.

name: Belgian Shepherd
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, alert, protective, work-focused
description: Versatile medium to large herding dog from Belgium known for intelligence, athleticism, and strong work ethic. The FCI recognizes four distinct varieties based on coat type and color: Groenendael (long-haired, black), Tervuren (long-haired, fawn/mahogany), Malinois (short-haired, fawn/mahogany), and Laekenois (rough-haired, fawn).
history: Developed in late 1800s in Belgium as all-purpose herding and guard dogs. Professor Adolphe Reul of the Belgian School of Veterinary Science established the breed type in 1891, categorizing native dogs into four distinct varieties based on coat type and color

name: Belgian Tervuren
temperament: Intelligent, courageous, alert, loyal, energetic
description: Elegant, athletic medium-sized herding dog with a long double coat ranging from rich fawn to mahogany with black overlay, known for intelligence, versatility and strong work drive
history: One of four distinct Belgian sheepdog varieties developed in the late 1800s, named after the village of Tervuren. Originally used as a versatile herding and guard dog for flocks, prized for intelligence, trainability and work ethic

name: Bergamasco Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, independent, loyal, alert, gentle, calm
description: Medium-large herding dog with a distinctive matted coat consisting of natural 'flocks' that provide protection from weather and predators, combined with an intelligent, independent temperament rooted in ancient pastoral heritage
history: Ancient shepherding breed from the Italian Alps near Bergamo, valued for intelligence and protective flocked coat that developed to shield from harsh mountain weather and predators while herding sheep, recognized by AKC in 2015

name: Berger Picard
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, alert, affectionate
description: Medium-sized herding dog with distinctive shaggy fawn or brindle coat, expressive upright ears, and lively expression. An ancient French breed known for its rustic appearance and intelligent, observant nature.
history: Ancient French herding breed from Picardy region, considered one of the oldest French sheepdogs. Nearly extinct after World Wars but restored by dedicated breeders.

name: Bernedoodle
temperament: Intelligent, friendly, gentle, playful, affectionate, loyal
description: A crossbreed between a Bernese Mountain Dog and a Standard Poodle, resulting in a medium to large-sized intelligent companion with a wavy or curly low-shedding coat and friendly disposition. Size and appearance can vary significantly depending on the generation and the Poodle parent's size.
history: Developed in Canada in 2003, the Bernedoodle is a recent designer crossbreed created by crossing Bernese Mountain Dogs with Standard Poodles to combine the calm, loyal nature of the Bernese Mountain Dog with the high intelligence and low-shedding coat of the Poodle

name: Bernese Mountain Dog
temperament: Calm, gentle, loyal, intelligent, affectionate, good-natured
description: Large, strong, and sturdy working dog with a distinctive long tricolored coat and calm, gentle temperament. One of four types of Swiss Mountain Dogs, known for being good-natured, loyal, and an excellent family companion.
history: One of four types of Swiss Mountain Dogs originating in the canton of Bern. Served as all-purpose farm dogs for droving cattle, pulling carts to market, and watchdogs. Nearly extinct by early 1900s but revived through careful breeding programs

name: Bichon Frisé
temperament: Happy, playful, affectionate, intelligent, gentle, energetic
description: A small, sturdy, and cheerful companion dog with a soft, curly white double coat, black eyes, and a lively, playful disposition
history: Descended from the Barbet, a Mediterranean water spaniel. The breed was developed in France and became a popular companion dog for European nobility. The modern breed standard was established in France after World War I.

name: Biewer Terrier
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, playful, devoted, affectionate
description: Small, elegant toy terrier with long, silky tri-colored coat and compact build. Known for their whimsical, lively personality and charming attitude that persists well into adulthood.
history: Developed in Germany in 1984 by Werner and Gertrud Biewer from a piebald genetic mutation in their Yorkshire Terrier breeding program. Through selective breeding, they established this distinct tri-colored toy breed. AKC recognized in 2021.

name: Billy
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, courageous, gentle, independent
description: Large, elegant French scent hound with a predominantly white coat and orange or lemon markings, known for speed, stamina and light-framed build bred for pack hunting
history: Developed in 19th century France by Gaston Hublot du Rivault at Château de Billy by crossing Montaimboeuf, Céris, and Larye breeds (now extinct). Nearly vanished after World War II but was revived by du Rivault's son. Recognized by FCI in Group 6.

name: Black and Tan Coonhound
temperament: Friendly, outgoing, intelligent, alert, adaptable, easygoing
description: Large, powerful American-bred scent hound with distinctive black and tan coat pattern and long ears, known for its cold nose, loud bawling bark, and determined trailing ability
history: Developed in the American South by frontiersmen in the 18th century, descending from English Foxhound, Bloodhound, and Talbot Hound crosses for raccoon hunting and tracking over rugged Appalachian terrain

name: Black Mouth Cur
temperament: Loyal, courageous, intelligent, energetic, protective, work-focused
description: Medium to large rugged, well-muscled working and hunting dog from the American South, known for its versatility, courage, intelligence, and characteristic black-pigmented muzzle and lips
history: Developed in the southern United States as versatile pioneer and farm dog for herding cattle and hogs protecting homestead and hunting variety of game from squirrels to bears valued for stamina and reliability

name: Black Norwegian Elkhound
temperament: Energetic, courageous, alert, independent, loyal, confident
description: Medium-sized spitz-type hunting dog with solid black double coat and robust, compact build. A bold, energetic breed smaller than its grey counterpart, built for endurance and agility as an all-purpose hunter with alert expression and confident demeanor.
history: Ancient Norwegian spitz breed developed over centuries as a versatile hunter of large game such as elk, moose, and bear in rugged Scandinavian terrain. Smaller and less common than the Gray Norwegian Elkhound, also served as a watchdog. Recognized by the FCI but not the AKC as of 2024.

name: Black Russian Terrier
temperament: Confident, courageous, intelligent, protective, calm, loyal
description: Large, powerful, and robust working dog with a distinctive dense black double coat and confident demeanor. Bred for military and guard work, this hardy breed combines courage with intelligence.
history: Developed in the Soviet Union in the late 1940s by the state-run Red Star Kennel for military use. Created by crossing Giant Schnauzer, Airedale Terrier, Rottweiler, and other breeds to produce a large, trainable, and hardy guard dog.

name: Bloodhound
temperament: Gentle, friendly, independent, alert, devoted, affectionate
description: Large, substantial scent hound with a noble, dignified expression, characterized by exceptional tracking ability, distinctive wrinkled face, long drooping ears, loose skin, and pendulous jowls. Renowned for its extraordinary sense of smell and unparalleled tracking abilities.
history: Ancient breed developed by monks at the Abbey of Saint-Hubert in Belgium and France during medieval times. Later refined in England and valued for centuries for tracking game and people, eventually imported to the US for search and rescue work.

name: Blue Picardy Spaniel
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, calm, devoted, affectionate
description: Medium-sized, strongly built gundog with distinctive blue-gray roan speckled coat that gives it a bluish appearance. Known for its gentle hunting temperament and versatile field abilities.
history: Developed in the Picardy region of France in the early 20th century by crossing Picardy Spaniels with English Setters (likely Gordon Setters) to produce a versatile, hardy pointing dog with the distinctive blue-speckled coat. Recognized by the FCI.

name: Bluetick Coonhound
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, loyal, devoted, work-focused
description: Large, athletic, and muscular scenthound with distinctive blue-ticked coat pattern and renowned 'cold nose' trailing ability, bred specifically for tracking and treeing raccoons
history: Developed in Louisiana from French Grand Bleu de Gascogne hounds crossed with English Foxhounds, American Foxhounds, and other local coonhound breeds, perfected for raccoon hunting in American terrain

name: Boerboel
temperament: Confident, intelligent, loyal, protective, calm
description: Large, powerful mastiff-type guardian breed from South Africa with a muscular build, short dense coat, and confident temperament. Known as a family guardian, the name 'Boerboel' translates to 'farmer's dog' in Afrikaans.
history: Developed by Dutch and other European settlers in South Africa starting in the 1600s from European mastiff breeds and native dogs. Bred as all-purpose farm dogs for protecting homesteads from predators.

name: Bohemian Shepherd
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, calm, confident, eager to please, energetic, gentle, adaptable
description: Medium-sized, multi-purpose herding dog of rectangular frame with a long, thick black-and-tan coat and noble, elegant appearance. Known for their versatility, gentle nature, and distinctive tan markings.
history: Ancient herding breed from the Chodsko region of Bohemia, also known as Chodský pes, dating back to the 14th century. Bred as herding and guard dogs by the Chodové people, nearly extinct after WWII but revived in the 1980s. Received provisional FCI recognition in 2019 and is part of the AKC Foundation Stock Service.

name: Bohemian Spotted Dog
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, adaptable, gentle, energetic
description: Medium-sized spotted companion dog known for its distinctive tricolor coat pattern and gentle, friendly nature. Officially known as Český strakatý pes, this adaptable breed combines intelligence with a calm temperament.
history: Created in the 1950s in Prague by cynologist František Horák for laboratory purposes. The breed's stable temperament and manageable size made them ideal subjects. After the research concluded, the dogs were made available to the public and developed into a beloved companion breed.

name: Bolognese
temperament: Calm, devoted, intelligent, playful, gentle, loyal
description: A small, stocky, and compact white toy breed with a long, fluffy, pure-white cotton-like coat, belonging to the Bichon family. Known for its calm, devoted temperament and affectionate nature as a companion dog.
history: An ancient noble breed of the Bichon family, originating in Bologna, Italy, dating to 11th-12th centuries. Prized companions to royalty and aristocracy from the Roman era through the Renaissance, favored as palace companions.

name: Border Collie
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, alert, work-focused, loyal, eager to please
description: Medium-sized, highly intelligent, and athletic herding dog with keen expression and intense work drive, renowned for its instinctual ability to control livestock with its signature stare
history: Developed by shepherds on the border of England and Scotland for herding sheep in hilly terrain, refined through selective breeding for working ability. The breed's lineage can be traced back to a single dog named Old Hemp, born in 1893.

name: Border Terrier
temperament: Affectionate, intelligent, alert, energetic, courageous, adaptable
origin: Border regions between England and Scotland
description: Small, sturdy working terrier with weather-resistant wiry coat and distinctive otter-like head, bred for fox hunting with a plucky and affectionate nature
history: Developed in the 18th century in the Cheviot Hills along the English-Scottish border by farmers and shepherds to hunt fox alongside foxhounds. Needed to be small enough to follow quarry into dens and burrows but tough enough to keep up with horses and work independently.

name: Bordoodle
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, playful, eager to please
description: An intelligent and energetic medium-to-large crossbreed combining the herding instincts and work ethic of the Border Collie with the low-shedding coat and athleticism of the Poodle, creating a highly trainable companion
history: Developed in the late 20th/early 21st century by crossing Border Collies with Standard Poodles to create a highly intelligent, low-shedding companion. Part of the popular 'doodle' designer breed trend

name: Borzoi
temperament: Gentle, independent, calm, intelligent, affectionate
description: Large, elegant sighthound with long, silky flowing coat and aristocratic bearing, known for grace, speed, and gentle nature. Originally bred by Russian nobility for coursing wolves and game.
history: Developed by Russian aristocracy in the 17th century for coursing wolves and other game. Also known as Russian Wolfhound until 1936. Numbers dwindled after the Russian Revolution but were preserved by fanciers outside Russia.

name: Boston Terrier
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, outgoing, playful
description: Compact, short-tailed dog with distinctive flat face, erect ears, and smooth 'tuxedo' coat markings. Known as 'The American Gentleman' for its friendly and intelligent demeanor.
history: Developed in Boston in the 1870s from crossing English Bulldog with the now-extinct White English Terrier. Refined in the late 19th century and became one of the first American-developed breeds recognized by the AKC.

name: Bouvier des Ardennes
temperament: Courageous, loyal, intelligent, energetic, work-focused, adaptable
description: Medium-sized, hardy and rustic herding dog from the Belgian Ardennes, characterized by a harsh, shaggy double coat, robust build, and impressive endurance with a courageous temperament
history: Ancient Belgian herding breed from the Ardennes region, traditionally used to drove cattle and pigs. Nearly extinct after the World Wars due to mechanization of farming but successfully revived by enthusiasts in the late 1980s

name: Bouvier des Flandres
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, protective, courageous, calm, gentle
description: Large, powerfully built herding dog with a rugged appearance and rough, tousled double coat. This compact breed gives the impression of great strength without heaviness and has a dense weather-resistant coat suited for farm work.
history: Developed by monks in the Flanders region of medieval Europe as cattle herding and all-purpose farm dog. Used to drive cattle, pull carts, and serve as a watchdog. Nearly extinct after World War I but revived by dedicated breeders.

name: Boxer
temperament: Playful, energetic, loyal, intelligent, friendly, alert
description: Medium to large, powerful and athletic working dog with a distinctive short-muzzled, flat face and short coat. Known for their fun-loving, bright temperament and expressive face, combining energy with loyalty.
history: Developed in late 19th century Germany from the now-extinct German Bullenbeisser (used for hunting large game like boar and bear) and English Bulldog, originally for bull-baiting and hunting, later refined for working and companion roles

name: Boxerdoodle
temperament: Intelligent, playful, energetic, loyal, friendly, affectionate
description: Athletic medium-to-large crossbreed combining the playful energy and loyalty of the Boxer with the intelligence and low-shedding coat of the Poodle. Physical characteristics can vary significantly based on genetic inheritance from parent breeds.
history: Modern designer crossbreed first intentionally bred in the United States in recent decades. Developed to combine the Boxer's athleticism, loyalty and playful temperament with the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat qualities. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Boykin Spaniel
temperament: Friendly, energetic, eager to please, intelligent, adaptable
description: Medium-sized, energetic, and versatile flushing and retrieving spaniel with rich liver or chocolate brown coat and enthusiastic work ethic, especially in water. Known for their amiable disposition and compact build ideal for hunting from small boats.
history: Developed in early 1900s in South Carolina by L. Whitaker Boykin. Foundation sire was a small stray spaniel-type dog named 'Dumpy' who showed great hunting aptitude. Bred by crossing Chesapeake Bay Retrievers, Springer Spaniels, Cocker Spaniels, and American Water Spaniels for hunting in South Carolina swamps, particularly the Wateree River Swamp. Recognized as the state dog of South Carolina.

name: Bracco Italiano
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, affectionate, loyal, calm, docile, adaptable
description: Large, noble Italian pointing breed with sculpted head, distinctive orange and white or chestnut and white coat, and pendulous ears. A powerful and athletic dog of ancient Italian lineage, known for its endurance and gentle, intelligent nature.
history: One of the oldest pointing breeds in Europe with ancient origins dating back to the 4th-5th century BC. Developed as a versatile hunting dog for bird hunting in Italian terrain, adept at pointing and retrieving. The modern breed is thought to be a cross of the Piedmontese Pointer and the Lombard Pointer.

name: Braque d'Auvergne
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, affectionate, devoted, work-focused
description: Medium-sized French pointing breed with distinctive black and white ticked coat marked with patches and speckles, noble head, and athletic build
history: Ancient French pointing breed from Auvergne region with history predating the French Revolution, refined in 18th-19th centuries with modern standard established in 20th century, recognized by FCI

name: Braque du Bourbonnais
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, loyal, calm, affectionate, adaptable
description: Ancient, medium-sized French pointing dog with compact, agile build, distinctive ticked speckled coat, naturally short tail, and expressive face. Known for natural hunting instincts and athletic ability.
history: One of the most ancient French pointing breeds, documented since the 16th century. Nearly extinct after World War II but successfully restored in the 1970s by dedicated breeders from remaining bloodlines following French and FCI standards.

name: Braque Français
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, loyal, affectionate, adaptable
description: An ancient French pointing breed coming in two types: the larger Gascogne and the smaller, more popular Pyrenean. This gentle, athletic, and highly trainable gundog features a distinctive brown and white coat and versatile hunting abilities.
history: Ancient French pointing breed dating to 15th century with two distinct varieties: the larger Gascogne and smaller Pyrenean. Descended from one of the oldest pointing-type dogs prized as versatile and biddable hunting companion

name: Braque Saint-Germain
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, affectionate, eager to please, loyal
description: Elegant and athletic medium-sized French pointing dog with distinctive white coat and orange markings, known for its gentle temperament and versatile hunting abilities
history: Developed in the 1830s at the royal kennels of Compiègne, France, from crossings between English Pointers and French Braque breeds, believed to involve an English Pointer gifted to King Charles X. Named after the Saint-Germain-en-Laye region and recognized by the FCI as a versatile gundog

name: Brazilian Terrier
temperament: Alert, friendly, energetic, intelligent, playful, loyal
description: Small, compact, and agile terrier with a short, smooth tri-color coat, known for intelligence, alertness, and energetic spirit, native to Brazil
history: Developed in the 19th and early 20th centuries from various European terriers, such as the Jack Russell and Fox Terrier, brought to Brazil by immigrants. These dogs were crossed with local Brazilian farm dogs, creating a unique breed adapted to the country's environment and standardized as a farm and small game terrier, recognized by the FCI.

name: Briard
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, protective, confident, courageous, devoted
description: Large, powerful French herding dog with a luxurious long wavy double coat, distinctive beard and eyebrows creating a peek-a-boo hairdo, and a loyal, intelligent disposition
history: Ancient French herding breed developed for centuries to herd and guard flocks. Served as sentries, messengers, and pack animals for the French army during World War I. Nearly extinct after World War II but successfully revived. Recognized by the AKC in 1928.

name: Briquet de Provence
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, alert, friendly, devoted, adaptable
description: Medium-sized, elegant French scent hound with short coat and distinctive tri-color markings, well-proportioned with lean muscle built for endurance and hunting small game in Mediterranean terrain
history: Ancient French scent hound from Provence region developed for hunting hare and small game. Faced near extinction after World War II but was reconstructed by M. Le Comte de Ravel and others, leading to breed club establishment in 1983 and official FCI recognition

name: Briquet Griffon Vendéen
temperament: Courageous, energetic, independent, friendly, alert, intelligent
description: Medium-sized rough-coated French scenthound known for its courage, determination, and stamina in the hunt, with a sturdy build designed for hunting in dense terrain and a friendly, energetic disposition
history: Developed in the Vendée region of France before World War I from the larger Grand Griffon Vendéen. Selectively bred down in size by the Comte d'Elva to create a smaller faster hound for hunting medium game like hare and fox. Nearly decimated by World Wars but carefully restored

name: Brittany
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, eager to please, friendly, athletic, happy
description: Medium-sized, compact and leggy sporting dog with an athletic build and distinctive orange and white feathered coat. Known for its versatility in pointing and retrieving, this agile gundog combines high energy for hunting and field work with an intelligent and happy-go-lucky disposition.
history: Developed in the 17th-18th century in the Brittany province of France as a versatile hunting dog capable of pointing and retrieving on various terrain. Refined through crosses with English Setters and recognized by AKC in 1934.

name: Brittanydoodle
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, friendly, eager to please, loyal
description: Medium to large crossbreed combining the athletic bird-dog instincts of the Brittany with the intelligence and low-shedding coat of the Standard Poodle, typically featuring a wavy or curly coat
history: Modern crossbreed developed in the United States as part of the 'doodle' trend. Created by crossing athletic Brittany sporting dogs with Standard Poodles to produce a versatile family and sporting companion with reduced shedding. Not recognized by major kennel clubs; standards vary by breeder.

name: Broholmer
temperament: Calm, friendly, confident, protective, loyal, gentle
description: Large, powerful mastiff-type dog with distinctive rectangular build and calm, confident demeanor. Known for its imposing presence and steady, powerful gait, this Danish guardian breed combines strength with a gentle nature.
history: Ancient Danish breed descended from mastiffs of the Middle Ages, originally used for stag hunting and later as estate guardian for large farms and manors. Standardized and named in 18th century by Count Sehested of Broholm. Nearly extinct after WWII but revived in 1970s through dedicated effort by Danish enthusiasts.

name: Bruno Jura Hound
temperament: Independent, alert, intelligent, gentle, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, noble Swiss scent hound with tan to black coat and distinctive black saddle, known for its keen nose, endurance, and strong tracking instincts. Features long, dropped ears and a gentle, sometimes melancholic expression, bred for hunting in mountainous terrain.
history: Ancient Swiss breed and variety of the Schweizer Laufhund, developed over centuries in the Jura Mountains for hunting hare, roe deer, and fox in challenging terrain. One of four Swiss running hound varieties recognized by FCI, with modern standard established in early 20th century.

name: Bucovina Shepherd Dog
temperament: Calm, protective, courageous, loyal, independent, devoted
description: Large, powerful livestock guardian dog from the Carpathian Mountains with a thick, dense weather-resistant double coat. Known for its calm demeanor, courage, and devotion to family and flock.
history: Ancient natural breed developed over centuries in the Carpathian Mountains of Romania by shepherds to guard livestock and property against large predators like bears and wolves. First breed standard written in 1982, officially recognized by FCI in 2019.

name: Bugg
temperament: Friendly, playful, affectionate, alert, intelligent
description: Small, sturdy crossbreed combining Boston Terrier athleticism with Pug charm, featuring a compact build, expressive short-muzzled face, and friendly disposition
history: Designer crossbreed developed in the late 20th/early 21st century by crossing Boston Terrier and Pug to create a compact companion dog combining the friendly alertness of the Boston Terrier with the charming affectionate personality of the Pug

name: Bulgarian Hound
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, loyal, independent, work-focused
description: Medium-sized scent hound of balanced and elegant conformation with strong hunting instincts and sturdy build. Features a tricolor or black-and-tan short, dense coat with well-developed undercoat, developed for tracking both small and large game in Bulgarian terrain.
history: Traditional Bulgarian scent hound, known locally as 'Barak' or 'Bulgarsko Gonche', descended from scenthounds brought to the Balkan region by the Slavs who interbred them with local hounds. Developed and bred for centuries for hunting wild boar and other game in the Balkans, the breed has been preserved in its current form and is highly valued for its hunting abilities in Bulgaria.

name: Bulgarian Scenthound
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, alert, energetic, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, well-proportioned scent hound with a strong, elegant build and short, dense coat. Developed for hunting in Bulgarian mountainous terrain, this robust breed has exceptional scenting ability and is traditionally used for trailing both small and large game including wild boar, deer, and hare.
history: An ancient breed from the Balkans, believed brought to the region by the Slavs. Developed in Bulgaria for hunting in mountainous terrain, it's the most popular hunting dog in Bulgaria, prized for exceptional scent capabilities and endurance. Recognized by FCI as 'Bulgarian Hound (Barak)'

name: Bull Arab
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, protective, courageous, energetic
description: Large, powerful, and athletic Australian hunting dog specifically developed for tracking and catching feral pigs. Known for its muscular build, strength, speed, and resilience with a short coat.
history: Developed in Australia in the 1970s by Mike Hodgens crossing Bull Terrier for tenacity, Greyhound for speed, and German Shorthaired Pointer for scenting ability and endurance to create the ultimate pig-hunting dog

name: Bulldog
temperament: Friendly, courageous, calm, docile, loyal
description: Medium-sized muscular dog with loose wrinkled skin, distinctive wrinkled face, and pushed-in nose, known for its gentle but courageous disposition
history: Originally developed in medieval England for bull-baiting, descended from ancient mastiff-type dogs. After blood sports were outlawed in 1835, refined into the gentle companion breed known today

name: Bullmastiff
temperament: Loyal, protective, calm, courageous, affectionate, confident
description: Large, powerful guardian breed with short coat and calm, confident demeanor, originally bred to guard estates and known as 'The Gamekeeper's Night-Dog'
history: Developed in mid-19th century England by gamekeepers crossing English Mastiff with Old English Bulldog to create an effective estate guard dog for tracking poachers

name: Bulloxer
temperament: Loyal, protective, playful, affectionate, courageous, energetic
description: A powerful and muscular mixed breed combining the sturdy, stocky build of the English Bulldog with the athleticism of the Boxer. Appearance and temperament can vary, often possessing the Boxer's athletic frame and playful nature with the Bulldog's loyal, courageous disposition.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in recent decades in the United States to combine the athletic, playful nature of the Boxer with the loyal, courageous temperament of the English Bulldog, while potentially reducing breathing issues common in English Bulldogs. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Bull Terrier
temperament: Playful, courageous, energetic, loyal, mischievous
description: A muscular, well-built terrier best known for its distinctive long, egg-shaped head that slopes down to the nose. This confident breed combines a playful, mischievous personality with a courageous temperament.
history: Developed in 19th-century England by crossing old English Bulldogs with various terriers, originally for bull-baiting and dog fighting. James Hinks is credited with standardizing the breed and creating the distinctive egg-shaped head, earning them the nickname 'The White Cavalier'.

name: Bull Terrier (Miniature)
temperament: Playful, energetic, courageous, confident, affectionate, outgoing
description: Small, upstanding, and muscular terrier with a distinctive egg-shaped head and triangular eyes. This compact version of the Bull Terrier is known for being comical, mischievous, and lively with a squarely built frame.
history: Developed in 19th-century England as a scaled-down version of the standard Bull Terrier, which was standardized by James Hinks. Created for fanciers who wanted a smaller companion version while maintaining the same characteristics and recognized as a separate breed by the AKC in 1991.

name: Bully Kutta
temperament: Protective, loyal, courageous, independent, intelligent
description: Very large, powerful mastiff-type landrace breed from the Indian subcontinent with ancient origins, known for its immense strength, muscular build, and use as a guardian dog
history: Ancient landrace breed with origins in the Punjab region of the Indian subcontinent. Descended from mastiff-type dogs, historically used by royalty and landowners for hunting large game and later bred for guarding, fighting, and estate protection. Not recognized by major international kennel clubs but standards exist in Pakistani and Indian breed registries

name: Burgos Pointer
temperament: Loyal, calm, intelligent, docile, work-focused
description: Large, robust Spanish pointing breed with distinctive white and liver coat, athletic build, and noble demeanor. Known for its calm temperament, droopy ears, and exceptional hunting prowess in varied terrain.
history: Ancient Spanish pointer breed descended from old Spanish Pointer, developed over centuries in the Castile region for hunting feathered and small game in varied terrain. Lineage dates back to at least the 16th century and recognized by the FCI.

name: Ca de Bou
temperament: Calm, courageous, loyal, confident, protective
description: Medium-sized, powerful Molosser-type breed with distinctive muscular build and alert expression, also known as the Perro de Presa Mallorquin. Originally bred for bull-baiting with a calm yet courageous demeanor.
history: Ancient breed descended from Iberian Mastiff and English Bulldogs brought to the Balearic Islands. Originally used for bull-baiting and as a farm guard dog, the breed nearly faced extinction after blood sports were banned but was revived by dedicated Spanish breeders in the 20th century.

name: Cairn Terrier
temperament: Alert, confident, friendly, intelligent, courageous, energetic
description: A small, sturdy working terrier with weather-resistant shaggy coat and alert expression, originating from the Scottish Highlands and known for its plucky, cheerful nature
history: Developed on the Isle of Skye in Scotland, one of the oldest terrier breeds. Originally used by farmers in the Scottish Highlands to hunt and flush out foxes and vermin from rock piles (cairns), which gave the breed its name. Recognized by the AKC in 1913.

name: Calupoh
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, alert, calm, protective, confident
description: Large, athletic wolf-like breed developed in Mexico through controlled hybridization of Mexican Gray Wolves and domestic dogs. Known for their distinctive appearance, strong build, and loyal temperament, combining the genetic heritage of wolves with the trainability of domestic dogs.
history: Developed in Mexico in the mid-1990s through controlled hybridization of Mexican Gray Wolves with domestic dogs, primarily German Shepherds and Alaskan Malamutes, to create a wolf-like companion breed that preserves cultural heritage while maintaining domestic dog temperament and trainability. Provisionally recognized by the FCI and the Federación Canófila Mexicana.

name: Ca Mè Mallorquí
temperament: Alert, intelligent, energetic, loyal, work-focused
description: Medium-sized working dog from Mallorca, Spain, with a short black coat and athletic build, traditionally used for hunting and herding in Mediterranean terrain
history: Traditional working dog of Mallorca developed for hunting and herding, adapted to local Mediterranean terrain and climate, recognized by Spanish kennel organizations

name: Campeiro Bulldog
temperament: Loyal, protective, courageous, calm, work-focused
description: A powerful, medium-sized Brazilian bulldog breed with muscular, athletic build, broad chest, and short coat. Historically developed for cattle work on ranches with strong work ethic and alert expression.
history: Descended from Old English Bulldogs brought to southern Brazil by European immigrants in the 19th century. Developed for ranch work, specifically for subduing and controlling cattle. After facing near extinction, the breed was revived through dedicated conservation efforts.

name: Canaan Dog
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, independent, protective, confident
description: Medium-sized primitive spitz-type pariah dog with erect ears, wedge-shaped head, and bushy tail carried curled over the back, known for natural survival instincts and versatility as herder and guardian
history: Ancient pariah dog from the land of Canaan that survived for thousands of years in the wild. In the 1930s, Dr. Rudolphina Menzel began redomesticating them for use as guard and service dogs. Recognized by AKC in 1997 and is the national dog of Israel.

name: Canadian Eskimo Dog
temperament: Alert, loyal, intelligent, courageous, independent, work-focused
description: A powerful, Spitz-type working dog of the Arctic with thick double coat and imposing stature, known for exceptional endurance in sledding and hunting work. This primitive breed combines strength with hardy Arctic adaptation.
history: Ancient aboriginal breed used by the Inuit people of the Canadian Arctic for thousands of years as a multi-purpose working dog for sledding, transportation and hunting. Nearly extinct with introduction of snowmobiles but saved through dedicated preservation and revival efforts.

name: Can de Chira
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, alert, work-focused
description: A medium-sized, agile, and rustic traditional Spanish herding dog from the Aragon region with a weather-resistant double coat and energetic temperament, prized for its intelligence and ability to manage livestock in mountainous terrain
history: Traditional Aragonese shepherd dog from the Pyrenees mountains valued for cattle and sheep herding. Its name derives from herding 'chiras,' a local sheep breed. The breed faced near extinction but is undergoing recovery thanks to efforts by the Club del Can de Chira and recognition as a Canine Ethnic Group by the Real Sociedad Canina de España.

name: Can de Palleiro
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, protective, work-focused, alert
description: A medium-to-large, rustic herding and guarding dog of lupoid (wolf-like) appearance, native to the Galicia region of Spain. Known for its sturdy build, weather-resistant double coat, balanced temperament, and versatility as an all-purpose farm dog.
history: Ancient Iberian landrace breed from northwestern Spain, traditionally used as an all-purpose farm dog for herding cattle, guarding property, and livestock management. Maintained genetic purity through geographic isolation but faced near extinction by late 20th century. Subject of successful recovery program since late 1990s and recognized by Spain's royal kennel club (RSCE).

name: Cane Corso
temperament: Confident, protective, intelligent, loyal, affectionate
description: Large, powerful Italian mastiff-type dog with muscular, athletic build and confident demeanor. This intelligent and majestic breed was historically used as a guardian and hunter, known for its loyalty to family.
history: Ancient Italian breed descended from Roman Molossian war dogs, developed as a versatile farmhand, guardian, and hunter of large game. The breed nearly faced extinction but was revived by enthusiasts in the 1970s.

name: Cane di Oropa
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, energetic, work-focused, alert, devoted
description: Medium-sized herding dog from the Italian Biellese Alps with a rustic appearance, strong build, and merle coat. Known for its mountain working heritage and keen, energetic temperament suited for flock management.
history: Ancient pastoral dog native to the Biellese Alps and Oropa Valley in Italy's Piedmont region. Traditionally used by local shepherds for centuries to manage sheep and cattle in mountain terrain. The breed faced extinction before preservation efforts by the Associazione Amici del Cane di Oropa breed club.

name: Cane Paratore
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, protective, work-focused, alert
description: A medium-sized, rustic Italian herding dog from the Piedmont Alps, known for its agility, intelligence, and typically merle coat. This athletic breed combines herding instincts with protective qualities, traditionally used for managing livestock in mountainous terrain.
