history: A landrace breed developed over centuries by shepherds in the western Italian Alps (Piedmont) to manage sheep and cattle in mountainous terrain. Valued for its hardiness and working ability, the breed is undergoing official recognition by the Italian Kennel Club (ENCI). Also known as the Pastore d'Oropa.

name: Cantabrian Water Dog
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, work-focused, energetic, alert, devoted
description: A rustic, medium-sized, and athletic water dog from the Cantabria region of northern Spain, characterized by a curly, woolly, waterproof coat. Originally bred for fishing and water work, this ancient breed is known for its loyalty, work-focused nature, and exceptional aptitude for swimming and retrieving.
history: An ancient landrace breed from the coastal Cantabrian region of northern Spain, historically used by fishermen for water work, retrieving fish and gear, guarding boats, and assisting with nets. Nearly extinct but being revived through conservation efforts, officially recognized by the Real Sociedad Canina de España (RSCE) in 2011.

name: Cão de Gado Transmontano
temperament: Protective, independent, calm, loyal, confident, courageous
description: Very large, powerful molossoid-type livestock guardian dog with thick double coat, bred in the mountainous regions of northern Portugal to protect flocks from predators with a calm yet imposing and rustic demeanor
history: Ancient Iberian mastiff-type breed developed in the remote Trás-os-Montes region of northern Portugal, used for centuries by shepherds to guard flocks against wolves and other predators in harsh mountain conditions. Recognized by the FCI for its courage, loyalty, and ability to work independently.

name: Ca Rater Mallorquí
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, loyal, courageous
description: Small, agile terrier-like breed from Mallorca with a compact, athletic build and smooth short coat. Known for intelligence, alertness, and exceptional skill in hunting rats and other vermin.
history: Developed on the Spanish island of Mallorca specifically for controlling rats and other vermin in farms, stables, and households. Used for centuries due to its small size and agility to pursue prey into tight spaces. Provisionally recognized by the FCI and recognized as a native Spanish breed.

name: Cardigan Welsh Corgi
temperament: Affectionate, loyal, intelligent, alert, adaptable
description: Low-set, sturdily built herding dog with a long body, fox-like brushy tail, and rounded ears, known for intelligence and versatility
history: One of the oldest herding breeds, believed brought to Wales by Celtic tribes around 1200 BC, developed as versatile farm dogs for cattle herding and property guarding

name: Carea Leonés
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, work-focused, energetic, alert, athletic
description: Medium-sized, rustic Spanish sheepdog of the lupoid type with a dense, weather-resistant coat and strong herding instincts. Known for intelligence, agility, and alert, expressive demeanor in mountainous terrain.
history: Ancient traditional herding dog from León Province in northwest Spain, used for centuries to manage and guide flocks, particularly Merino sheep. Highly valued for hardiness, intelligence, and unwavering work ethic in mountainous terrain and rural environments.

name: Carolina Dog
temperament: Alert, intelligent, independent, loyal, adaptable, gentle
description: Medium-sized, athletic primitive breed with sand-colored coat and erect ears, resembling a dingo or small wolf with ancient pariah dog characteristics
history: Primitive landrace breed descended from ancient dogs that accompanied early human migrations across the Bering land bridge. Discovered living as free-roaming population in cypress swamps of South Carolina and Georgia by Dr. I. Lehr Brisbin Jr. Recognized by UKC and seeking AKC recognition.

name: Carpathian Shepherd Dog
temperament: Loyal, protective, courageous, calm, independent, alert, devoted
description: Large, powerful livestock guardian dog native to the Carpathian Mountains with a wolf-like appearance and thick double coat. Known for its courageous and protective nature, this robust mountain dog was developed for guarding sheep flocks from predators including wolves and bears.
history: Ancient breed developed over centuries by Romanian shepherds in the Carpathian Mountains for protecting sheep flocks from predators. First breed standard written in 1934 by the National Institute for Animal Husbandry and officially recognized by the FCI in 2005.

name: Castro Laboreiro Dog
temperament: Loyal, protective, intelligent, independent, alert, courageous
description: An ancient, large, and powerful Portuguese livestock guardian dog with a distinctive wolf-like appearance and movement. This rustic mountain breed is known for its formidable and unique graduated bark that starts low and rises in pitch.
history: Ancient Portuguese livestock guardian breed from the Peneda-Gerês mountains of northern Portugal. Developed to protect herds from wolves and other predators, it is one of the oldest and rarest breeds on the Iberian Peninsula.

name: Catahoula Leopard Dog
temperament: Independent, intelligent, loyal, protective, energetic, work-focused
description: Medium to large, athletic herding and hunting breed known for its distinctive spotted or merle coat patterns, striking heterochromatic (glass) eyes, and strong work ethic. This versatile working dog combines intelligence with independence.
history: Developed in Northern Louisiana from Native American dogs crossed with Spanish and French settlers' dogs for hunting feral hogs and herding cattle. Named after Catahoula Parish and designated as the official state dog of Louisiana.

name: Catalan Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, protective, courageous
description: Medium-sized, agile herding dog from the Catalan Pyrenees with long shaggy coat, distinctive beard and mustache, and alert expression. Known for intelligence, courage, and watchful nature as a versatile herding breed.
history: Ancient herding breed developed in the Catalan Pyrenees for managing sheep and goats. Used as messengers and sentinels during Spanish Civil War. Nearly extinct afterward but restored through dedicated breeding programs in the 1970s.

name: Caucasian Shepherd Dog
temperament: Protective, independent, courageous, loyal, confident
description: Giant, powerfully built livestock guardian breed with dense, weather-resistant double coat and imposing presence. Bred to be a formidable guardian in harsh mountain conditions with a courageous, confident demeanor.
history: Ancient breed originating in the Caucasus Mountain region, used for centuries to protect livestock from large predators like wolves and bears. Standardized by Soviet cynologists and used as military and prison guard dogs.

name: Caucasian Shepherd Dog
temperament: Protective, independent, confident, courageous, alert
description: Massive, powerful livestock guardian breed with thick, weather-resistant double coat, originally bred to protect flocks from large predators like wolves and bears in the Caucasus Mountains. This formidable guardian combines strong protective instincts with courageous temperament.
history: Ancient breed developed in the Caucasus Mountain region to protect flocks from large predators like wolves and bears, as well as human thieves. Selectively bred by shepherds for protection ability, later utilized by the Soviet Union as military and prison guard dogs

name: Cavalier King Charles Spaniel
temperament: Affectionate, gentle, friendly, playful, eager to please, adaptable
description: Small, elegant toy spaniel with silky coat, large expressive eyes, and noble appearance, known for its gentle expression and affectionate temperament, equally at home as a lap dog or active companion
history: Named after King Charles II who favored the breed, descended from toy spaniels depicted in 16th-18th century British court paintings. The modern breed was revived in the 1920s by enthusiasts seeking to restore the longer-nosed type seen in old portraits

name: Cavapom
temperament: Friendly, affectionate, playful, intelligent, outgoing, alert
description: Small, lively companion dog created by crossing a Cavalier King Charles Spaniel and Pomeranian, known for its affectionate nature, fluffy plush coat, and friendly demeanor that combines the gentle nature of the Cavalier with the compact size of the Pomeranian
history: A modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States by crossing a Cavalier King Charles Spaniel with Pomeranian to create a small companion dog combining the gentle nature of the Cavalier with the lively, fluffy appearance of the Pomeranian. Gained popularity in the late 20th and early 21st centuries

name: Cavapoo
temperament: Affectionate, intelligent, friendly, playful, gentle, outgoing
description: Small companion crossbreed combining Cavalier King Charles Spaniel and Poodle traits, known for its soft wavy or curly coat, friendly nature, and affectionate temperament
history: Modern designer crossbreed first intentionally developed in Australia in the 1990s by crossing Cavalier King Charles Spaniel with Poodle, aiming to create a friendly family companion with low-shedding coat suitable for allergy sufferers

name: Central Asian Shepherd Dog
temperament: Independent, protective, confident, courageous, calm
description: An ancient, large, and powerful livestock guardian breed with massive bone structure and powerful muscles. Known for its courage, confidence, territorial nature, and natural protective instincts developed over thousands of years.
history: Ancient breed originating over 5,000 years ago in the geographical area between the Ural Mountains, the Caspian Sea, Asia Minor, and Northwest China. Shaped by natural selection in harsh climates and constant struggle against predators, developed to guard livestock and property across Central Asian regions.

name: Český fousek
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, devoted, eager to please, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, wire-haired versatile pointing dog of noble appearance with distinctive beard, known for endurance, adaptability, and devotion as a hunting companion
history: One of the oldest Czech hunting breeds, the Český fousek nearly became extinct after the World Wars but was restored in the 1960s through careful breeding programs using remaining typical specimens

name: Cesky Terrier
temperament: Calm, gentle, playful, intelligent, loyal, devoted
description: A well-muscled, short-legged hunting terrier with a silky, wavy coat and natural drop ears, known for its calm and gentle temperament compared to other terriers. Bred for hunting in Bohemian forests, this less-active and quieter terrier was developed to work effectively in packs.
history: Developed in the mid-20th century by Czech breeder František Horák, who crossed Scottish Terriers with Sealyham Terriers to create a tenacious but more tractable hunting terrier for the forests of Bohemia. Recognized by the FCI in 1963 and AKC in 2011.

name: Chart Polski
temperament: Independent, loyal, alert, calm, confident, courageous, protective, intelligent
description: Large, powerful, and muscular sighthound with an elegant build, short coat, and dignified appearance, known for its speed, courage, stamina, and reserved nature. Bred for hunting in Polish terrain with great determination and character.
history: Ancient Polish sighthound breed with origins dating back to the 13th century used by nobility for hunting hare fox deer boar and wolves. Nearly extinct after WWII but successfully revived through careful breeding programs in the latter half of the 20th century.

name: Chesapeake Bay Retriever
temperament: Intelligent, courageous, loyal, protective, confident
description: A powerful, muscular gundog with distinctive wavy, waterproof double coat and amber eyes, bred for retrieving waterfowl in the harsh conditions of the Chesapeake Bay
history: Developed in the 19th century along the Chesapeake Bay from two shipwrecked Newfoundland-type puppies (Sailor and Canton) bred with local retrievers to create a water gundog capable of withstanding icy conditions and retrieving waterfowl in harsh environments

name: Chien Français Blanc et Noir
temperament: Courageous, intelligent, work-focused, independent, loyal
description: Large, powerful, and elegant French scent hound distinguished by its distinctive black and white coat, bred for hunting large game in packs with endurance and persistence
history: Developed in 19th century France from crosses of Gascon-Saintongeois and the now-extinct Montaimboeuf hounds, with English Foxhounds. Meticulously created as a powerful and enduring pack hound specialized for hunting roe deer and large game

name: Chien Français Blanc et Orange
temperament: Gentle, calm, loyal, intelligent, energetic, work-focused
description: Large, noble, and muscular French scent hound with distinctive white and orange coat, bred for pack hunting large game such as deer and boar. Known for stamina, endurance, and ability to work collaboratively in packs.
history: Developed in France from old hounds of Saintonge and crosses with the Billy breed, rooted in various French hounds and English foxhounds. Created for pack hunting large game, recognized by FCI with official breed club founded in 1957.

name: Chien Français Tricolore
temperament: Work-focused, intelligent, energetic, loyal, athletic, alert
description: Large, elegant, and powerful French scenthound with distinctive tricolor coat, built for speed and endurance while pack hunting large game, particularly deer
history: Created in the mid-20th century by Henri de Falandre by crossing the Poitevin and the Gascon-Saintongeois for large game hunting particularly deer. Officially recognized by the FCI in 1957

name: Chihuahua
temperament: Alert, devoted, intelligent, confident, courageous, lively
description: World's smallest dog breed with long, flowing coat and bold, confident personality despite tiny size
history: Ancient breed descended from Techichi dogs of the Toltec civilization, refined in Mexican state of Chihuahua and standardized in Mexico and the US. Recognized by AKC since 1904

name: Chilean Terrier
temperament: Alert, energetic, loyal, intelligent, adaptable
description: A small, well-balanced, short-coated terrier known for its elegant yet sturdy build and lively, alert disposition. Often called the 'Fox Terrier Chileno' in its native country, this compact and agile breed combines intelligence with typical terrier energy.
history: Descended from Fox Terriers and other European terrier breeds brought to Chile by immigrants in the 18th and 19th centuries. Developed as a distinct breed for hunting vermin on farms and estates, becoming Chile's first recognized native breed, though not yet recognized by major international kennel clubs.

name: Chinese Crested
temperament: Affectionate, alert, playful, lively, intelligent, gentle
description: Small, elegant toy dog with fine-boned build that comes in two distinctive varieties: the Hairless with soft skin and tufts of hair, and the Powderpuff with a soft, straight double coat
history: Ancient breed believed to have descended from African or Mexican hairless dogs and refined in China. Chinese mariners kept them on ships to hunt vermin and traded them among port cities worldwide. Served as companions to Chinese nobility and were believed to possess magical healing powers

name: Chinese Shar-Pei
temperament: Loyal, independent, calm, alert, protective, intelligent
description: An ancient Chinese breed known for its distinctive deep wrinkles, hippopotamus-like muzzle, blue-black tongue, and compact, medium-sized build with a calm, independent temperament
history: Ancient Chinese breed from southern provinces dating back to the Han Dynasty, originally used as all-purpose farm dog for hunting, herding, guarding, and farm work. Nearly extinct in mid-20th century but revived from very small population by dedicated breeders in 1970s

name: Chinook
temperament: Intelligent, calm, devoted, friendly, eager to please
description: Large, athletic American sled dog with distinctive tawny double coat, known for combining strength, endurance, and a calm, gentle temperament. This versatile working breed was developed for drafting and sled-dog racing in New England conditions.
history: Developed in the early 20th century by polar explorer Arthur Treadwell Walden by crossing Mastiff-type dogs with Huskies and Belgian Shepherds. Named after the foundation male 'Chinook', bred to be an ideal sled dog for New England conditions. Recognized by the AKC in 2013.

name: Chipoo
temperament: Intelligent, alert, playful, affectionate, energetic, loyal
description: Small designer crossbreed combining the sassy, spirited personality of the Chihuahua with the intelligence and low-shedding coat of the Toy Poodle, resulting in a compact, alert companion with varied coat types
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States by crossing a Chihuahua with a Toy Poodle to combine the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat with the Chihuahua's spirited personality and compact companion size

name: Chippiparai
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, independent, alert, gentle, athletic
description: Ancient Indian sighthound breed from Tamil Nadu, known for its lean, elegant build and exceptional speed. This athletic breed has a very short, close coat typically in shades of fawn, reddish-brown, or brindle, and is prized for its hunting prowess and reserved loyalty to family.
history: Ancient sighthound breed developed by royalty in the Chippiparai region near Madurai in Tamil Nadu, India. Traditionally used by royal families and aristocrats for hunting wild boar, deer, and hare. The breed is prized for its speed, endurance, hunting prowess, and robust health adapted to the local climate.

name: Chiweenie
temperament: Loyal, alert, playful, confident, energetic, affectionate
description: A small, spirited crossbreed resulting from a Chihuahua and Dachshund with a compact elongated body, short legs, and confident personality combining traits from both parent breeds
history: Designer crossbreed that gained popularity in the late 1990s in North America, developed by crossing Chihuahua and Dachshund breeds to create a small companion dog

name: Chongqing Dog
temperament: Loyal, protective, alert, confident, independent
description: Medium-sized ancient Chinese spitz-type dog with distinctive bamboo-like tail, short reddish-brown coat, upright ears, and muscular build with protective temperament
history: Ancient Chinese breed from Chongqing and Sichuan provinces, traditionally used for hunting and guard work. Nearly extinct but undergoing revival efforts, with breed standards established by regional Chinese registries.

name: Chorkie
temperament: Alert, loyal, energetic, confident, affectionate, playful, intelligent
description: Small designer crossbreed combining Chihuahua and Yorkshire Terrier traits, known for having a big personality in a tiny body with spirited and affectionate temperament
history: Popular designer crossbreed developed in the late 20th century (likely 1990s) in the United States by intentionally crossing Chihuahua and Yorkshire Terrier to create a small, loyal companion dog with spirited temperament

name: Chortai
temperament: Independent, intelligent, loyal, athletic, calm
description: Rare, elegant sighthound from Central Asia with athletic build and short coat, bred for exceptional speed and endurance when coursing game across harsh steppe terrain
history: Ancient sighthound breed developed in the steppe regions of Kazakhstan and southern Russia for hunting hare, fox, and larger game. Bred purely for function—speed, stamina, and hunting ability—across vast steppes. Nearly extinct after Russian Revolution but preserved by enthusiasts.

name: Chow Chow
temperament: Loyal, independent, aloof, alert, calm, protective
description: An ancient, powerful, and sturdily built dog of Arctic spitz-type, known for its distinctive lion-like mane, unique blue-black tongue, stilted gait, and dense double coat
history: One of the oldest recognizable breeds with origins dating back over 2,000 years in northern China, where it served as hunting, herding, guarding, and companion dog. Refined during Tang Dynasty and recognized by AKC in 1903.

name: Chug
temperament: Playful, affectionate, loyal, alert, confident
description: A small companion dog resulting from a cross between a Pug and a Chihuahua, known for its compact build, expressive face, and spirited yet affectionate nature
history: A modern crossbreed developed in North America in the late 20th century by crossing a Pug with a Chihuahua. The goal was to create a small companion dog combining the affectionate nature of the Pug with the spirited personality of the Chihuahua. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Chukotka Sled Dog
temperament: Hardy, energetic, independent, loyal, alert, work-focused
description: Arctic spitz-type sled dog from northeastern Siberia, developed by the Chukchi people for sledding, endurance, and transportation in extreme cold conditions
history: Ancient breed developed by the indigenous Chukchi people of northeastern Siberia for sledding, hunting, and survival in Arctic conditions. Traditional sled dog bred for centuries for transportation and endurance, considered the foundation breed for the Siberian Husky.

name: Cimarrón Uruguayo
temperament: Courageous, intelligent, loyal, protective, calm, confident
description: Large, powerful molossoid breed with robust bone structure and athletic build, known for its balanced temperament, courage, and protective instincts. This rugged working breed features a short brindle or fawn coat and strong working heritage.
history: Descended from dogs brought by Spanish and Portuguese colonizers, developed as a feral breed then shaped by natural selection in the wild. Used for herding cattle, guarding property, and hunting large game. Officially recognized in Uruguay in 1989 and recognized by the FCI.

name: Cirneco dell'Etna
temperament: Affectionate, independent, alert, gentle, intelligent
description: Ancient medium-sized sighthound with short coat, elegant slender build, and distinctive large ears. Bred for hunting on the rugged volcanic terrain of Mount Etna, this athletic breed combines endurance with graceful movement.
history: Ancient Sicilian breed developed for rabbit hunting on Mount Etna's rugged volcanic terrain for over 2,500 years. Nearly extinct, the breed was revived in the 20th century and is now recognized by FCI and AKC Foundation Stock Service.

name: Clumber Spaniel
temperament: Gentle, loyal, calm, intelligent, playful
description: A long, low, and substantial flushing spaniel with a massive head, heavy bone structure, pensive expression, and predominantly white coat with lemon or orange markings
history: Developed in the 18th century, associated with the French Duc de Noailles and Duke of Newcastle's Clumber Park estate in Nottinghamshire. Likely developed from Alpine Spaniel, Basset Hound crosses. Prized by British royalty and nobility as gentleman's hunting spaniels for game flushing

name: Cockapoo
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, affectionate, playful, outgoing
description: Popular crossbreed between Cocker Spaniel and Poodle with soft, wavy or curly coat and friendly temperament. One of the oldest and most popular 'designer' breeds, known for intelligence, affectionate nature, and low-shedding coat.
history: One of the first 'designer breeds' with intentional breeding starting in the United States in the 1960s. Goal was to combine the outgoing, loving personality of the Cocker Spaniel with the low-shedding, intelligent nature of the Poodle to create a healthy companion dog

name: Cocker Spaniel
temperament: Friendly, affectionate, playful, intelligent, outgoing
description: Compact sporting breed with silky coat, merry expression, and affectionate nature
history: Descended from English Cocker Spaniels, developed in the US for smaller size and specialized for woodcock hunting; officially recognized as a distinct breed by the AKC in 1946

name: Colombian Fino Hound
temperament: Affectionate, docile, energetic, intelligent, loyal, work-focused, devoted, alert
description: A medium-sized Colombian scenthound of noble appearance with athletic build, characterized by long ears, a sad expression, short smooth coat, and exceptional scenting abilities. Known in its native country as the Sabueso Fino Colombiano.
history: Traditional Colombian scent hound descended from European and North American scenthounds brought to Colombia, adapted over centuries to local terrain and climate. Developed by rural hunters for tenacity and scenting skills from local and Spanish scent hounds. Officially recognized in Colombia in early 2000s and received provisional FCI recognition in 2014.

name: Continental Bulldog
temperament: Friendly, confident, calm, alert, adaptable, loyal
description: Medium-sized, athletically built bulldog with a broad head, muscular compact body, and short coat, designed for improved health and breathing compared to traditional bulldogs with smooth movement and endurance
history: Developed in Switzerland in the early 2000s by breeder Imelda Angehrn to create a healthier more athletic bulldog with fewer respiratory issues by crossing English Bulldogs with Olde English Bulldogges, provisionally recognized by FCI in 2022

name: Corgipoo
temperament: Intelligent, friendly, playful, affectionate, energetic, alert
description: A designer crossbreed between a Cardigan Welsh Corgi and Miniature Poodle, known for its compact size, intelligence, and friendly nature. Coat type, size, and temperament can vary significantly based on genetic inheritance from the parent breeds.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States by combining the Cardigan Welsh Corgi with a Miniature Poodle. The aim was to create an intelligent companion dog with the low-shedding coat of the Poodle and the sturdy, affectionate nature of the Corgi. Not formally recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Corsican Dog
temperament: Independent, alert, loyal, protective, intelligent
description: Medium-sized, versatile and athletic dog native to the island of Corsica with a rustic build and alert demeanor. This ancient multi-purpose breed combines hunting ability with herding and guarding instincts, displaying both independence and devotion to family.
history: Ancient multi-purpose breed native to Corsica, used for hunting wild boar, herding livestock, and property protection. Nearly extinct in the 20th century but revived by enthusiasts starting in 1989. Recognized by French Kennel Club and provisionally by FCI as the Cursinu.

name: Coton de Tulear
temperament: Happy, playful, affectionate, alert, friendly, intelligent, adaptable
description: Small companion dog with soft cotton-like white coat, charming personality and cheerful temperament
history: Originated as royal dog and companion to nobles in Madagascar, developed from small white dogs brought by traders. Named for city of Tuléar and recognized by FCI and AKC as beloved adaptable companion breed

name: Cretan Hound
temperament: Intelligent, independent, alert, energetic, athletic, gentle, affectionate
description: Ancient, primitive Mediterranean sighthound from Greece with slender, athletic build, erect ears, and curled tail. Known for exceptional speed, agility, and keen hunting instincts over harsh, rocky terrain.
history: Ancient Hellenic breed native to the Greek island of Crete, with depictions dating back over 4,000 years. Developed for hunting hare and wild rabbits over harsh, rocky terrain. Regarded as one of the oldest European hounds and recognized provisionally by the FCI.

name: Croatian Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, alert, energetic, loyal, work-focused, eager to please
description: Medium-sized, agile herding dog with a distinctive wavy or curly black coat and athletic build. This intelligent and lively breed displays an alert expression and was developed for livestock work in Croatia.
history: Ancient herding breed believed to have descended from dogs brought by the Croats in the 7th century, developed in eastern regions of Slavonia as versatile farm and herding dog. Referenced in historical documents from 1374, systematic breeding program began in 1935 with FCI recognition granted in 1969.

name: Curly-Coated Retriever
temperament: Confident, intelligent, independent, loyal, energetic
description: Large, elegant retriever and one of the oldest retriever breeds, distinguished by its distinctive tight, water-resistant curly coat and confident, athletic temperament
history: One of the oldest retriever breeds, developed in England during the 18th-19th century from English Water Spaniel, St. John's Newfoundland, and Poodle ancestry, prized by gamekeepers for waterfowl hunting versatility

name: Czechoslovakian Wolfdog
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, energetic, courageous, alert, independent
description: Athletic, wolf-like working breed with a powerful build and primitive appearance, developed from German Shepherds and Carpathian wolves. Known for high energy, endurance, and independent nature with distinctive wolf-like features.
history: Developed in 1955 in Czechoslovakia through biological experiment crossing German Shepherd Dogs with Carpathian wolves. Goal was to blend trainability of German Shepherd with stamina and pack instinct of wolf for military border patrol use. Officially recognized by FCI in 1989.

name: Dachshund
temperament: Courageous, intelligent, independent, alert, playful, devoted
description: Long-bodied, short-legged hound with distinctive elongated build, available in three coat types (smooth, wirehaired, and longhaired). Originally bred for hunting badgers in burrows, this bold and lively scent hound is known for its courageous, intelligent, and playful personality.
history: Developed in Germany over 300 years ago (records date to 15th century) specifically for hunting badgers in their burrows. The name 'Dachshund' translates to 'badger dog' in German. Wirehaired varieties were developed by crossing with terriers for work in thorny terrain, while longhaired varieties were created using spaniels for colder climates.

name: Dalmadoodle
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, friendly, loyal, alert
description: An intelligent and athletic mixed breed cross between Dalmatian and Standard Poodle, combining the Dalmatian's distinctive spotted appearance and dignified nature with the Poodle's keen intelligence and potentially low-shedding coat
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed by mixing a Dalmatian with a Standard Poodle to combine the Dalmatian's distinctive appearance and athletic ability with the Poodle's intelligence and potentially hypoallergenic coat. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Dalmatian
temperament: Energetic, outgoing, intelligent, alert, athletic
description: Medium-sized, athletic dog with distinctive black or liver spotted white coat and elegant build, originally bred as a carriage dog with exceptional endurance
history: Ancient breed with roots in Dalmatia, Croatia, originally bred as carriage dogs to run for miles alongside horse-drawn coaches, guarding horses and coaches. Also served as circus performers and iconic firehouse mascots, particularly associated with American fire departments. Recognized by AKC in 1888.

name: Dandie Dinmont Terrier
temperament: Independent, intelligent, loyal, affectionate, confident, gentle
description: Small, long, low-bodied terrier with distinctive topknot of silky hair, large soulful eyes, and unique pepper or mustard colored crisp double coat. Originally bred for hunting badgers and otters with a gentle expression despite their tenacious nature.
history: Developed in the 18th century in the border region between Scotland and England for hunting badgers and otters. Named after a character in Sir Walter Scott's 1815 novel 'Guy Mannering'. Recognized by The Kennel Club (UK) in 1875 and AKC in 1886.

name: Danish Spitz
temperament: Friendly, alert, intelligent, energetic, loyal
description: Medium-sized, compact spitz-type dog with a dense white double coat, pricked ears, and curled tail carried over the back. Known for its friendly, lively disposition and wedge-shaped head with alert expression.
history: An old Danish landrace breed used on farms as an all-purpose companion, watchdog, and vermin catcher. The breed faced near extinction in the 20th century but was revived by dedicated breeders and officially recognized by the Danish Kennel Club in 1988

name: Danish-Swedish Farmdog
temperament: Alert, friendly, energetic, intelligent, adaptable, eager to please
description: Small, compact, and slightly rectangular working dog with short smooth coat and alert expression, developed as a versatile all-purpose farm dog in Denmark and Sweden
history: Ancient Scandinavian farm dog breed from eastern Denmark and southernmost Sweden, used as versatile all-purpose farm dog for ratting, herding, guarding, and companionship. Officially recognized in Denmark and Sweden in 1987, by FCI in 2008-2019, and The Kennel Club (UK) in 2019

name: Denmark Feist
temperament: Energetic, alert, intelligent, courageous, loyal
description: Small to medium agile hunting dog of the Treeing Feist type, specifically developed for treeing small game, especially squirrels. Known for its tenacity, high energy, and alert nature with strong hunting instincts.
history: The Denmark Feist is a specific line of Treeing Feist developed by the Slade family in Denmark, Tennessee, originating from dogs brought to the area in 1917. Developed from terrier and hound crosses, these historic American hunting dogs were prized by early settlers for their vermin-hunting and treeing abilities.

name: Dikkulak
temperament: Alert, energetic, loyal, intelligent
description: A rare Turkish landrace dog characterized by distinctive erect ears from which it gets its name ('prick ear'). Small to medium-sized with spitz-type features and sighthound heritage, bred for guarding homesteads and hunting in rural Anatolia.
history: The Dikkulak is a rare, regional landrace from eastern Turkey, primarily found in rural Anatolia. Its name translates to 'prick ear' in Turkish, distinguishing it by its erect ears. Traditionally used for guarding homesteads and controlling vermin, this breed has been shaped by its environment and function rather than formal breeding programs. Not officially recognized by major international kennel clubs.

name: Dingo
temperament: Independent, alert, intelligent, adaptable
description: An ancient, primitive wild canid native to Australia with a lean, medium-sized build, wolf-like appearance, pricked ears, and bushy tail. Recognized for its reddish-tan coat and high intelligence, it is considered a wild dog rather than a domesticated breed.
history: Ancient wild canid that arrived in Australia approximately 3,500-4,000 years ago, establishing itself as the continent's apex predator and adapting to diverse harsh Australian environments

name: Doberman Pinscher
temperament: Intelligent, alert, loyal, energetic, confident
description: Medium-large, sleek, athletic and powerful dog known for exceptional loyalty and intelligence. This elegant working breed combines strength with agility and has a confident, alert demeanor.
history: Created in late 19th century by Karl Friedrich Louis Dobermann using breeds such as the Rottweiler, German Pinscher, and others for protection and companionship

name: Dogo Argentino
temperament: Courageous, loyal, protective, confident, intelligent, energetic
description: Large, powerful, all-white mastiff-type dog with massive head and athletic build, originally developed for big-game hunting with exceptional courage and strength
history: Developed in Argentina in the 1920s by Dr. Antonio Nores Martinez who systematically crossed the now-extinct Cordoba Fighting Dog with Great Dane, Boxer, Bull Terrier, and Pointer to create a versatile big-game hunter for wild boar and puma

name: Dogo Sardesco
temperament: Protective, loyal, courageous, intelligent, confident, independent
description: Large, powerful Molossian-type guardian breed from Sardinia with a sturdy, muscular build and strong protective instincts. Also known as the Pastore Fonnese, this ancient landrace breed was traditionally used for livestock and property protection.
history: Ancient landrace breed from Sardinia used for centuries as a fierce guardian of flocks and property against predators and thieves. Provisionally recognized by the FCI as the Pastore Fonnese, though not recognized by major international kennel clubs.

name: Dogue Brasileiro
temperament: Protective, loyal, intelligent, courageous, calm, devoted
description: Medium to large, powerful mastiff-type breed developed in Brazil with strong guarding instincts, athletic build, and short coat. Known for its devotion to family and protective temperament.
history: Developed in Brazil in the 1970s and 1980s by crossing various breeds including Boxers, Bullmastiffs, and local breeds to create a versatile family guardian and protection dog. Not recognized by major international kennel clubs but has official breed standard in Brazil.

name: Dogue de Bordeaux
temperament: Loyal, affectionate, courageous, calm, protective, devoted
description: Massive, powerful French mastiff with distinctive short, wrinkled muzzle and red mask. This imposing molossoid dog combines a muscular build with a calm, loyal disposition and protective temperament.
history: One of the oldest French breeds and ancient molossoid type dating to 14th century. Traditionally used for guarding, pulling carts, and big game hunting. Nearly extinct after World Wars but restored by dedicated breeders and standardized in 19th century.

name: Donggyeongi
temperament: Friendly, gentle, alert, intelligent, loyal, courageous, adaptable
description: A medium-sized, spitz-type dog native to Korea, characterized by its naturally short or bobbed tail, alert expression, and gentle, friendly disposition
history: An ancient indigenous Korean breed from the Silla Dynasty period, originating in Gyeongju. Primarily used for hunting and valued for companionship and as a guard dog for centuries. The breed faced near extinction but was revived through dedicated conservation efforts and was designated a Korean National Treasure in 2012. Recognized as a Korean cultural heritage breed but not by major international kennel clubs.

name: Dorkie
temperament: Affectionate, intelligent, playful, alert, loyal, courageous
description: Small designer crossbreed combining Dachshund and Yorkshire Terrier traits with compact body and variable coat type. Known for spirited and affectionate personality, though appearance can vary significantly depending on traits inherited from each parent.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the late 20th century by intentionally breeding Dachshunds and Yorkshire Terriers in the United States to create a small companion dog combining traits from both parents. Not recognized by major kennel clubs like AKC or FCI.

name: Doxiepoo
temperament: Intelligent, playful, affectionate, loyal, alert
description: Small to medium designer crossbreed between a Dachshund and a Poodle, known for its variable appearance and intelligent, playful nature combining traits from both parent breeds
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States by crossing a Dachshund and typically a Miniature Poodle to create a low-shedding companion dog combining traits from both parent breeds. Not recognized by major kennel clubs as a standardized breed

name: Drentse Patrijshond
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, loyal, gentle, energetic, alert
description: Medium-sized versatile hunting dog from the Netherlands with distinctive white coat and orange/brown markings, known for pointing and retrieving abilities. This intelligent spaniel-type breed serves as both an excellent gundog and devoted family companion.
history: Ancient Dutch breed originating in the 16th century from Spioenen dogs from Spain, developed in Drenthe province as versatile farm and hunting dog for pointing, tracking and retrieving game birds. Nearly extinct after WWII but successfully revived.

name: Drever
temperament: Alert, calm, friendly, intelligent, loyal, even-tempered, courageous
description: Short-legged, long-bodied Swedish scent hound with distinctive white markings, known for endurance, keen nose, and even-tempered nature. Built for tracking deer and game over difficult terrain with robust, agile construction.
history: Developed in early 20th century Sweden from German Westphalian Dachsbracke imported around 1910, refined to be slower and better suited for driving deer toward hunters. Name 'Drever' officially adopted in 1947 by Swedish Kennel Club.

name: Dunker
temperament: Friendly, calm, gentle, intelligent, energetic
description: Medium-sized Norwegian scenthound with athletic build and distinctive harlequin, blue-merle, or marbled coat pattern, known for endurance and bred specifically for hare hunting
history: Developed in Norway during the early 19th century by Captain Wilhelm Dunker, crossing Russian Harlequin Hounds with reliable local Norwegian scenthounds to create a superior hound for hunting hare on foot in challenging terrain

name: Dutch Shepherd
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, alert, energetic, athletic, work-focused, independent
description: Medium-sized herding dog with athletic, muscular build and distinctive brindle coat pattern. An intelligent, versatile, and work-focused breed of Dutch origin that served as an all-purpose farm dog.
history: Developed in the Netherlands as an all-purpose farm dog for herding sheep and cattle, cart-pulling, and guarding. Nearly extinct after WWII but preserved through dedicated breeding efforts. Known for its brindle coat in three varieties: short, long, and wire-haired.

name: Dutch Smoushond
temperament: Friendly, alert, affectionate, playful, lively, intelligent
description: A small, lively terrier-type companion dog of Dutch origin, characterized by its shaggy, wiry yellow coat, cheerful expression, and friendly disposition. Originally bred as a stable ratter in 19th century Amsterdam, it is now a cherished family companion.
history: Developed in 19th century Amsterdam as a stable dog for controlling vermin. Nearly faced extinction after World War II but was revived through dedicated breeding program led by Mrs. H.M. Barkman van der Weel in the 1970s. Recognized by FCI and UK Kennel Club.

name: East European Shepherd
temperament: Loyal, protective, confident, intelligent, work-focused
description: Large, powerful working dog developed in Soviet Union from German Shepherds and local breeds. Similar to German Shepherd but larger and more cold-resistant, with a slightly elongated body built for strength and endurance in harsh climates. Possesses a noble, confident demeanor.
history: Developed in Soviet Union during 1930s-1950s from German Shepherds and local breeds for military, police, and border patrol duties in harsh climates

name: East Siberian Laika
temperament: Independent, alert, intelligent, courageous, loyal, work-focused
description: Medium to large rugged spitz-type hunting dog from the Siberian taiga with erect ears, curled tail, and thick double coat. Built for harsh climates with strong working drive and balanced temperament.
history: Developed from aboriginal spitz-type dogs by indigenous peoples of the East Siberian taiga region for hunting big game including bear and wild boar. Standardized in mid-20th century for versatile hunting abilities and adaptability to harsh climates.

name: Ecuadorian Hairless Dog
temperament: Calm, alert, loyal, gentle, affectionate, intelligent, independent
description: Rare, ancient hairless landrace breed from Ecuador with medium-sized, sleek body, elegant build, and expressive features. This indigenous breed is known for its warm skin, calm demeanor, and loyalty to family.
history: Ancient indigenous landrace breed from pre-Columbian coastal Ecuador, believed to share ancestry with other South American hairless dogs. Extremely rare with limited breeding programs and preservation efforts. Not recognized by major kennel clubs but described in regional breed registries.

name: English Cocker Spaniel
temperament: Merry, affectionate, intelligent, energetic, playful, gentle, eager to please
description: A medium-sized, merry sporting spaniel known for its silky coat, long lush ears, expressive eyes, and energetic attitude. Compact and athletic, built for endurance and agility in the field with exceptional abilities as both hunting companion and family dog.
history: Developed in England from land spaniels as a specialist flushing spaniel for woodcock and other game birds. Officially separated from American Cocker Spaniel in the 1940s by breed standards.

name: English Foxhound
temperament: Friendly, energetic, gentle, independent, courageous
description: Large, athletic scent hound with classic pack-hound build, bred for fox hunting with exceptional endurance and stamina. Features a short, hard, dense coat and powerful, musical voice with distinctive bay.
history: Developed in England since the 16th century specifically for fox hunting, with studbooks maintained by the Masters of Foxhounds Association since the 1700s representing some of the most complete breed records

name: English Mastiff
temperament: Gentle, loyal, protective, calm, courageous
description: Massive, powerful giant breed with a short fawn, apricot, or brindle coat, distinctive black mask, and dignified demeanor. Known for combining imposing size with a gentle, loyal temperament and protective instincts.
history: Ancient breed known in Britain for over 2,000 years, descended from Roman Molossian war dogs. Used as estate guardian, war dog, and big-game hunter. Modern breed refined in medieval England and saved from extinction after World War II.

name: English Setter
temperament: Friendly, gentle, affectionate, intelligent, energetic
description: Elegant and athletic gundog distinguished by its unique speckled (belton) coat, feathered markings, and graceful movement, known for gentle hunting instincts and friendly disposition
history: Developed in England over 400 years ago from spaniel stock, refined in the 19th century by Edward Laverack and R. Purcell Llewellin to create a specialized bird dog for locating and setting game birds

name: English Shepherd
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, alert, devoted, adaptable
description: Exceptionally intelligent and versatile medium-sized herding dog with a dense double coat and strong work ethic, developed as an all-purpose farm dog with devotion to family and adaptable temperament
history: Developed in the United States from British and Scottish collie-type farm dogs brought by early settlers, bred as versatile all-purpose farm dogs for herding livestock, guarding property, and hunting vermin

name: English Springer Spaniel
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, energetic, eager to please, playful
description: Medium-sized athletic sporting dog with long ears, feathered silky coat, and enthusiastic, eager-to-please personality. Known for their compact body, gentle intelligent expression, and versatile gundog abilities.
history: Developed in 19th century England as a versatile gundog to find and 'spring' game for the net, falcon, or gun. Originally separated from smaller 'cocker' spaniels by size and hunting use. Officially recognized by The Kennel Club in 1902 and AKC in 1910.

name: English Toy Terrier (Black & Tan)
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, playful
description: Small, elegant black and tan terrier with fine-boned build and distinctive pointed, candle-flame ears
history: Descended from the Old English Black and Tan Terrier, bred as a ratter in Victorian England and now a rare companion breed. Recognized by The Kennel Club (UK).

name: Entlebucher Mountain Dog
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, alert, confident, devoted
description: The smallest of the four Swiss Mountain Dogs, the Entlebucher is a compact, sturdy, and muscular herding dog with a distinctive tricolor coat. Known for its intelligence, loyalty, and agile working ability.
history: The smallest of the four Swiss Sennenhund breeds, developed in the Entlebuch valley region of Switzerland as an all-around farm dog for herding cattle and driving livestock to market. First documented in 1889 and recognized for its working versatility.

name: Erbi Txakur
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, alert, loyal, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, agile scent hound from the Basque Country, specifically bred for hunting hare and small game, known for its endurance, keen sense of smell, and ability to work over challenging terrain
history: Ancient landrace breed from the Basque regions of Spain, used for centuries by local hunters for stamina and scenting ability in pursuit of hare and small game over challenging terrain. Recognized by the Real Sociedad Canina de España (RSCE) and regional registries.

name: Eskapoo
temperament: Intelligent, friendly, playful, affectionate, alert, energetic
description: Small to medium designer breed combining the fluffy coat and friendly nature of the American Eskimo Dog with the intelligence and low-shedding curly coat of the Miniature Poodle
history: Modern designer breed developed by crossing American Eskimo Dogs with Miniature Poodles to create an intelligent companion dog with a low-shedding coat. Part of the popular Poodle-mix trend that began in the late 20th century. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Estonian Hound
temperament: Friendly, energetic, alert, intelligent, calm
description: Medium-sized, strong-boned scent hound with short, smooth tricolor coat and sturdy build, known for its tenacity in hunting small game and stamina in Estonia's terrain
history: Developed in Estonia between 1947-1954 after Soviet decree mandating each republic establish a national breed. Created by crossing local hunting dogs with Beagles, Swiss hounds, and English Foxhounds to create versatile hunting dog suited to Estonian terrain under restrictive hunting laws. Officially recognized by FCI in 2019.

name: Estrela Mountain Dog
temperament: Loyal, protective, intelligent, calm, courageous, independent
description: Large, powerful, and imposing livestock guardian dog from the Estrela Mountains of Portugal with a dense double coat and protective temperament. Known for its intelligence, loyalty, and calm yet fearless demeanor, this athletic breed combines strength with a naturally protective nature.
history: One of the oldest breeds in Portugal, developed in the Estrela Mountains as a livestock guardian to protect flocks from wolves and other predators. Ancient breed with lineage tied to Mastiff-type dogs of the region, recognized by FCI.

name: Eurasier
temperament: Calm, confident, loyal, intelligent, gentle, reserved
description: Medium-sized Spitz-type dog with balanced proportions and thick, plush double coat that comes in a variety of colors. Known for its calm, devoted nature and confident temperament.
history: Developed in Germany in the 1960s by Julius Wipfel by crossing Chow Chow, Wolfspitz (Keeshond), and Samoyed to create an ideal Spitz-type family companion dog. Recognized by the FCI.

name: Faroese Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, alert, work-focused, independent
description: Medium-sized spitz-type herding dog from the Faroe Islands with dense, weather-resistant double coat adapted to harsh Nordic climate. This landrace breed is known for its hardiness, intelligence, and ability to work sheep in rugged terrain.
history: Ancient landrace breed descended from Norse dogs brought to the Faroe Islands by Vikings over a thousand years ago. Related to Icelandic Sheepdog, evolved in isolation to work sheep in challenging climate. Nearly extinct by 1960s but restored through preservation efforts managed by Danish Kennel Club and local enthusiasts.

name: Field Spaniel
temperament: Docile, intelligent, affectionate, gentle, sensitive
description: Medium-sized sporting dog with silky, glossy coat in black, liver, or roan colors, built for endurance and agility in the field with an adaptable, noble temperament
history: Developed in England during the late 19th century from Cocker Spaniels and other spaniel varieties, initially for show but later redeveloped for field work. The breed faced near extinction after World War II but was revived by dedicated breeders.

name: Fila Brasileiro
temperament: Loyal, protective, courageous, devoted, independent
description: Large, powerful Molosser-type mastiff guardian breed with distinctive loose skin, natural guarding instincts, and tracking ability
history: Developed in Brazil from Mastiff, Bulldog, and Bloodhound ancestry for plantation work, estate guarding, cattle herding, and tracking large game. Recognized by FCI but not AKC-recognized.

name: Finnish Hound
temperament: Friendly, energetic, independent, alert, intelligent
description: Medium-sized tricolor scent hound developed for endurance hunting of hare and fox in challenging Nordic terrain, known for its energetic nature and melodious voice
history: Developed in Finland since the 1800s by crossing various European hounds including French, German, Russian, and Swedish hounds to create a scent hound capable of hunting in the country's snowy, difficult terrain and harsh Nordic conditions

name: Finnish Lapphund
temperament: Friendly, alert, intelligent, calm, courageous, loyal, adaptable
description: Medium-sized, sturdy spitz-type dog with a thick profuse double coat and fox-like features, originally bred for herding reindeer in arctic conditions by the Sami people
history: Ancient spitz breed developed by the indigenous Sami people of Lapland to herd and guard reindeer in Arctic conditions. Nearly extinct after WWII but successfully restored by dedicated Finnish breeders, officially recognized by Finnish Kennel Club in 1945

name: Finnish Spitz
temperament: Friendly, alert, intelligent, independent, playful, courageous
description: Medium-sized fox-like spitz hunting dog with dense golden-red double coat, erect ears, curled plumed tail, and alert expression. Known as Finland's national dog and called the 'barking pointer' for their distinctive hunting style.
history: Ancient Finnish breed developed through centuries of selective breeding to hunt all types of game from squirrels to bears. Nearly extinct in late 1800s before restoration efforts. Became Finland's national dog in 1979, bred specifically for barking ability and stamina in harsh climates.

name: Flat-Coated Retriever
temperament: Friendly, outgoing, optimistic, intelligent, energetic, playful, devoted
description: Large, elegant sporting dog with a lustrous, sleek flat coat in solid black or liver, known for their cheerful, perpetually youthful 'Peter Pan' personality and optimistic temperament
history: Developed in mid-19th century England by crossing St. John's Water Dog with various retrievers, setters, spaniels and sheepdogs to create a versatile gundog for work on both land and water

name: Florida Cracker Cur
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, energetic, protective, work-focused, alert
description: A medium-sized, athletic cur-type herding dog native to Florida, known for its intelligence, loyalty, and tenacity in working cattle and hunting in rugged terrain
history: Descended from Spanish dogs brought to Florida in the 1500s, developed by early cattlemen ('Crackers') to work the tough Florida Cracker Cattle in rugged terrain. The breed faced extinction but was revived by dedicated enthusiasts and maintained by regional breed organizations.

name: Foxhoodle
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, friendly, alert, loyal, playful
description: A modern mixed breed cross between a Foxhound and Standard Poodle that combines the Poodle's trainability and potentially low-shedding coat with the Foxhound's stamina, scenting abilities, and pack mentality. Coat type and temperament can vary significantly depending on which parent's traits are more dominant.
history: A modern designer crossbreed created by mixing American or English Foxhound with Standard Poodle to combine scenting ability, stamina, and sociable nature with intelligence and potentially lower-shedding coat. Not recognized by major kennel clubs

name: French Bulldog
temperament: Affectionate, playful, alert, adaptable, smart
description: Small, compact muscular companion dog known for its distinctive large bat-like ears, flat smooshed face, and short smooth coat. Originally developed as a beloved companion breed with a playful, alert disposition.
history: Developed in 19th century France when English lacemakers brought Toy Bulldogs to France and crossed them with local French ratters becoming highly fashionable in Parisian society

name: French Spaniel
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, calm, adaptable, eager to please
description: Medium to large versatile pointing dog with elegant silky brown and white coat and gentle expression, known for hunting ability and calm temperament. One of the oldest spaniel-type pointing breeds.
history: One of the oldest pointing breeds, descending from longhaired setting spaniels used since the Middle Ages. Described by Gaston Fébus in the 14th century, standardized in the 19th century, and revived in early 20th century after near extinction

name: Frenchton
temperament: Friendly, playful, intelligent, affectionate, adaptable
description: Small, compact crossbreed combining French Bulldog and Boston Terrier traits with distinctive bat ears, muscular build, and expressive features. Known for its sturdy build with a slightly longer snout than the French Bulldog parent.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States starting in the 1990s, combining French Bulldog and Boston Terrier to create a healthier companion with fewer respiratory issues while retaining the friendly, compact nature of both parent breeds

name: Frug
temperament: Playful, affectionate, outgoing, adaptable, alert, happy
description: Small, charming companion crossbreed resulting from a cross between a French Bulldog and Pug, known for its playful personality, distinctive flat-faced (brachycephalic) appearance, and compact build
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in late 20th century by crossing French Bulldog and Pug to combine the desirable companion traits of both parent breeds

name: Gascon Saintongeois
temperament: Gentle, calm, affectionate, loyal, intelligent, work-focused
description: Large, noble and powerfully built French scenthound with distinctive tricolor coat and elegant appearance. Known for its superb nose, melodious voice, and pack-hunting endurance, this athletic breed was developed for hunting large game in southwestern France.
history: Developed in the mid-19th century by Comte Joseph de Carayon-Latour, who crossed the last remaining Saintonge Hounds with the Grand Bleu de Gascogne. Originally used for hunting wolves, now used for hunting deer and wild boar. FCI recognized.

name: Gaucho Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, protective, alert, calm
description: Medium-sized, athletic herding dog from southern Brazil with dense double coat, strong working instincts, and calm yet protective demeanor
history: Developed in Rio Grande do Sul, southern Brazil by Gaúcho shepherds through crossbreeding European herding dogs brought by immigrants, including Collies, German Shepherds, and Serra da Estrela Mountain Dogs. Selectively bred for herding and guarding livestock in the region's unique terrain and open ranges. Recognized by CBKC and provisionally recognized by FCI.

name: Georgian Shepherd
temperament: Protective, independent, loyal, courageous, calm, confident
description: Large, powerful livestock guardian dog from the Caucasus mountains of Georgia, with a thick weather-resistant double coat and strong protective instincts. An ancient landrace breed known for its courage, decision-making abilities, and formidable guarding capabilities against large predators like wolves and bears.
history: Ancient landrace breed developed in the Caucasus mountains of Georgia for centuries as a formidable livestock guardian, protecting sheep and cattle from large predators like wolves and bears. Closely related to and sometimes considered a regional variant of the Caucasian Shepherd Dog, recognized by FCI as part of the broader Caucasian Shepherd Dog (FCI No. 328).

name: Gerberian Shepsky
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, alert, protective, independent
description: Large athletic mixed breed combining the loyalty and intelligence of the German Shepherd Dog with the endurance and striking appearance of the Siberian Husky. A powerful crossbreed with thick double coat and high energy, though characteristics can vary significantly depending on genetic inheritance.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in North America in the late 20th or early 21st century, intentionally combining the German Shepherd Dog and Siberian Husky to blend intelligence, loyalty, working drive, and endurance. Not recognized by major kennel clubs as it is not a standardized breed.

name: German Hound
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, calm, confident
description: Medium-sized scent hound with athletic build and tricolor short coat, bred for hunting in packs with elegant, resilient structure and muscular build
history: The German Hound (Deutsche Bracke) was developed in the Westphalia region of Germany from various local Bracke types for hunting wild boar and deer. The official breed standard was established in 1900 after breed clubs merged in 1896 to unify the type.

name: German Longhaired Pointer
temperament: Intelligent, gentle, calm, loyal, eager to please, work-focused
description: Noble, versatile medium to large gundog with elegant build and distinctive long, silky, weather-resistant coat with feathering. Developed as an all-purpose German hunting breed known for calm intelligence and athletic ability.
history: Developed in 19th-century Germany from various pointer breeds, setters, bird dogs, water dogs, and scent hounds to create a versatile and noble gundog for work in field, forest, and water terrain

name: German Pinscher
temperament: Alert, courageous, intelligent, energetic, confident, loyal
description: Medium-sized, well-muscled working dog with elegant, athletic build and smooth coat. Known for its high-spirited, alert, and confident demeanor, this is one of Germany's oldest breeds.
history: Ancient German breed developed for vermin control, ratting, and farm work. Ancestor to the Doberman Pinscher and Miniature Pinscher. Nearly faced extinction after World War II but was revived by Werner Jung in West Germany in the late 1950s. Recognized by AKC in 2003.

name: German Roughhaired Pointer
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, confident, work-focused
description: Medium to large versatile gundog with dense, harsh, bristly (stichelhaarig) weatherproof coat and distinctive beard. Known for strong pointing instincts and calm, even temperament, this noble hunting companion is considered the oldest of the German rough-coated pointing breeds.
history: The oldest of the German rough-coated pointing breeds, developed in the late 19th century from remnants of old German rough-coated bird dogs (Hühnerhunde) crossed with Pudelpointer and Griffon breeds. Standardized with focus on functional hunting ability as a versatile rough-coated hunting dog.

name: German Shepherd
temperament: Confident, courageous, intelligent, loyal, protective
description: Large, athletic working dog with erect ears, noble expression, and confident bearing. Known for high intelligence, loyalty, courage, and versatility in herding and working roles.
history: Developed in 1899 by Captain Max von Stephanitz to create the ideal herding dog, standardized from local shepherd dogs. Intelligence and trainability led to worldwide use in police and military roles.

name: German Sheprador
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, friendly, energetic, protective, eager to please
description: Large, athletic crossbreed with sturdy build combining the intelligence and loyalty of German Shepherd with the friendly, outgoing retrieving nature of Labrador Retriever
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in late 20th century as part of the 'designer dog' trend, combining working abilities of German Shepherd with friendly temperament of Labrador Retriever to create a versatile family companion and working dog

name: German Shorthaired Pointer
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, energetic, eager to please, athletic
description: A versatile, athletic, and noble all-purpose hunting dog with a distinctive short, dense liver and white coat, known for its intelligence, energy, and friendly disposition
history: Developed in 19th-century Germany by crossing Spanish Pointers, Bloodhound, and local German hunting dogs to create a versatile pointer-retriever capable of working on land and water. Recognized by AKC in 1930.

name: German Spaniel
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, adaptable, confident, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, muscular hunting spaniel known as the Deutscher Wachtelhund in its native Germany, with a dense wavy double coat featuring feathering and strong tracking abilities developed for versatile forest hunting
history: Developed in Germany in the 1890s from older German landrace dogs known as Stöberhund, crossing various spaniels to create a versatile hunting dog capable of tracking, flushing, and retrieving game in dense forests and water. FCI-recognized and bred primarily for hunting ability and versatility rather than appearance.

name: German Spitz
temperament: Alert, intelligent, confident, lively, devoted
description: Compact, lively spitz-type dog with abundant double coat, fox-like expression, and characteristic plumed tail carried over the back. Available in multiple size varieties with alert, attentive demeanor.
history: Descended from ancient Stone Age 'Peat Dogs', considered the oldest dog breed in Central Europe. Developed in various sizes throughout Germany as vigilant farm watchdogs and companions, with FCI recognizing multiple size varieties sharing common ancestry.

name: German Wirehaired Pointer
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, affectionate, alert, eager to please
description: Medium to large versatile hunting dog with distinctive dense wiry double coat, bushy beard, and facial furnishings, known for endurance and weather resistance
history: Developed in late 19th-century Germany by crossing German Shorthaired Pointers, Foxhounds, Pudelpointers, and Wirehaired Pointing Griffons to create versatile, rugged, weather-resistant gundog capable of working on land and water

name: Giant Schnauzer
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, alert, energetic, courageous, confident
description: Large, powerful, and robust working dog with a dense wiry, weather-resistant coat, distinctive beard and eyebrows, and commanding, imposing presence. Originally bred for driving cattle and guarding in Germany.
history: Developed in Bavaria and Württemberg, Germany as a larger version of the Standard Schnauzer by crossing with larger breeds like the Great Dane. Originally used for driving cattle to market, guarding property, breweries and farms, and later as police and military working dogs.

name: Glen of Imaal Terrier
temperament: Courageous, gentle, loyal, intelligent, spirited
origin: Glen of Imaal, County Wicklow, Ireland
description: A strong, low-set working terrier of medium length with a sturdy, powerful build. Known for its harsh, weather-resistant double coat and courageous working temperament developed in the rugged Irish mountains.
history: Originated in the rugged Glen of Imaal valley as an all-around farm dog for hunting badgers and foxes, eradicating vermin, and historically used as turnspit dogs. Recognized by Irish Kennel Club in 1934 and AKC in 2004.

name: Goberian
temperament: Friendly, energetic, intelligent, loyal, outgoing, affectionate
description: Medium to large energetic crossbreed combining the Golden Retriever's friendly, affable nature with the Siberian Husky's striking looks and athleticism. Known for their intelligence, dense double coat, and friendly expression.
history: Modern designer crossbreed first intentionally bred in the early 2000s, combining Golden Retriever and Siberian Husky traits for an active family companion. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Goldendoodle
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, gentle, affectionate, outgoing, playful
description: Medium to large crossbreed between a Golden Retriever and Poodle, known for its friendly temperament, intelligent nature, and low-shedding wavy or curly coat. As a mixed breed, appearance and temperament can vary by generation.
history: Designer crossbreed first developed in the late 20th century, becoming popular in the 1990s. Originally created to produce a guide dog for individuals with allergies by combining the low-shedding coat of the Poodle with the gentle, trainable nature of the Golden Retriever.

name: Golden Mountain Dog
temperament: Friendly, gentle, intelligent, loyal, calm, affectionate
description: Large, friendly mixed breed combining the sociable, eager-to-please nature of the Golden Retriever with the sturdy frame, calm loyalty, and potential tri-color coat of the Bernese Mountain Dog
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in North America to combine Golden Retriever friendliness with Bernese Mountain Dog calm temperament and sturdy build, with goals of improved health and longevity through hybrid vigor

name: Golden Retriever
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, devoted, confident, loyal, eager to please
description: Large, athletic sporting dog with a dense, lustrous, flowing golden coat and friendly disposition, known for their versatility as family companions and working dogs
history: Developed in the Scottish Highlands during the mid-to-late 19th century by Dudley Marjoribanks (Lord Tweedmouth), who crossed a yellow retriever with the now-extinct Tweed Water Spaniel, with later additions of Irish Setter and Bloodhound for hunting and retrieving in rugged terrain and cold water

name: Gończy Polski
temperament: Intelligent, courageous, loyal, alert, confident
description: Medium-sized Polish scent hound with muscular, compact build and strong hunting instincts, known for its courage, intelligence, and distinctive sonorous voice
history: Ancient Polish hunting hound with documented history since the 13th century. Nearly lost after WWII but reconstructed through efforts of Colonel Józef Pawłusiewicz. Officially recognized by FCI in 2006.

name: Gordon Setter
temperament: Confident, loyal, affectionate, intelligent, eager to please, alert
description: Large, athletic bird dog with distinctive glistening black-and-tan coat. Built sturdily for work in rugged terrain, it is the heaviest and most substantial of the setter breeds, known for endurance and loyalty with an alert expression.
history: Developed in Scotland by Alexander the fourth Duke of Gordon in the late 18th and early 19th centuries from black and tan setters. Established as a durable one-man bird dog capable of working long days in rugged Scottish Highland terrain.

name: Grand Anglo-Français Blanc et Noir
temperament: Energetic, courageous, intelligent, independent, work-focused
description: Large, powerful, and elegant French scent hound with distinctive black and white coat markings, bred for pack hunting large game with exceptional stamina and fine nose
history: Developed in mid-19th century France by Comte de Chabot, crossing French scenthounds including old Saintongeois with English Foxhounds to create stamina, scenting ability, and pack hunting cooperation for large game

name: Grand Anglo-Français Blanc et Orange
temperament: Energetic, loyal, independent, courageous, work-focused
description: Large, powerful, and elegant French scenthound with distinctive white and orange coat, bred for pack hunting of large game. This athletic hunting hound combines French and Anglo heritage with strong hunting abilities and determined tracking instincts.
history: Developed in 19th century France by crossing native French scenthounds with English Foxhounds to create a powerful and fast pack hound for hunting large game like deer. Breed standard established by FCI.

name: Grand Anglo-Français Tricolore
temperament: Energetic, courageous, loyal, work-focused, independent, alert
description: Large, powerful, and elegant French scent hound with a distinctive tricolor coat, specifically bred for pack hunting large game like deer. This athletic breed combines French hound heritage with English Foxhound influences.
history: Developed in late 19th century France by crossing native French scent hounds like the Poitevin with English Foxhounds to create faster, more powerful hounds capable of hunting roe deer. Recognized by FCI as typical French scenthound for traditional pack hunting.

name: Grand Basset Griffon Vendéen
temperament: Happy, outgoing, independent, intelligent, courageous
description: Medium-sized, long-backed, short-legged French scent hound with a rough, shaggy coat, noble head, and cheerful, independent personality bred for hunting hare and rabbit over rough terrain
history: Developed in the Vendée region of France centuries ago from selective breeding of larger French scent hounds and the Grand Griffon to hunt small game over rough local terrain. The 'Grand' distinguishes it from the smaller Petit Basset Griffon Vendéen.

name: Grand Bleu de Gascogne
temperament: Gentle, calm, independent, intelligent, loyal, devoted
description: Large, noble French scenthound with distinctive blue-mottled coat and pendulous ears, known for its powerful nose, deep sonorous voice, and noble bearing. Bred for hunting large game in packs.
history: Ancient French scenthound descended from original Gallic hounds, developed in Gascony region for hunting wild boar, deer, and wolves in packs. Foundational breed for many other hound types, recognized by FCI and The Kennel Club (UK)

name: Grand Griffon Vendéen
temperament: Independent, courageous, intelligent, energetic, alert
description: Large, sturdily built French scent hound with a rough double coat, distinctive beard and mustache, and noble appearance. Known for exceptional stamina, courage, and independent nature, bred for hunting wild boar and deer in rough terrain.
history: One of the oldest French scent hounds, descended from ancient Celtic hounds and royal white hounds of France. Developed in the Vendée region during the Middle Ages for hunting large game such as wild boar, deer, and wolves with exceptional courage and stamina.

name: Great Dane
temperament: Friendly, gentle, confident, loyal, affectionate
description: Giant, regal breed known as the 'Apollo of Dogs' with a powerful, well-muscled body, sleek coat, and dignified presence combined with noble bearing despite imposing size
history: Originally bred by German nobility from Mastiff-type dogs to hunt wild boar and deer, later refined as estate guardians and companions. Declared national dog of Germany in 1876 and standardized as 'Deutsche Dogge'

name: Greater Swiss Mountain Dog
temperament: Gentle, loyal, confident, alert, friendly
description: Large, powerful, and striking tricolor working dog with distinctive tri-colored coat and gentle, dependable temperament. Known for strength and robust build as a draft dog.
history: One of Switzerland's oldest breeds, likely descended from Roman Mastiffs, used as an all-purpose draft, herding, and farm guardian dog in the Swiss Alps. Nearly extinct until rediscovered and revived in early 1900s by Dr. Albert Heim.

name: Greek Harehound
temperament: Energetic, independent, alert, courageous, intelligent, loyal
description: Medium-sized, athletic scenthound with a short, dense black and tan coat, bred for stamina and endurance when hunting hare in rugged Greek terrain. Known for its keen sense of smell and tenacious tracking ability.
history: Ancient breed native to Greece believed descended from hounds brought by Phoenicians. Developed as hardy tenacious scenthound for hunting hare in rugged rocky terrain. Officially recognized by FCI in 1996 as national breed of Greece.

name: Greek Shepherd
temperament: Protective, independent, loyal, courageous, calm
description: Large, powerful livestock guardian dog native to the mountainous regions of Greece, known for its thick double coat, independent nature, and strong protective instincts developed over centuries of guarding flocks
history: An ancient landrace breed developed over centuries in the mountainous regions of Greece by shepherds to protect flocks from predators. Documented by the FCI under breed standard No. 246 as 'Greek Shepherd Dog'

name: Greenland Dog
temperament: Independent, energetic, courageous, loyal, athletic, confident
description: A powerful, heavy-built spitz-type dog of ancient origin with thick double coat, bred for endurance as a sled dog in harsh Arctic conditions. One of the world's oldest dog breeds with erect ears and a tail that curls over the back.
history: One of the world's oldest dog breeds, descended from dogs brought to Greenland by the Thule people over 1,000 years ago. Kept in purest form by the Inuit as essential partner for sled pulling and hunting in harsh Arctic environment.

name: Greyhound
temperament: Gentle, calm, intelligent, affectionate, independent
description: An ancient sighthound with tall, lean, elegant build designed for exceptional speed. Known for their gentle temperament and often described as a '45-mph couch potato', combining incredible athleticism with calm, affectionate nature.
history: Ancient sighthound breed dating back thousands of years, depicted in Egyptian, Greek, and Roman art. The modern Greyhound was standardized in Great Britain for coursing game and later racing. Famed as the fastest dog breed.

name: Griffon Bleu de Gascogne
temperament: Alert, energetic, independent, courageous, devoted, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, rustic-looking French scenthound with distinctive blue-mottled, rough coat and strong hunting instincts. Known for exceptional stamina and a melodious voice, this persistent and versatile hunter combines keen scenting ability with rugged durability.
history: Ancient French hound developed in the Gascogne region by crossing Grand Bleu de Gascogne with rough-coated griffon breeds to create a robust, keen-nosed hunting hound suited for rough terrain. Prized for exceptional nose and stamina.

name: Griffon Bruxellois
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, confident
description: A small, intelligent toy dog from Belgium with a distinctive bearded, almost human-like expressive face and alert demeanor. Available in rough and smooth coat varieties, known for their big personality despite their small size.
history: Developed in Brussels as stable dogs and ratters from local rough-coated terriers in the 1800s, refined by crossing with Pug and English Toy Spaniel, became popular with Belgian nobility and coachmen

name: Griffon Fauve de Bretagne
temperament: Courageous, independent, intelligent, energetic, affectionate
description: Medium-sized rough-coated French scent hound with harsh, wiry fawn to red coat and keen hunting instincts, known for tenacity in the hunt and rustic appearance
