history: Ancient French scenthound dating back to the 13th century from the Brittany region, originally used to hunt wolves and wild boar. Nearly extinct after WWII but revived by dedicated enthusiasts using remaining bloodlines and crosses with other French hounds

name: Griffon Nivernais
temperament: Independent, courageous, energetic, alert, work-focused
description: Medium-sized French scent hound with a robust build, rough shaggy coat, and rugged appearance. Known for exceptional hunting ability in difficult terrain and strong work-focused instincts.
history: Ancient French scent hound from the Nivernais region, originally used by medieval French nobility for hunting wolf and wild boar. Nearly extinct after World War II and the French Revolution, but revived in the 19th century through careful breeding using Grand Griffon Vendéen, Otterhound, and local stock.

name: Gull Dong
temperament: Protective, courageous, loyal, independent, intelligent
description: A large, powerful, and muscular mastiff-type dog from Pakistan, known for its formidable presence and strong protective instincts. Developed as a cross between the Gull Terrier and the Bully Kutta, this breed features a short, smooth coat and agile build designed for guarding and protection work.
history: Developed in the 19th century in the Punjab region of Pakistan by crossing the Gull Terrier with the Bully Kutta, bred specifically for guarding, personal protection, and historically for dog fighting. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Gull Terrier
temperament: Loyal, courageous, intelligent, protective, independent, energetic, alert
description: An athletic, muscular, medium-sized landrace breed from Pakistan with a smooth coat and strong build. Known for its strength, tenacity, guarding instincts, and alert demeanor. Often predominantly white in color with strong predatory instincts.
history: Developed in the Punjab region of Pakistan and India during the British Raj by crossing imported English Bull Terriers with local bully-type landrace dogs. Traditionally used for hunting, guarding, animal baiting, and rural protection. Maintains strong working abilities from its historical background.

name: Halden Hound
temperament: Gentle, alert, intelligent, confident, energetic
description: Medium-sized, rectangular Norwegian scenthound with distinctive short, dense coat, built for speed and endurance as a hare hunter in challenging terrain. Features a proud yet gentle expression with the stamina and resilience needed for Norwegian hunting conditions.
history: Developed in Halden, Norway in the late 19th and early 20th centuries by crossing local scenthounds with English, Swedish, and German hounds to create a swift, resilient hare hunter suited for Norwegian terrain. Recognized by FCI.

name: Hällefors Elkhound
temperament: Courageous, alert, independent, intelligent, loyal
description: Medium-sized spitz-type hunting dog developed in Sweden for elk and large game hunting, featuring a dense double coat and rectangular build designed for persistence and courage in dense forests
history: Developed in the Hällefors region of Sweden during the 20th century for moose hunting. Created by crossing Finnish Spitz with Ostiak Laika in the 1930s to produce a sturdy, single-minded hound for baying at moose in dense forests. Recognized by the Swedish Kennel Club.

name: Hamiltonstövare
temperament: Friendly, energetic, intelligent, alert, independent
description: Medium-sized Swedish tricolor scent hound with athletic build and noble appearance, bred for endurance and skill in hunting fox and hare over challenging terrain
history: Developed in the late 19th century by Count Adolf Patrick Hamilton using English Foxhounds, Harriers, and German hounds including Holsteiner Hounds to create a dedicated Swedish hunting breed

name: Hanoverian Scenthound
temperament: Calm, loyal, determined, independent
description: Robust medium-large scenthound known for its exceptional tracking ability and calm, determined temperament. Descended from medieval Liam hounds, this breed was specifically developed to track wounded game for hunters.
history: Descended from medieval Liam hounds in Germany; developed to track wounded game for hunters. Recognized by the FCI (Standard No. 213).

name: Harrier
temperament: Friendly, outgoing, energetic, intelligent, gentle
description: Medium-sized athletic scent hound bred for pack hunting hare, resembling a smaller English Foxhound with sturdy build, short dense coat, and excellent stamina
history: Developed in medieval England for hare hunting in packs, documented since at least the 13th century. Likely descended from English Foxhound, Talbot Hounds, and other French hounds

name: Havanese
temperament: Outgoing, intelligent, friendly, playful, affectionate, adaptable
description: Small, sturdy companion dog with long, silky double coat and cheerful temperament. Cuba's national dog, known for its intelligent disposition and making an ideal city companion.
history: Developed from Bichon-type ancestors brought by Spanish settlers, became companions to Cuban aristocracy. Nearly extinct after Cuban Revolution but preserved by Cuban families who fled to the United States.

name: Havapoo
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, playful, affectionate, outgoing
description: Small designer crossbreed between Havanese and Toy Poodle, known for its intelligent, affectionate personality and soft, low-shedding coat that ranges from wavy to curly
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States in recent decades, combining Cuban Havanese with Toy Poodle to create a small companion dog with intelligence, low-shedding coat, and gentle disposition. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Himalayan Sheepdog
temperament: Protective, loyal, independent, intelligent, alert, courageous
origin: Himalayan region of India and Nepal
description: An ancient and powerful livestock guardian breed from the Himalayan region with formidable size, thick double coat, and fiercely protective instincts. Also known as the Gaddi Kutta, Bhote Kukur, or Himalayan Mastiff, this sturdy mountain dog was developed for herding and guarding livestock in harsh Himalayan conditions.
history: An ancient landrace developed by nomadic shepherds, particularly the Gaddi tribe, in the Himalayan regions of India and Nepal. Bred to be a versatile and resilient guardian capable of protecting flocks from large predators like leopards and wolves at high altitudes.

name: Hmong Bobtail Dog
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, alert, protective, affectionate
description: Medium-sized spitz-type dog with characteristic naturally short tail and thick double coat, developed by Hmong people as versatile working companion
history: Ancient indigenous breed of Vietnam's Hmong people, traditionally developed for hunting, guarding, and companionship over many generations. Recognized by the Vietnam Kennel Association, not by FCI or AKC.

name: Hokkaido
temperament: Alert, loyal, courageous, intelligent, independent, devoted
description: Medium-sized, athletic spitz-type hunting dog with thick double coat, prick ears, curled tail, and alert expression. Originally bred by the Ainu people for hunting large game in harsh climates of Hokkaido, Japan.
history: Ancient Japanese breed developed by the indigenous Ainu people of Hokkaido for hunting large game such as bears and deer. Designated as living natural monument in Japan in 1937.

name: Hovawart
temperament: Loyal, protective, intelligent, confident, devoted, alert
description: Large, powerful working dog with a long, dense, slightly wavy coat and strong protective instincts, originally bred as a farmyard guardian but known for its balanced temperament as both devoted family companion and vigilant watchdog
history: Ancient German working breed meaning 'yard' or 'farm watcher', nearly extinct after WWI but reconstructed in the 1920s by selectively breeding dogs with similar characteristics found on farms in the Black Forest region to restore the original type

name: Huntaway
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, work-focused, loyal, adaptable
description: Large, sturdily-built herding dog from New Zealand, known for its deep, powerful bark used to drive sheep over large distances using voice and physical presence
history: Developed in late 19th and early 20th century New Zealand by farmers who crossed various British sheepdogs, collies, and herding breeds to create a dog that could move large flocks of sheep over vast distances using its powerful voice and stamina

name: Huskimo
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, playful, friendly, alert, loyal
description: Medium to large mixed breed combining Siberian Husky and American Eskimo Dog traits, featuring a wolf-like appearance with Spitz-type characteristics, thick double coat, and energetic, intelligent temperament
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed by crossing Siberian Husky with American Eskimo Dog, aiming to create a Husky-like appearance in a more adaptable package while combining working heritage with companion traits. Not recognized by major kennel clubs

name: Hygen Hound
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, independent, alert, affectionate, loyal
description: Medium-sized, strong and compact Norwegian scenthound with lean athletic build, developed for endurance in rough terrain and known for excellent tracking abilities and affectionate nature with family
history: Developed in the 19th century by Norwegian breeder Hans Fredrik Hygen, created by crossing German Holsteiner hounds with local Norwegian scent hounds and other Scandinavian breeds like the Småland Hound to produce a resilient hound for hunting in challenging Arctic terrain. Recognized by the FCI as a Norwegian scent hound.

name: Ibizan Hound
temperament: Independent, intelligent, athletic, gentle, alert, playful
description: Elegant sighthound with distinctive large erect ears, lean athletic build, and deer-like grace. Known for remarkable jumping ability and bred for hunting rabbit by sight, this ancient breed comes in both smooth and wire-coated varieties.
history: Ancient breed whose origins trace back to Phoenician traders who brought Egyptian hunting dogs to the Balearic Islands around the 8th century BC. Developed in isolation on Ibiza and neighboring islands for hunting rabbits on rocky terrain.

name: Icelandic Sheepdog
temperament: Friendly, alert, energetic, intelligent, playful, confident, hardy
description: Medium-sized spitz-type herding dog with thick weatherproof double coat, prick ears, and curved bushy tail, known for its cheerful and inquisitive nature
history: Iceland's only native dog breed, brought by Viking settlers around 874-930 AD and bred in isolation for centuries. Essential for herding sheep and livestock in harsh Icelandic conditions

name: Indian Pariah Dog
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, adaptable, independent
description: Medium-sized primitive landrace breed native to the Indian subcontinent with erect ears, short coat, and naturally hardy constitution developed through thousands of years of natural selection
history: Ancient aboriginal landrace breed native to the Indian subcontinent, among the world's oldest and most natural dog breeds. Developed as a free-ranging village dog over thousands of years, adapted to local climate and conditions through natural selection.

name: Indian Spitz
temperament: Alert, intelligent, playful, loyal, friendly, adaptable
description: Small to medium-sized spitz-type companion dog with pointed ears, curled tail, and dense white or cream double coat, developed in India from German Spitz ancestors and adapted for warm Indian climate conditions
history: Descended from German Spitz dogs introduced to India by the British, developed and promoted by the Kennel Club of India through selective breeding for smaller size and adaptation to Indian climate conditions, becoming a popular family companion breed in the late 20th century

name: Irish Doodle
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, energetic, playful, affectionate, outgoing
description: Medium to large crossbreed combining the friendly, outgoing nature and athleticism of the Irish Setter with the intelligence and low-shedding, wavy or curly coat of the Standard Poodle
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed primarily in the United States in the 1990s and 2000s by crossing Irish Setter with Standard Poodle, aiming to combine the energetic sporting nature and temperament of the Setter with the hypoallergenic coat qualities and intelligence of the Poodle

name: Irish Red and White Setter
temperament: Friendly, energetic, intelligent, courageous, devoted, gentle, eager to please
description: Medium to large athletic gundog with distinctive red and white coat pattern and aristocratic appearance. This versatile sporting breed combines a powerful, athletic build with a kind expression and was bred specifically for pointing and retrieving game birds.
history: One of Ireland's oldest gundogs with history dating back to the 17th century, predating the Irish Setter. Bred as versatile gundogs for finding and retrieving gamebirds. Nearly extinct by the 1920s but revived through dedicated breeding programs by enthusiasts in the 20th century.

name: Irish Setter
temperament: Friendly, outgoing, energetic, intelligent, playful, affectionate
description: Elegant, athletic gundog known for its stunning mahogany or rich chestnut red flowing coat and high-energy, friendly temperament
history: Developed in 18th-century Ireland as a versatile gundog for setting and retrieving game birds by crossing Irish Water Spaniel, English Setter, and Pointer. The breed's striking mahogany color became a hallmark in the 19th century, distinguishing it from earlier red-and-white setters

name: Irish Terrier
temperament: Courageous, loyal, intelligent, energetic, spirited
description: Medium-sized terrier with a distinctive wiry, fiery red coat and bold, spirited temperament. Known for its courage and loyalty, it is one of the oldest terrier breeds.
history: One of the oldest terrier breeds, developed in County Cork, Ireland as all-purpose farm dogs for hunting vermin and guarding homes. Their courage was proven in World War I where they served as messenger dogs and sentinels.

name: Irish Water Spaniel
temperament: Intelligent, playful, alert, courageous, eager to please
description: The tallest of all spaniels, this large, versatile sporting dog is distinguished by its crisp, curly purplish-brown waterproof coat, distinctive smooth tapered 'rat tail', and characteristic topknot. Bred for retrieving waterfowl from cold Irish waters, combining intelligence with a playful, alert nature.
history: Ancient Irish breed with modern development in the 1830s primarily by Justin McCarthy. Bred for retrieving waterfowl from cold Irish waters, mentioned in Irish literature as early as 1600s and recognized by AKC in 1884

name: Irish Wolfhound
temperament: Gentle, calm, dignified, courageous, loyal
description: A giant-sized sighthound of imposing appearance and dignified nature, the Irish Wolfhound is the tallest of all dog breeds. Known for its wiry, rough coat and gentle giant temperament, this ancient breed combines courage with a calm, dignified demeanor.
history: Ancient breed from Ireland originally used in war and for hunting wolves and Irish elk. Nearly extinct by the 1860s but revived by Captain George Augustus Graham who standardized the breed.

name: Istrian Coarse-haired Hound
temperament: Alert, energetic, gentle, loyal, independent, intelligent
description: Medium-sized, noble-looking scent hound with distinctive harsh, wiry white coat and orange markings, bred for hunting in the mountainous terrain of Istria. Known for its calm, gentle demeanor and tenacious hunting ability with a deep, resonant voice.
history: Ancient breed from the Istrian peninsula with depictions dating back to the 15th century. Developed for centuries to hunt fox, hare, and wild boar in mountainous terrain, prized for its weather-resistant coat and endurance. Formally standardized by FCI in the mid-20th century.

name: Istrian Shorthaired Hound
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, independent, gentle, loyal
description: Medium-sized, noble-looking scent hound of ancient lineage with a smooth white coat and distinctive orange markings, bred for hunting hare and fox with a strong, sonorous voice
history: Ancient breed from the Istrian peninsula with depictions dating back to 1497. Developed as resilient scenthound for hunting hare and fox, breed standard established in 1924 and FCI recognized in 1949. Considered one of oldest hound breeds in Balkan region.

name: Italian Greyhound
temperament: Affectionate, gentle, playful, alert, intelligent
description: Elegant small sighthound with slender, delicate build and graceful movement, combining the look of a large hound with the size of a lap dog and fine, smooth coat
history: Ancient breed with origins tracing back over 2,000 years to the Mediterranean basin, favored by nobility in ancient Greece, Rome, and Italian Renaissance, AKC recognized since 1886

name: Jack Russell Terrier
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, alert, courageous, confident, playful
description: Small, energetic earth-working terrier with a predominantly white coat and sturdy build, known for its bold, athletic nature and lively temperament, originally bred for fox hunting
history: Developed in southern England during the mid-1800s by Reverend John Russell for fox hunting, bred to bolt foxes from dens. The shorter-legged variety standard was primarily developed in Australia.

name: Jagdterrier
temperament: Courageous, intelligent, energetic, work-focused, independent
description: Small, compact German hunting terrier with wiry black and tan coat, bred for versatility, tenacity, and intense working drive in the field
history: Developed in Germany after World War I by hunters who wanted to create a breed solely for hunting performance. Selectively bred from Fox Terrier lines and various terrier breeds to create a versatile, tenacious, and courageous hunting dog.

name: Jämthund
temperament: Courageous, intelligent, calm, independent, alert, loyal, devoted
description: Large, athletic spitz-type hunting dog from Sweden with wolf-like appearance and strong build, known for its endurance and courageous nature in hunting large game under harsh Nordic conditions
history: Developed over centuries in Jämtland province of Sweden as versatile hunter of large game like moose and bear, officially recognized as distinct breed in 1946 by Swedish Kennel Club and FCI

name: Japanese Chin
temperament: Alert, intelligent, affectionate, independent, playful, calm
description: Small, elegant toy breed with distinctive flat face, large wide-set eyes, and luxurious silky coat. Known for their aristocratic bearing and unique cat-like demeanor, often cleaning their faces and perching in high places.
history: Ancient breed believed to have originated in the Chinese imperial court, then refined in Japan by nobility as cherished lap dogs and companions. Often given as gifts to diplomats and royalty, including to Commodore Matthew Perry who brought them to the West in 1853.

name: Japanese Spitz
temperament: Intelligent, playful, alert, loyal, affectionate, friendly
description: Small to medium-sized spitz-type dog with profuse, pure-white double coat, fox-like face with pointed muzzle, and plumed tail carried over its back. This lively companion dog has an alert, friendly disposition.
history: Developed in Japan in the 1920s-1930s from various spitz breeds including large white German Spitz dogs brought to Japan around 1920. The breed was developed through selective breeding for friendly temperament and white coat, first exhibited in Tokyo in 1921. After World War II, the breed standard was unified by the Japan Kennel Club.

name: Japanese Terrier
temperament: Lively, alert, intelligent, playful, affectionate
description: Small, compact terrier with distinctive short, smooth coat and lively, alert disposition. Characterized by its clean-cut, smart appearance and cheerful character, this breed combines typical terrier alertness with a friendly companion nature.
history: Descended from Smooth Fox Terriers brought to Japan by Dutch traders in the 17th century, crossed with native Japanese dogs. Refined as companions in port cities like Kobe and Yokohama with planned breeding beginning around 1920 and official recognition by Japan Kennel Club in 1930.

name: Jeju Dog
temperament: Alert, loyal, intelligent, courageous, independent, energetic
description: Medium-sized spitz-type landrace breed from Jeju Island, South Korea, with erect ears, curled tail, and keen alert expression. Known for brindle coat, strong hunting instincts, and courageous nature. Historically used for hunting badgers and roe deer.
history: Ancient landrace native to Jeju Island, traditionally used for hunting and guarding. Nearly became extinct during Japanese colonial period and Korean War. Restoration project began in 1986, designated Natural Monument of South Korea (No. 540) in 2010. Very rare outside homeland, not recognized by major international kennel clubs.

name: Jindo
temperament: Loyal, independent, intelligent, alert, courageous, protective
description: Medium-sized spitz-type hunting dog native to Jindo Island in South Korea with erect ears, curled tail, and strong hunting instincts, prized for loyalty, intelligence, and bravery
history: Ancient breed that developed naturally on Jindo Island over 1500 years ago, prized for hunting abilities, courage, and fierce loyalty. Designated as Natural Treasure #53 by Korean government in 1962 to preserve breed purity.

name: Jonangi
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, loyal, alert, independent
description: Medium-sized agile Indian spitz-type dog with short smooth coat, webbed feet, and pricked ears. Known for its unique yodel-like vocalization and traditionally used for duck herding, hunting, and fishing activities.
history: Ancient landrace from coastal Andhra Pradesh traditionally used by fishermen and shepherding communities for duck herding, hunting small game, and fishing activities. Faced near-extinction but recent preservation efforts are underway.

name: Kaikadi
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, loyal, independent, courageous
description: Small to medium-sized sighthound from Maharashtra, India, with a slender, athletic build and short, fine coat. Named after the Kaikadi nomadic tribe, this ancient landrace breed is known for its agility and exceptional hunting abilities.
history: Ancient landrace sighthound from the Maharashtra region of India, traditionally used by the Kaikadi nomadic tribe for hunting small game, vermin, and as companion dogs. Considered one of the aboriginal hound breeds of India and recognized by the Indian National Kennel Club.

name: Kai Ken
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, courageous, independent, athletic
description: Medium-sized Japanese spitz-type breed with distinctive brindle coat and athletic build, renowned for agility and loyalty as a skilled mountain hunter
history: Ancient Japanese breed from the mountainous Kai region where brindle coat provided camouflage. Used for hunting wild boar and deer, designated as a national monument in 1934 to preserve breed purity. One of six native Japanese spitz breeds.

name: Kamchatka Sled Dog
temperament: Hardy, independent, work-focused, loyal, intelligent, energetic
description: An ancient, powerful landrace sled dog from Russia's Kamchatka Peninsula with a thick double coat and strong, athletic build. This large spitz-type breed was developed for endurance and work in harsh Arctic conditions, possessing a primitive appearance and resilient nature.
history: Ancient aboriginal landrace sled dog developed over centuries by indigenous peoples including the Itelmen and Koryak peoples of Kamchatka Peninsula for transportation and hauling in severe Arctic conditions. A foundational type for many modern sled breeds valued for stamina, resilience, and work ethic.

name: Kangal Shepherd Dog
temperament: Loyal, protective, independent, calm, intelligent, alert
description: Large, powerful, and heavy-boned livestock guardian dog with distinctive fawn coat, black mask and ears, and curled tail, bred for protecting flocks from predators
history: Ancient livestock guardian breed originating from the Kangal district in the Sivas province of Turkey. For centuries used by shepherds to protect flocks from wolves, bears, and jackals. Considered a national treasure in Turkey and valued for strength, loyalty, and independence.

name: Kanni
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, independent, alert, athletic, reserved
description: Rare, elegant sighthound landrace from Tamil Nadu, India, with a lean, athletic build and short, fine coat typically in black-and-tan or cream. Known for incredible speed, loyalty, and independent nature, traditionally prized for hunting.
history: The Kanni, meaning 'pure' in Tamil, is an ancient sighthound landrace from Tamil Nadu, traditionally prized by royalty, zamindars, and tribal communities for hunting deer, wild boar, hare, and other small game. Recognized by Indian government and kennel clubs, though not by major international kennel clubs.

name: Karakachan
temperament: Independent, protective, loyal, alert, courageous, intelligent
description: Large, powerful livestock guardian dog with dense double coat, bred by nomadic shepherds to protect flocks in mountainous terrain of the Balkans. Known for its formidable presence and unwavering loyalty to its flock and family.
history: One of Europe's oldest breeds descended from dogs of the Thracians, developed and preserved by the Karakachans nomadic shepherds of the Balkans for livestock protection. Nearly extinct but being preserved through breeding programs and recognized by FCI

name: Karelian Bear Dog
temperament: Courageous, independent, loyal, alert, protective
description: Medium-sized, sturdily-built spitz-type hunting dog with distinctive black and white coat, bred for courage and tenacity in hunting large game including bears, moose, and wild boar
history: Ancient breed developed in the Karelia region of Finland and Russia from spitz-type dogs to hunt large game. Standardized in the 1930s and nearly became extinct after World War II but was saved by dedicated Finnish breeders. One of Finland's most prized national breeds

name: Karelo-Finnish Laika
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, courageous, alert, independent
description: Medium-sized, fox-like spitz-type hunting dog with a vibrant red-gold double coat, erect ears, and curled tail. Known for its intelligence, courage, and distinctive 'bark-pointing' hunting style used for tracking game birds and small mammals.
history: Ancient spitz breed developed in the Karelia region from native spitz-type hunting dogs for hunting game birds, particularly capercaillie and black grouse. Standardized in late 19th century and recognized as Finland's national dog. In 2006, FCI merged the similar Russian Karelo-Finnish Laika into the Finnish Spitz breed standard.

name: Kars
temperament: Protective, independent, courageous, loyal
description: Large, rugged livestock guardian dog with dense weather-resistant coat and protective nature
history: An ancient Turkish livestock guardian from the Kars region, developed to protect flocks from predators in harsh climates; closely related to other regional shepherd breeds. Recognized by Turkish registries and FCI Group 2 as a regional variant.

name: Karst Shepherd
temperament: Protective, loyal, courageous, confident, calm, independent
description: A robust, medium-large livestock guardian dog with a thick iron-grey double coat and calm, self-confident disposition. Ancient breed from the Karst Plateau region developed for centuries to guard livestock from predators including wolves and bears.
history: Ancient livestock guardian breed native to the Karst Massif region of Slovenia, used for centuries to guard livestock from wolves and bears. Originally recognized by FCI in 1939 as the Illyrian Shepherd Dog, renamed to Karst Shepherd in 1968.

name: Kazakh Tazy
temperament: Independent, intelligent, alert, loyal, calm, confident
description: Ancient Central Asian sighthound with elegant, long-legged build and characteristic feathering on ears, tail, and legs. Known for exceptional speed, stamina, and endurance, developed for hunting in harsh steppe conditions.
history: Ancient sighthound developed by nomadic Kazakh tribes for hunting game such as fox, hare, badger, and wolf across vast steppes. Name 'Tazy' means 'pure' or 'noble' reflecting esteemed status. Provisionally recognized by FCI in 2024, preservation efforts ongoing.

name: Keeshond
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, outgoing, alert, eager to please
description: Medium-sized compact spitz with plush silver-gray double coat, fox-like face, distinctive spectacles markings around the eyes, and plumed tail curled over back
history: Ancient Dutch breed known as the Dutch Barge Dog, popular companion and watchdog on barges and riverboats along the Rhine River. Became symbol of Dutch Patriots Party in 18th century, nearly extinct after WWII but restored from dogs exported to England and America

name: Kerry Beagle
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, loyal, independent, friendly, alert
description: Medium-large Irish scent hound with a smooth, dense coat and distinctive black and tan markings, one of Ireland's oldest native breeds known for stamina and strong hunting instincts
history: Ancient Irish scenthound and one of Ireland's oldest native breeds, possibly descending from Celtic Hounds and dating back to at least the 16th century. Developed in County Kerry for hunting stag, deer, fox, and hare in packs over mountainous terrain. Despite its name, it is a medium-large hound, not a small beagle. Recognized by the Irish Kennel Club.

name: Kerry Blue Terrier
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, playful, energetic, courageous
description: Medium-sized, muscular Irish terrier with distinctive soft, wavy blue-gray coat and bearded face, known for its spirited, intelligent personality
history: Originating in County Kerry, Ireland, developed as a versatile all-purpose farm dog for herding cattle, hunting vermin and small game, and guarding. Became a symbol of Irish patriotism with breed records dating to the 19th century; recognized by the AKC in 1922.

name: Khala
temperament: Loyal, independent, intelligent, alert, affectionate
description: A rare, primitive hairless landrace breed from Bolivia existing in two size varieties (Medio and Grande), with a distinctive sighthound-like build, hairless body, and tufts of hair on feet and tail. Also has a coated variety.
history: A primitive landrace breed from the highlands of Bolivia, believed to have existed for centuries. Traditionally used as a companion and sight-hunter, closely related to other South American hairless breeds like the Peruvian Inca Orchid and Xoloitzcuintli. Recognized by FCI as Bolivian Hairless Dog (Khala).

name: King Charles Spaniel
temperament: Gentle, affectionate, intelligent, friendly, happy, playful
description: Small, compact toy spaniel with distinctive domed head, flat face, short nose, large dark eyes, long ears, and silky coat available in four color varieties (Blenheim, Tricolour, Ruby, and Black and Tan). Known as the English Toy Spaniel in the USA.
history: Descended from toy spaniels popular with European nobility since the 16th century, famously associated with King Charles II who favored companion spaniels. Modern flat-faced appearance developed through crossings with short-nosed Asian toy breeds in the 18th-19th centuries. The four color varieties were once considered separate breeds.

name: King Shepherd
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, confident, protective, gentle, courageous
description: Large, robust shepherd-type dog developed as an improved German Shepherd with better health and temperament. Resembles a giant, heavy-boned German Shepherd with a plush or coarse double coat and calmer, more stable demeanor intended as a family companion and guardian.
history: Developed in the 1990s by American breeders Shelley Watts-Cross and David Turkheimer. Created by crossing American German Shepherds with Shiloh Shepherds and later infusing Great Pyrenees and Alaskan Malamute to create a larger, healthier, more robust shepherd with improved temperament. Recognized by the American King Shepherd Club and United Kennel Club.

name: Kintamani
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, independent, protective, devoted
description: Medium-sized spitz-type dog native to Bali, Indonesia, known for its thick double coat, erect ears, plumed curled tail, and alert, intelligent disposition. This indigenous breed serves as both village guardian and companion dog.
history: Indigenous national breed of Indonesia that originated in the Kintamani region of Bali. Evolved from local free-roaming dogs and is genetically distinct. Traditionally used as village guardian and companion dog. Officially recognized by the FCI in 2019.

name: Kishu Ken
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, alert, courageous, independent, reserved
description: A medium-sized, well-balanced spitz-type hunting dog of ancient Japanese lineage, known for its nobility, endurance, and dignified temperament. Features erect ears, curled tail, and typically solid white coat, though sesame and red are also found.
history: Ancient Japanese hunting breed from the mountainous Kishu region of the Kii Peninsula, prized for hunting wild boar and deer. Designated as a Natural Monument of Japan in 1934 to preserve its purity and recognized by the FCI in the Spitz and Primitive Types group.

name: Kokoni
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, alert, playful, affectionate, loyal
description: Small ancient Greek companion dog with a lively and cheerful disposition, characterized by a slightly elongated body, fluffy coat, plumed tail carried in a curve, and intelligent, alert expression
history: Ancient Greek breed with depictions found on artifacts dating back thousands of years. Developed from small landrace dogs throughout the region, served as companions and household watchdogs. Formally recognized by Greek Kennel Club in 2004-2005 and in AKC Foundation Stock Service.

name: Kombai
temperament: Courageous, loyal, protective, intelligent, alert, independent
description: Medium-sized, powerful and athletic Indian hunting dog from Southern India with a distinctive tan coat and muscular build, known for its courage in hunting large game like wild boar, bison, and deer
history: Ancient Indian breed from Tamil Nadu, believed to have been used by the armies of the Marudhu brothers in the 18th century. Nearly extinct but preservation efforts ongoing, recognized by the Indian Kennel Club.

name: Komondor
temperament: Loyal, protective, independent, courageous, calm
description: Large, muscular Hungarian livestock guardian dog with distinctive heavy coat of white cords resembling a mop. Built for endurance and protection, this imposing breed possesses a dignified, courageous demeanor.
history: Ancient breed descended from Tibetan dogs brought to Hungary by nomadic Magyar tribes over a thousand years ago around 1000 AD. Developed to be formidable independent livestock guardian protecting sheep and cattle from wolves bears and harsh weather.

name: Kooikerhondje
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, alert, energetic, cheerful, adaptable
description: Small, agile spaniel-type dog of Dutch origin with a distinctive flowing orange-red and white particolored coat and feathered ears, originally bred for luring ducks into traps
history: Ancient Dutch breed developed as early as the 16th century to work as duck decoy dogs, luring ducks into traps called 'eendenkooien'. Nearly extinct after World War II but saved and re-established by Baroness van Hardenbroek van Ammerstol from remaining bloodlines. Recognized by AKC in 2018.

name: Koolie
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, loyal, work-focused, alert, devoted
description: Medium-sized versatile Australian working dog known for herding ability, intelligence, high energy levels, and often striking merle coat. Athletic and agile with strong stamina, developed specifically for Australian livestock work.
history: Developed in 19th century Australia from imported British working dogs including smooth collies and other herding breeds, possibly with German herding dog influence. Bred for endurance, versatility and stamina to work livestock in harsh Australian landscape. Not recognized by major kennel clubs but has established breed standards by Koolie breed societies.

name: Kromfohrländer
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, loyal, playful, adaptable, alert
description: Medium-sized German companion dog with distinctive white coat and brown markings, available in both rough-coated (tousled) and smooth-coated varieties, known for its happy disposition and lively temperament
history: Developed in Germany after World War II by Ilse Schleifenbaum from a stray terrier-type dog (Original Peter) found by American soldiers, believed to be crossed with Wire Fox Terriers and Grand Griffon Vendéen. Officially recognized by the FCI in 1955 and named after the local landscape 'krom Fohr' (crooked furrow)

name: Kuchi
temperament: Protective, independent, loyal, courageous, intelligent
description: Large, rugged landrace livestock guardian dog from the mountainous regions of Afghanistan with powerful build, thick double coat, and protective instincts. Developed by nomadic Kuchi people for guarding livestock and caravans against large predators like wolves and snow leopards.
history: Ancient landrace breed developed over centuries by the nomadic Kuchi people of Afghanistan for guarding livestock and caravans. Bred to be hardy and formidable, capable of surviving harsh mountain climates and defending against large predators.

name: Kunming Dog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, courageous, alert, protective, work-focused
description: Medium to large, wolf-like working dog from China resembling a German Shepherd, developed specifically for military and police duties with strong protective instincts and high intelligence
history: Developed in the 1950s in Kunming, China, by crossing German Shepherd Dogs with local Chinese dogs and wolfdogs. Bred specifically as a military working dog for the People's Liberation Army, suited for high-altitude conditions, and officially recognized in China in 1988. Not recognized by major international kennel clubs.

name: Kurdish Mastiff
temperament: Protective, loyal, independent, courageous, alert, confident
description: Large, powerful, and ancient landrace livestock guardian dog originating from the Kurdistan region, known for its protective instincts, independent nature, and robust build. Also known as the Pshdar Dog, this breed was developed for strength, courage, and resilience in harsh mountainous conditions.
history: An ancient landrace breed developed over centuries by Kurdish shepherds in the mountainous Kurdistan region primarily to protect livestock from large predators like wolves and bears. Valued for strength, courage, and resilience in harsh conditions, but not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Kuvasz
temperament: Loyal, protective, independent, courageous, intelligent, calm
description: Large, powerful white Hungarian livestock guardian dog with a dense double coat, noble bearing, and fiercely loyal, protective nature
history: Ancient Hungarian breed descended from large molosser-type dogs from Tibet. Historically served as royal guard dogs for Magyar kings and fierce livestock guardians, prized for their loyalty and courage in protecting flocks and estates from predators and thieves

name: Labradoodle
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, energetic, gentle, outgoing, affectionate
description: Energetic medium-to-large crossbreed between Labrador Retriever and Poodle with curly or wavy coat, originally developed as a hypoallergenic companion and guide dog. Size and coat type can vary significantly based on generation and Poodle parent size.
history: First intentionally developed in Australia in the late 1980s by Wally Conron for the Royal Guide Dog Association of Australia. Created by crossing Labrador Retrievers with Poodles to produce a hypoallergenic guide dog combining the low-shedding coat of the Poodle with the temperament and trainability of the Labrador Retriever. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Labrador Retriever
temperament: Friendly, outgoing, energetic, intelligent, gentle, eager to please
description: Medium to large, strong athletic retriever with short dense coat and friendly outgoing temperament. Consistently one of the most popular breeds worldwide.
history: Originated from St. John's Water Dogs used by fishermen in Newfoundland for retrieving nets and escaped fish. Further developed and standardized in England during the 1800s by English nobles for waterfowl retrieving

name: Labraheeler
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, alert, friendly, work-focused
description: Medium to large athletic crossbreed combining the loyal, hard-working nature of the Australian Cattle Dog with the friendly, outgoing temperament of the Labrador Retriever. This intelligent and energetic mixed breed features a short double coat and strong work ethic.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in recent decades, primarily in North America and Australia. Created to combine the intelligence, work ethic, and sturdiness of the Australian Cattle Dog with the friendly and trainable nature of the Labrador Retriever.

name: Labsky
temperament: Friendly, energetic, intelligent, loyal, outgoing, playful
description: Medium to large energetic crossbreed combining Labrador Retriever and Siberian Husky traits, known for its athletic build, striking appearance, and friendly yet independent nature. Physical appearance and temperament can vary significantly between individuals.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed by combining Labrador Retriever and Siberian Husky, aiming to blend the friendly, trainable nature of the Labrador with the striking looks, athleticism and stamina of the Husky. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Lacy Dog
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, work-focused, devoted, loyal, alert
description: Medium-sized athletic working dog developed in the American Southwest for herding and hunting with a short, sleek coat and intense drive. Known for its high energy and strong work focus, this breed can be blue, red, or tricolor.
history: Developed by the Lacy family in Burnet County, Texas, in the mid-1800s as an all-purpose working dog for herding, trailing, and hunting, using English Shepherd, Greyhound, scent hound, and possibly coyote stock. Officially designated as the State Dog of Texas in 2005.

name: Lagotto Romagnolo
temperament: Affectionate, intelligent, energetic, loyal, alert, work-focused
description: Medium-sized rustic water retriever with dense, curly, waterproof coat and exceptional sense of smell, specialized as the world's premier truffle-hunting dog
history: Ancient breed from the Romagna region of Italy, originally used as a water retriever in the marshlands. When the marshes were drained, the breed was adapted and became the world's premier truffle-hunting dog.

name: Lài
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, athletic, alert, protective
description: Medium-sized, primitive spitz-type dog native to northern Vietnam; agile, hardy, and versatile
history: An ancient indigenous breed of Vietnam's northern mountains, the Lài dog (sometimes called Chó Lài or Vietnamese Dingo) developed as a versatile hunting, herding, and guard dog. It is widely considered one of Vietnam's native primitive spitz breeds, known for intelligence, adaptability, and resilience. Not recognized by major international kennel clubs but documented in local standards.

name: Laizhou Hong
temperament: Loyal, protective, confident, intelligent, alert
description: Large, powerful Molosser-type working dog from Shandong, China, with distinctive reddish-brown coat and black mask, developed for guarding and protection
history: Modern Chinese breed developed in the 1950s-1970s in the Laizhou region of Shandong province by crossing German Shepherds, Great Danes, Rottweilers, and local Chinese breeds to produce a versatile working and guard dog

name: Lakeland Terrier
temperament: Confident, alert, courageous, intelligent, friendly, energetic
description: A small, sturdy terrier from the Lake District of England with a dense, weather-resistant wiry coat and lively expression. Known for its confident 'big dog' demeanor in a compact body and bold, courageous temperament.
history: Developed in the rugged Lake District by farmers to hunt foxes that preyed on lambs and sheep. Ancestry includes the old English Black and Tan Terrier and Bedlington Terrier. Recognized by The Kennel Club in 1921 and AKC in 1934.

name: Lancashire Heeler
temperament: Intelligent, alert, energetic, friendly, playful, courageous
description: Small, sturdy herding dog with short legs and a low-set build. Features a short black and tan weatherproof double coat and distinctive foxlike expression with alert nature.
history: Developed in Lancashire, England as an all-purpose farm dog for herding cattle by nipping at heels and for ratting. Believed to be developed from crosses between Welsh Corgis and Manchester Terriers. Nearly extinct by the 1960s but saved through dedicated breeding programs. Recognized by The Kennel Club (UK) in 1981 and AKC in 2024.

name: Landseer
temperament: Gentle, calm, intelligent, loyal, devoted, protective
description: Large, powerful, and agile dog of the molossoid type, distinguished by its striking white coat with black patches. Known for exceptional swimming ability and gentle nature, the Landseer (European Continental Type) is taller and more athletic than its Newfoundland cousin, bred specifically for water rescue work.
history: Named after 19th-century painter Sir Edwin Landseer who frequently depicted these dogs in his work. Developed as a distinct breed in Germany and Switzerland from Newfoundland dogs to preserve the taller, more agile structure. Recognized as separate breed by FCI in 1960.

name: Langqing
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, independent, athletic, energetic
description: A rare, athletic sighthound landrace from China, known for its lean build, fine coat, and exceptional hunting ability. Also known as the Guizhou Hound.
history: An ancient and rare sighthound landrace originating from the Guizhou province of China. Traditionally used by the Miao people for hunting, its history is long but not formally documented by major kennel clubs. It is valued for its speed, stamina, and hunting prowess.

name: Lapponian Herder
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, loyal, work-focused, calm, friendly
description: Medium-sized, spitz-type herding dog with a strong but agile build, slightly longer than tall, developed for managing reindeer in arctic conditions with a dense double coat and alert expression
history: Developed by the indigenous Sami people in Finland's Lapland region for herding and managing reindeer. The breed was formally standardized in the mid-20th century, bred from local spitz-type dogs and possibly German Shepherd crosses in the 1960s.

name: Large Münsterländer
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, eager to please, friendly
description: Versatile medium to large German pointing and retrieving dog with distinctive black and white coat and feathering, known for its noble appearance and athletic build
history: Originated in the Münster region of Germany in the early 1900s, developed from the black and white offspring of German Longhaired Pointers and continental pointers and spaniels. When the German Longhaired Pointer standard was set to only allow liver and white, dedicated breeders preserved and developed the black and white variety into today's versatile hunting dog.

name: Leonberger
temperament: Gentle, calm, loyal, friendly, intelligent, confident
description: Giant, majestic working dog with a lion-like appearance, thick water-resistant double coat, and gentle, balanced temperament. This powerful yet serene breed combines an imposing presence with a calm, patient demeanor.
history: Developed in the 1840s by Heinrich Essig in Leonberg, Germany, crossing Newfoundlands, Saint Bernards, and Pyrenean Mountain Dogs to create a 'lion-like' dog resembling the lion on the town crest. Became popular companion for European royalty

name: Levriero Sardo
temperament: Independent, alert, loyal, intelligent, reserved
description: Rare, ancient sighthound landrace from Sardinia, Italy, known for its lean, athletic build and speed. This slender breed has strong hunting instincts and a reserved, independent temperament, traditionally used for hunting hares and small game across rugged terrain.
history: The Levriero Sardo, or Sardinian Greyhound, is an ancient landrace sighthound native to the island of Sardinia. Believed to have descended from sighthounds brought to the region by ancient civilizations such as the Phoenicians, it has been prized by local hunters for centuries for its speed and stamina. The breed is rare and not recognized by major international kennel clubs but is documented in regional breed registries and is the focus of preservation efforts by local enthusiasts.

name: Lhasa Apso
temperament: Confident, independent, alert, loyal, smart, protective
description: Small, hardy, and sturdy ancient Tibetan breed known for its floor-length coat of hair that parts down the back and alert, confident expression, originally bred as palace and monastery sentinel
history: Ancient breed developed in Tibetan monasteries and palaces over a thousand years ago as indoor sentinel dogs, alerting monks to intruders. Highly prized and rarely left the country until brought to England in 1920s

name: Lhasapoo
temperament: Intelligent, affectionate, playful, alert, confident
description: Small designer crossbreed between Lhasa Apso and Poodle, combining the intelligence and low-shedding coat of the Poodle with the loyal, alert nature of the Lhasa Apso. Features a fluffy, wavy or curly coat and affectionate, friendly temperament.
history: Developed in the United States during the designer dog trend of the late 20th century by crossing the Lhasa Apso and Poodle. The goal was to create a small, intelligent companion dog with a low-shedding coat. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Liangshan Dog
temperament: Loyal, courageous, independent, intelligent, protective, alert
origin: Liangshan Yi Autonomous Prefecture, Sichuan Province, China
description: A primitive, large native Chinese breed from the mountainous Liangshan region of Sichuan, known for its lean athleticism, protective instincts, and versatility as both a hunting and livestock guardian dog with exceptional courage and endurance
history: An ancient regional landrace breed developed over centuries by the Yi people in the Liangshan mountains of Sichuan. Traditionally used both for hunting boar, rabbits, and mountain game, as well as protecting livestock from predators. Developed in isolation and recognized regionally in China but not by major international kennel clubs.

name: Lithuanian Hound
temperament: Energetic, loyal, intelligent, courageous, alert, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, strong and tenacious scenthound with a short, glossy black and tan coat, developed for hunting in Lithuanian forests and known for endurance and strong hunting instincts
history: The Lithuanian Hound has been known since the 16th century and is the only recognized dog breed indigenous to Lithuania. Nearly extinct after World War II, it was revived through dedicated breeding efforts starting in 1957 with the first official standard published in 1986. Developed by crossing local hunting dogs with Polish and Russian hounds for forest hunting.

name: Lobito Herreño
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, alert, independent, protective
description: A primitive, wolf-like medium-sized herding dog from the island of El Hierro, known for its agility, loyalty, and reserved nature. Also known as Pastor Herreño, this rustic breed was traditionally used for livestock management in volcanic terrain.
history: A primitive landrace dog native to El Hierro island, descended from ancient dogs brought by pre-Hispanic settlers. Traditionally used for herding goats and sheep in volcanic terrain. Formally recognized by the Real Sociedad Canina de España (RSCE) in 2013 after recovery efforts saved it from near extinction.

name: Löwchen
temperament: Affectionate, outgoing, playful, intelligent, alert
description: Small companion dog known as the 'Little Lion Dog' with a long, flowing coat traditionally clipped into a distinctive lion trim featuring a plumed tail and mane. This bright, lively toy breed combines an affectionate nature with alert intelligence.
history: Ancient breed with documented history in pre-Renaissance Europe, depicted in historical artworks for centuries and cherished by European nobility. Nearly extinct after WWII but saved through dedicated breeding efforts by Madame Bennert and other breeders. Once known as the rarest dog in the world before numbers recovered.

name: Lucas Terrier
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, alert, affectionate, adaptable
description: Small, sturdy working terrier with a dense, shaggy wiry coat, specifically bred for a friendly, non-quarrelsome temperament while maintaining robust health and working ability
history: Developed in the late 1940s by Sir Jocelyn Lucas crossing Norfolk Terriers and Sealyham Terriers to create a small, sturdy working terrier with a more companionable temperament and fewer health problems

name: Lupo Italiano
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, alert, independent, work-focused
description: Medium to large Italian working dog developed from wolf-dog crosses, resembling a wolf in appearance with a sturdy, athletic build and erect ears. Known for its wolf-like features, endurance, and suitability for specialized work such as search and rescue.
history: Created in Italy in 1966 by Dr. Mario Messi through crossing a wild she-wolf from the Northern Apennines with German Shepherds. Developed under Italian government control for specialized utility roles including search and rescue, anti-poaching, and civil protection work.

name: Lurcher
temperament: Intelligent, gentle, loyal, affectionate, athletic, independent
description: Athletic, lean sighthound crossbreed combining speed, agility, and stamina, traditionally used for hunting and coursing. Known for versatility as a cross between sighthounds and pastoral or terrier breeds.
history: Traditional British and Irish crossbreed combining sighthounds (primarily Greyhounds) with terriers, pastoral, or herding breeds. Developed by traveling communities and poachers who needed fast, silent, and efficient hunting dogs for coursing and poaching.

name: Mackenzie River Husky
temperament: Independent, hardy, work-focused, loyal, intelligent, alert
origin: Mackenzie River Region, Northwest Territories, Canada
description: Large, powerful landrace of freighting sled dog developed in northern Canada, known for exceptional endurance, strength, and cold weather adaptation in sub-arctic regions
history: Developed by indigenous peoples and fur traders in the Mackenzie River region as a functional landrace for freight hauling across vast northern territories. Created by crossing native sled dogs with large European breeds in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Magyar Agár
temperament: Loyal, calm, gentle, intelligent, athletic, independent
description: Ancient Hungarian sighthound with elegant, robust build and short coat, bred for speed, stamina, and open-field coursing game across the Hungarian plains
history: Ancient Hungarian sighthound whose origins trace back to the Magyar conquest of the Carpathian Basin, developed for coursing hare and deer with stamina over long distances and rugged terrain, nearly extinct after World War II but revived by dedicated breeders

name: Mahratta Hound
temperament: Independent, loyal, intelligent, alert, athletic
description: A rare, medium-sized sighthound from the Maharashtra region of India, known for its lean build, speed, stamina, and keen eyesight. Closely related to the Caravan Hound, this ancient breed is prized for its hunting prowess.
history: An ancient sighthound breed originating from the Deccan Plateau in the state of Maharashtra, India. Traditionally used by local nobility and villagers for hunting hare, deer, and other small game. Maintained in rural communities as a rare landrace breed with no formal international breed standard.

name: Majorca Shepherd Dog
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, protective, courageous, devoted
description: Large, powerful, and noble black-coated shepherd dog with short coat, naturally adapted to Mediterranean climate. Known as Ca de Bestiar in its native land, this rustic livestock guardian and herder combines strength and agility with protective instincts.
history: An ancient breed from the Balearic Islands, used for centuries as a shepherd and livestock guardian. Developed for herding and protecting livestock in Mediterranean conditions. Officially recognized with breed standard in 1980 as 'Ca de Bestiar'.

name: Maltese
temperament: Playful, gentle, affectionate, charming, fearless, alert, adaptable
description: An ancient toy breed from the Mediterranean with a long, silky white coat and charming, playful personality combined with a fearless nature despite their diminutive size
history: One of the world's oldest known breeds dating back over 2000 years, originating from the island of Malta. Historically known as 'Ye Ancient Dogge of Malta' and prized as companions to royalty and aristocracy throughout Mediterranean civilizations and European nobility

name: Maltipoo
temperament: Affectionate, friendly, intelligent, playful, gentle
description: Small affectionate crossbreed between a Maltese and a Poodle, known for its soft, wavy or curly, low-shedding coat and playful companion nature
history: Popular designer crossbreed developed in the United States in the late 20th century, gaining popularity in the 1990s by crossing Maltese with Toy or Miniature Poodle to create a small, low-shedding companion dog

name: Maltshi
temperament: Affectionate, playful, friendly, gentle, outgoing, intelligent
description: Small designer crossbreed resulting from crossing a Maltese and Shih Tzu, known for their affectionate nature, low-shedding silky coat, and charming companion temperament
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States in the late 20th century by crossing the Maltese and Shih Tzu breeds. The goal was to create a small, affectionate companion dog with a low-shedding coat. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Manchester Terrier
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, energetic, spirited, independent
description: Sleek, elegant terrier with distinctive black-and-tan coat and keen, alert expression. Known as the 'Gentleman's Terrier' in Victorian England, this racy, athletic breed exists in both Standard and Toy varieties with a clean-cut, spirited appearance.
history: Developed in 19th-century Manchester by crossing Black and Tan Terriers with Whippets. Created as proficient rat-killer and rabbit-courser for working men of the industrial era. Recognized by AKC since 1886.

name: Maneto
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, alert, loyal, affectionate, devoted, independent
description: A small, short-legged (achondroplastic) sighthound from Andalusia, Spain, specifically developed from the Podenco Andaluz for hunting rabbits in dense undergrowth. Known for agility in hunting small game with a low-set build and smooth coat.
history: The Maneto is a variety of the Podenco Andaluz, characterized by its short legs (achondroplasia). This ancient Spanish breed was selectively bred in the provinces of Málaga and Cádiz to create a dog adept at navigating dense scrub and brambles to hunt rabbits and partridges. It was officially recognized by the Real Sociedad Canina de España (RSCE) in 2004, with FCI provisional recognition.

name: Maremma Sheepdog
temperament: Protective, independent, loyal, alert, calm
description: Large, well-balanced livestock guardian with thick white coat and confident demeanour. Also known as 'Maremmano-Abruzzese Sheepdog'
history: Ancient livestock guardian from central Italy, developed to protect sheep from predators in the regions of Maremma and Abruzzo. Recognized by FCI as Cane da Pastore Maremmano-Abruzzese.

name: Markiesje
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, calm, alert, affectionate, energetic
description: Small, elegant black spaniel-type companion dog from the Netherlands, characterized by its silky, medium-length coat and intelligent, calm expression. This attentive and lively breed has a friendly temperament and appeared in Dutch paintings from the 17th and 18th centuries as a companion to noblewomen.
history: Ancient Dutch breed appearing in paintings from the 17th and 18th centuries as companion to noblewomen. Nearly extinct by 1970s but revived through dedicated breeding program starting in 1980s. Officially recognized by Dutch Kennel Club in 1999 and provisionally recognized by FCI.

name: Mastidoodle
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, loyal, protective, calm
description: Large crossbreed combining the gentle giant nature of the Mastiff with the intelligence and low-shedding coat of the Standard Poodle
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed to combine the gentle temperament of Mastiffs with the intelligence and reduced shedding of Poodles

name: McNab
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, alert, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, agile herding dog with athletic build and exceptional working ability, known for intelligence, stamina, and versatility. Developed for ranch work in Northern California
history: Developed in the late 1800s by Scottish immigrant Alexander McNab on his Mendocino County ranch, crossing Scottish Collies and local working collies with other herding dogs to create a versatile ranch dog suited to California terrain

name: Miniature American Shepherd
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, devoted, loyal, eager to please, work-focused
description: Small, athletic herding dog with a striking double coat and keen intelligence, essentially a compact version of the Australian Shepherd with intense work drive and devotion
history: Developed in California during the late 1960s from small Australian Shepherds, bred for compact size while maintaining herding ability. Became popular with equestrians traveling to horse shows and was recognized by the AKC Herding Group in 2015.

name: Miniature Bull Terrier
temperament: Playful, energetic, courageous, loyal, mischievous, outgoing
description: Small, muscular terrier with distinctive egg-shaped head and spirited, mischievous personality. A strongly built breed known for its playful and comical nature.
history: Developed in 19th-century England as a smaller version of the Bull Terrier by selecting for smaller size while maintaining distinctive characteristics. Initially used for ratting and vermin control, evolved into companion dogs prized for courage and comical nature. Recognized by AKC in 1991.

name: Miniature Fox Terrier
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, confident, loyal
description: Small, agile, and smooth-coated terrier known for its spirited, alert nature and compact build. Developed in Australia as a versatile vermin hunter and lively companion with an intelligent, courageous temperament.
history: Developed in Australia during the late 19th and early 20th centuries from crosses of English Fox Terriers and other small terriers brought by settlers, refined as a versatile vermin hunter and spirited companion for farm work

name: Miniature Pinscher
temperament: Energetic, alert, confident, intelligent, courageous, fearless
description: Small, compact, and elegant toy breed with a distinctive high-stepping 'hackney' gait and spirited personality. Despite resembling a miniature Doberman, this is an older, distinct German breed with a proud carriage and 'big dog' attitude in a small package.
history: An old German breed (Zwergpinscher) developed centuries ago to hunt rats in homes and stables. Not a miniaturized Doberman Pinscher, but a distinct older breed likely descended from German Pinschers with contributions from Italian Greyhounds and Dachshunds.

name: Miniature Schnauzer
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, alert, outgoing, energetic
description: Small, sturdy terrier-type dog with distinctive wiry coat, bushy eyebrows, and characteristic beard, known for its alert, friendly nature and square build
history: Developed in late 19th century Germany by crossing Standard Schnauzers with smaller breeds like Affenpinschers and Poodles to create an all-purpose farm dog and efficient ratter for vermin control and companionship

name: Mojee
temperament: Affectionate, playful, intelligent, gentle
description: Small companion crossbreed with uncertain parentage, possibly involving Japanese Chin crossed with either Maltese or Poodle. Not recognized by major kennel clubs, this modern designer breed typically displays a cheerful and affectionate temperament.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the early 21st century, though exact parentage and origins are uncertain. Not recognized by major authoritative kennel clubs.

name: Molossus of Epirus
temperament: Protective, loyal, independent, calm, courageous, confident
description: Large, powerful, and imposing livestock guardian dog from the Epirus region of Greece, descended from ancient Molossian dogs. Characterized by its robust build, dense double coat, and calm yet protective demeanor with strong guardian instincts.
history: Ancient livestock guardian breed from the mountainous Epirus region of Greece, descended from the Molossian dogs of antiquity and developed over centuries to guard livestock from predators like wolves and bears. Officially recognized by the Kennel Club of Greece in 2006.

name: Mongrel
temperament: Variable depending on ancestry and individual traits
description: Mixed breed dog of unknown or varied ancestry, combining traits from multiple breeds. Physical characteristics, temperament, and health are highly variable and unpredictable, though often display hybrid vigor.
history: Mixed breed dogs resulting from generations of natural or unintentional breeding between dogs of various ancestries. They have existed as long as domesticated dogs, predating modern purebred concepts, and are found worldwide though not recognized by kennel clubs.

name: Montenegrin Mountain Hound
temperament: Alert, loyal, energetic, docile, intelligent, devoted
description: Medium-sized, agile scenthound from the Balkans with a distinctive black and tan coat and robust build. Known for its docility, dedication to hunting, and keen tracking ability in mountainous terrain.
history: Ancient breed native to Montenegro's mountainous regions, developed for hunting hare, fox, and other small game. Formerly known as the 'black hound', it shares origins with other Balkan scenthounds and was recognized by the FCI with breed standard established in 1924. Prized by local hunters for stamina and strong sense of smell.

name: Morkie
temperament: Affectionate, playful, alert, energetic, intelligent
description: Small mixed breed companion dog combining the gentle nature of the Maltese with the spirited personality of the Yorkshire Terrier. Known for its long, silky, low-shedding coat and toy-sized stature.
history: Modern designer crossbreed that became popular in the late 1990s and early 2000s in North America. Developed by crossing purebred Maltese with Yorkshire Terrier to create a small, low-shedding companion dog. Not recognized by major kennel clubs but popular with designer breed enthusiasts.

name: Moscow Watchdog
temperament: Protective, confident, loyal, alert, intelligent, independent
description: Large, powerfully built guardian breed developed in Soviet Russia with a pronounced guarding instinct, combining Saint Bernard and Caucasian Shepherd traits. Known for its balanced temperament, self-confidence, and devotion to family while maintaining strong protective instincts.
history: Developed in the 1950s at Soviet 'Red Star' military kennels in Moscow post-WWII by crossing Saint Bernard, Caucasian Shepherd Dog, and Russian Spotted Hound. Goal was to create ideal military guard dog adapted to harsh Russian climate.

name: Mountain Cur
temperament: Courageous, intelligent, loyal, energetic, protective
origin: Southern United States (Appalachian and Ozark Mountains)
description: Medium-sized rugged treeing hound and farm dog with strong, muscular build, bred as a pioneer's all-purpose hunting companion known for courage, athleticism, and excellent treeing abilities in mountainous terrain
history: Developed by early settlers in the rugged Southern Mountains from European cur-type dogs, serving as invaluable all-purpose pioneer dogs for treeing game, farm work, and homestead protection. Formal breed registry established in 1956

name: Mountain Feist
temperament: Energetic, alert, intelligent, courageous, loyal
origin: Southern United States (Appalachian and Ozark Mountains)
description: Small to medium-sized hunting dog with compact, muscular build and short coat, developed in the southern United States for treeing small game. Known for exceptional agility, alertness, and tenacious hunting ability.
history: Developed by pioneers and settlers in the rural mountainous regions of the southern United States for hunting small game. Descended from various terriers and hunting dogs brought by European immigrants, bred for tenacity, intelligence, and treeing ability.

name: Mudhol Hound
temperament: Loyal, independent, intelligent, alert, courageous
description: Ancient, elegant Indian sighthound with lean, athletic build and narrow head, known for remarkable speed, stamina, and loyalty. Also known as the Caravan Hound.
history: Ancient Indian sighthound developed by rulers of Mudhol State for hunting in Deccan plateau region. Also known as Caravan Hound, prized for hunting prowess and guarding abilities. Recognized by Kennel Club of India and provisionally by FCI, recently inducted into Indian Army.

name: Mudi
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, alert, loyal, adaptable, work-focused
description: Medium-sized, versatile Hungarian herding dog with a wavy to curly coat, known for its intelligence, agility, and energetic nature. This all-purpose farm dog excels in various dog sports and herding activities.
history: Developed in Hungary during the 19th and early 20th centuries as a versatile all-purpose herding and farm dog, likely from spontaneous crosses of Puli, Pumi, and German Spitz-type dogs. Nearly extinct after WWII but preserved by dedicated breeders. Officially recognized by FCI and AKC.

name: Neapolitan Mastiff
temperament: Protective, loyal, calm, confident, affectionate
description: Massive and powerful ancient guardian breed with distinctive heavily wrinkled face, loose skin, and imposing presence. Despite its formidable appearance, it is a steady and loyal companion with protective instincts.
history: Ancient Italian guardian descended from Roman molossus war dogs. The modern breed was refined in the Naples region where it served as estate and farm guardian, and was revitalized in the mid-20th century as a distinct breed.

name: Nenets Herding Laika
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, work-focused, independent, alert, loyal, hardy
description: Medium-sized spitz-type herding dog from the Siberian Arctic with erect ears, thick weather-resistant double coat, and agile build. Developed by the indigenous Nenets people to manage reindeer herds in harsh tundra conditions, possessing enduring stamina and an independent, work-focused nature.
history: An ancient landrace breed developed by the indigenous Nenets people of the Yamal Peninsula in Siberia. For centuries, it has been an essential partner for herding, guarding, and transporting reindeer in harsh Arctic conditions. The breed is an ancestor to the modern Samoyed and was fundamental in the development of several modern northern breeds.

name: Newfoundland
temperament: Gentle, loyal, devoted, calm, intelligent
description: Massive, powerful working dog known for exceptional water rescue abilities and gentle temperament. Often called the 'gentle giant,' this breed features a thick, water-resistant double coat, webbed feet, and natural swimming ability.
history: Developed by fishermen on the island of Newfoundland for water rescue, hauling nets, and cart pulling. Descended from indigenous water dogs and European breeds brought by fishermen, recognized by major kennel clubs in the 19th century.

name: Newfypoo
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, loyal, friendly, affectionate, calm
description: Large mixed breed cross between Newfoundland and Standard Poodle, combining the gentle, patient nature of the Newfoundland with Poodle intelligence and typically low-shedding coat qualities
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed over the past few decades in North America by crossing the giant Newfoundland with the intelligent Standard Poodle, aiming to combine the loving, gentle temperament and water rescue abilities of the Newfoundland with the low-shedding coat and high trainability of the Poodle

name: New Guinea Singing Dog
temperament: Independent, alert, intelligent, energetic, cautious
description: Primitive medium-small wild dog known for distinctive howling vocalizations, fox-like appearance, and exceptional agility. Closely related to the dingo, this rare breed retains wild characteristics and is considered semi-domesticated.
history: Ancient primitive breed native to New Guinea highlands and forests, believed to be one of the oldest dog breeds with limited human domestication. Very rare and considered wild or semi-domesticated, not recognized by major kennel clubs but noted for distinctive melodious vocalizations.

name: New Zealand Heading Dog
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, work-focused, loyal, alert
description: Medium-sized, agile working dog renowned for its intelligence and strong herding instinct, specifically developed for controlling sheep with keen eyes, quick movement, and exceptional responsiveness in New Zealand's rugged terrain
history: Developed in New Zealand during the late 19th and early 20th centuries from imported Border Collies and other British herding breeds, selectively bred for 'heading' ability using strong eye and silent, agile movements to cast out and bring sheep towards the shepherd

name: Norfolk Terrier
temperament: Alert, courageous, affectionate, energetic, playful
description: Small, sturdy, and fearless terrier with a wiry, weather-resistant coat and characteristic drop ears. Known for its spirited, affectionate nature and bred for ratting and companionship.
history: Developed in Norfolk and East Anglia, England in the 1880s-early 20th century as farm dogs to control vermin and hunt foxes. Originally classified as the drop-eared variety of the Norwich Terrier, separated as distinct breed in 1964 by The Kennel Club (UK) and 1979 by the AKC based on ear carriage.

name: Norrbottenspets
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, courageous, confident, independent
description: Small, compact spitz-type hunting dog from northern Sweden with erect ears, curled tail, and white coat with colored patches. Known for its alert, agile, and fearless nature with a well-muscled body built for athleticism.
history: Ancient spitz breed from northern Sweden developed as versatile farm and hunting dog for small game from grouse to moose. Nearly extinct after WWII but revived by dedicated breeders and enthusiasts in the 1960s.

name: Northern Inuit Dog
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, loyal, independent, gentle, alert
description: Large, athletic, wolf-like dog developed in the UK to resemble wolves while maintaining a domestic temperament suitable for companionship. Known for intelligence and loyalty but also independent nature.
history: Developed in the UK from the late 1980s by Eddie Harrison (Edwina Harrison) crossing German Shepherds, Siberian Huskies, Alaskan Malamutes, and selected Inuit-type dogs to create a companion dog with wolf-like appearance. Not recognized by major international kennel clubs but governed by the Northern Inuit Society.

name: Norwegian Buhund
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, friendly, confident, alert, loyal, playful
description: An ancient Nordic spitz-type herding dog with a compact, square build, dense double coat, curled tail, and alert expression. Known for their cheerful disposition and intelligence, this energetic breed combines sturdy construction with an agile, confident demeanor.
history: An ancient Nordic spitz-type breed from Norway with roots tracing back to the Viking age, where their skeletons have been found in Viking graves. They served as versatile farm dogs, primarily for herding sheep and cattle, and as loyal watchdogs and companions.

name: Norwegian Elkhound
temperament: Friendly, confident, energetic, loyal, independent
description: Medium-sized spitz-type hunting dog with thick double coat and curled tail, bred for hunting elk and moose. Bold and energetic, this hardy gray hunting dog is known for its versatility, courage, and stamina in tracking large game.
history: Ancient Nordic spitz-type breed dating back 6000 years, companion of Vikings used for hunting elk bear and moose in Scandinavian forests. Officially recognized by AKC in 1913.

name: Norwegian Lundehund
temperament: Alert, energetic, loyal, intelligent
description: A small, agile spitz-type breed from Norway with unique anatomical features including six functional toes per foot and extraordinary flexibility, specifically adapted for hunting puffins on steep, rocky cliffs
history: An ancient Norwegian spitz breed from the remote Lofoten Islands, developed specifically for hunting puffins on steep, rocky cliffs. Its unique anatomical traits allowed it to navigate treacherous terrain and access bird nests. Nearly extinct after World War II but saved through dedicated breeding programs and now recognized by the AKC and FCI.

name: Norwich Terrier
temperament: Alert, energetic, affectionate, courageous, outgoing
description: Small, sturdy, and compact terrier with distinctive prick ears, wiry double coat, and lively disposition. Known for its hardy constitution and fearless but affectionate nature.
history: Developed in East Anglia, England in the 1880s as a rugged earth-dog for ratting and fox bolting. Originally grouped with Norfolk Terrier until official separation in 1964 based on ear carriage (Norwich has prick ears). Recognized by AKC in 1979.

name: Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, playful, outgoing, alert, affectionate
description: The smallest of the retrievers, this medium-sized, athletic sporting dog is known for its distinctive reddish coat with white markings and enthusiastic personality. Originally bred for luring and retrieving waterfowl by playing along the shoreline.
