history: Developed in early 19th century Nova Scotia, specifically in the Little River Harbour area of Yarmouth County, to 'toll' (lure) ducks within gunshot range by playing along the shoreline, then retrieve shot birds. Recognized by AKC in 2003.

name: Nureongi
temperament: Intelligent, alert, loyal, independent
description: Medium-sized Korean landrace dog with characteristic yellowish coat, not a formally standardized breed but a distinct genetic group with spitz-type features including erect ears and curled tail
history: Traditional Korean landrace dog meaning 'yellow one' with long presence in rural Korea. Historically served as multipurpose farm dog, livestock guardian, and watchdog. Not formally recognized by major kennel clubs but confirmed as distinct landrace group through genetic studies. Currently facing conservation efforts

name: Old Danish Pointer
temperament: Calm, gentle, intelligent, loyal, confident, courageous
description: Medium to large, strongly-built pointer of noble appearance with distinctive white coat and brown markings, bred for endurance hunting in Danish terrain and known for its calm, stable temperament
history: Ancient Danish hunting breed also known as Gammel Dansk Hønsehund, dating back to around 1710. Developed by crossing local Danish farm dogs with Spanish Pointers and scenthounds to create a versatile, steady gundog with endurance for varied terrain. Nearly extinct after WWII but preserved by dedicated breeders.

name: Olde English Bulldogge
temperament: Confident, courageous, alert, friendly, loyal
description: Athletic and muscular, medium-sized bulldog breed developed to recreate the healthier, more athletic Regency-era English Bulldog with improved health, stable temperament, and well-balanced features without exaggeration
history: Developed in the 1970s by David Leavitt to recreate the healthier, more athletic bulldog of the 18th and 19th centuries (Regency Period) using crosses of English Bulldog, American Bulldog, American Pit Bull Terrier, and Bullmastiff. Recognized by UKC but not AKC

name: Old English Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, gentle, playful, adaptable, affectionate
description: Large, athletic herding dog of great strength with distinctive profuse, shaggy double coat in gray and white, famous for its peek-a-boo hairdo and characteristic bear-like gait
history: Developed in western England in the late 18th century likely from breeds like Bearded Collie and Russian Owtchar. Bred as drovers' dogs to drive cattle and sheep to market. Tails were historically docked to identify them as working dogs exempting them from taxes. Recognized by AKC in 1888

name: Otterhound
temperament: Friendly, gentle, independent, energetic, amiable
description: Large, shaggy-coated scent hound with webbed feet and waterproof double coat, bred for hunting otters in medieval England. Known for its boisterous and amiable temperament, keen nose, and distinctive love of water.
history: Developed in medieval England for otter hunting, perfected for water work with webbed feet, oily double coat, and powerful scenting ability. Nearly extinct when otter hunting was banned in 1978, now among the rarest British breeds.

name: Pachón Navarro
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, calm, work-focused, docile, alert, affectionate
description: Medium to large-sized, robust Spanish pointing dog with distinctive split or 'double' nose, renowned for its hunting abilities as a versatile and athletic worker. This resilient and tireless breed is a prized hunting companion with excellent scenting ability.
history: Ancient Spanish pointing dog with origins tracing back to the Middle Ages in the Navarre region. Famed for its scenting ability and distinctive double nose, it was a prized hunting companion for centuries. The breed faced near extinction in the 20th century but was revived through dedicated conservation efforts and breeding programs. Recognized by the Real Sociedad Canina de España and provisionally by the FCI.

name: Pampas Deerhound
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, energetic, courageous, devoted
description: Large athletic sighthound developed for hunting deer in the South American grasslands. Also known as Veadeiro Pampeano, this slender but strong scenthound is known for its keen hunting ability, endurance, and resistance.
history: Developed in South America from Iberian hounds and adapted over centuries for hunting pampas deer and large game in the harsh Pampas grasslands. Shares lineage with European sighthounds and recognized by FCI as Galgo Barbucho. The breed was nearly extinct but has been revived through dedicated breeder efforts.

name: Papillon
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, alert, energetic, happy
description: Small, fine-boned, and elegant toy spaniel with distinctive butterfly-like ears (papillon is French for 'butterfly') and silky flowing coat, known for their alert, friendly, and energetic nature
history: Descended from continental toy spaniels that were popular in European courts for centuries, appearing in paintings by masters like Titian, Goya, and Rubens. The breed was refined in France and Belgium, with the original drop-eared variety (Phalène) later joined by the erect-eared 'butterfly' version (Papillon)

name: Papipoo
temperament: Intelligent, alert, playful, affectionate, friendly
description: Small designer crossbreed combining the Papillon's distinctive butterfly ears and alertness with the Toy Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding qualities, featuring a coat that ranges from straight and silky to wavy or curly
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in recent decades by intentionally crossing Papillon and Toy Poodle to create a small, intelligent companion dog with a low-shedding, potentially hypoallergenic coat. Not recognized by major kennel clubs but seen in designer breed registries.

name: Parson Russell Terrier
temperament: Alert, confident, energetic, intelligent, athletic, courageous
description: Athletic, energetic white terrier with a square build and weather-resistant coat, bred for fox hunting with exceptional stamina and endurance. Known for their bold yet friendly nature and ready-for-anything attitude.
history: Developed in the mid-1800s by Reverend John 'The Sporting Parson' Russell in southern England. Created to be an ideal earth-working terrier that could keep up with foxhounds and bolt, but not kill, fox during hunts. Standardized and recognized by the AKC in 1997 as distinct from the Jack Russell Terrier.

name: Pastore della Lessinia e del Lagorai
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, alert, work-focused
origin: Lessinia and Lagorai regions, Triveneto, Northern Italy
description: A medium-sized, rustic sheepdog with a wolfish appearance and agile build, characterized by its endurance and semi-long, often merle coat. Developed for herding livestock in the mountainous regions of Northeast Italy.
history: An ancient sheepdog breed from northeastern Italy, used for centuries by shepherds in the Lessinia and Lagorai mountain regions for herding and transhumance. Nearly extinct, the breed was formally recognized through recovery efforts starting in the 1990s, with FCI granting provisional recognition in 2018.

name: Pastor Garafiano
temperament: Docile, intelligent, loyal, alert, work-focused
description: A medium to large, lupoid-type herding dog from the Canary Islands with a robust build, long abundant double coat, and strong working instincts. Known for its docile character and suitability as both a herding dog and family companion.
history: An autochthonous breed from La Palma island in the Canary Islands, developed from pre-Hispanic dogs and used traditionally for herding goats and sheep in mountainous terrain. Officially recognized by the Real Sociedad Canina de España and provisionally by the FCI (Standard No. 321)

name: Patagonian Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, work-focused, protective, energetic, independent
description: A medium-to-large, hardy herding dog developed in the harsh climate of Patagonia, known for its dense, weather-resistant double coat, strong work ethic, and unwavering loyalty to its handlers
history: Developed in the late 19th and early 20th centuries from various European sheepdogs brought by settlers to the Patagonian regions of Chile and Argentina. Through natural selection in the harsh environment, it evolved into a distinct landrace uniquely suited for herding sheep in the region's challenging climate. Not recognized by major kennel clubs but established in Patagonian ranching communities

name: Patterdale Terrier
temperament: Energetic, courageous, confident, independent, alert, work-focused
description: Small, compact, and tough working terrier developed in the Lake District of England for hunting and dispatching vermin. Known for its strong build, endurance, and confident, work-focused nature with smooth or broken coat.
history: Developed in the Lake District of Northern England in the early 20th century as a tough, game working terrier for fox hunting and vermin control in rocky fells. Bred for stamina, courage, and ability to work in tight burrows. Not currently recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Peekapoo
temperament: Affectionate, intelligent, alert, playful
description: Small companion mixed breed with soft curled or wavy coat combining the Pekingese and Toy Poodle
history: Peekapoo originated in the United States in the 1950s as a deliberate cross between a Pekingese and Toy Poodle, valued for companionship and hypoallergenic potential. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Pekeapoo
temperament: Affectionate, intelligent, alert, playful, loyal
description: Small designer crossbreed combining Pekingese and Poodle traits, creating an intelligent and affectionate companion dog with a compact size and low-shedding coat
history: Designer crossbreed developed in the United States in the 1950s-1960s as one of the earliest intentional hybrids, crossing Pekingese with Toy or Miniature Poodle to create a small companion dog with reduced shedding and playful temperament

name: Pekingese
temperament: Loyal, independent, confident, courageous, affectionate, alert
description: Small, compact toy dog of Chinese nobility with a long, profuse double coat, distinctive flat-faced expression, rolling gait, and regal bearing originally bred for Chinese royalty
history: Ancient breed developed in Chinese imperial court and held sacred in the Imperial Palace in Peking (now Beijing). Exclusively owned by royalty for over 1000 years until Western dogs were brought to England after British troops entered the Forbidden City in 1860 during the Opium Wars.

name: Pembroke Welsh Corgi
temperament: Intelligent, affectionate, alert, outgoing, playful
description: A low-set, strong, and sturdily built herding dog with short legs, fox-like face, alert expression, and naturally bobbed tail. Known for their intelligence, sturdy build, and distinctive appearance.
history: Ancient herding breed from Pembrokeshire, Wales, brought by Flemish weavers in 1107. Developed as a cattle herding dog, their low stature allowed them to nip at cattle's heels and avoid being kicked. Closely associated with British royalty.

name: Perdigueiro Galego
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, docile, energetic, alert
description: Medium-sized, noble Spanish pointing dog from the Galicia region with a refined build, short dense coat, and excellent hunting instincts. Known for its calm temperament and versatile abilities as an upland game retriever.
history: Ancient Iberian pointing breed native to northwestern Spain's Galicia region, developed for hunting upland game birds in rugged terrain. Nearly extinct but recovered through dedicated preservation efforts, formally recognized by Spain's Ministry of Agriculture in 2001 and Real Sociedad Canina de España in 2002.

name: Perro Majorero
temperament: Loyal, protective, intelligent, alert, confident, calm, courageous
description: Medium-sized primitive-type molossoid dog with a distinctive brindle coat, originating from Fuerteventura in the Canary Islands. This rustic livestock guardian dog combines the sturdy build of a working breed with the alert temperament needed for protecting goats and cattle.
history: Ancient indigenous breed from Fuerteventura in the Canary Islands, descended from dogs brought in pre-Hispanic times. Traditionally used by the Majorero people (natives of Fuerteventura) for herding goats and cattle and guarding livestock. Recognized by Spanish breed clubs and the Real Sociedad Canina de España.

name: Peruvian Inca Orchid
temperament: Loyal, affectionate, alert, intelligent, agile
description: Ancient hairless sighthound breed known for its graceful build, smooth skin, and affectionate personality
history: An ancient Peruvian breed dating back to pre-Incan civilizations valued for companionship and hunting, recognized by FCI and AKC.

name: Petit Basset Griffon Vendéen
temperament: Happy, outgoing, independent, alert, confident, energetic
description: Small, rough-coated French scent hound with sturdy build, distinctive beard and mustache, built low to the ground for hunting rabbit and hare through dense underbrush. Known for their happy demeanor and lively personality.
history: Developed in the 16th century Vendée region of France from larger Grand Griffon Vendéen hounds, bred specifically for hunting rabbit and hare in dense brush. The name describes the breed: Petit (small), Basset (low to the ground), Griffon (rough-coated), and Vendéen (from Vendée). First officially recognized in late 19th century.

name: Petit Bleu de Gascogne
temperament: Gentle, calm, intelligent, loyal, affectionate, alert
description: Medium-sized, noble French scent hound distinguished by its distinctive mottled 'blue' coat, long drooping ears, and exceptional sense of smell. Originally developed as a smaller version of the Grand Bleu de Gascogne, this diligent and determined hunter excels at tracking small game.
history: Originating from the Gascogne province of southwestern France, the Petit Bleu de Gascogne is a direct descendant of the Grand Bleu de Gascogne. Developed as a smaller version specifically for hunting small game such as hare and rabbit, recognized and standardized by the FCI.

name: Phalène
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, alert, gentle, playful, happy
description: The Phalène is the drop-eared variety of the Continental Toy Spaniel, an elegant and fine-boned toy breed with a long, flowing silky coat and alert expression. Known as the 'moth-eared' spaniel, this small, delicate breed is closely related to the Papillon.
history: The Phalène, or 'moth-eared' spaniel, is the original drop-eared variety of the Continental Toy Spaniel, predating the erect-eared Papillon. Known since at least the 16th century, these toy spaniels were favorites in European royal courts and are depicted in famous paintings. The FCI recognizes it as distinct from the Papillon for its ear carriage, though both types are often born in the same litters.

name: Pharaoh Hound
temperament: Intelligent, friendly, playful, alert, affectionate
description: Medium-sized elegant sighthound with short tan coat, alert expression, and distinctive ability to 'blush' on nose and ears when excited. A graceful, powerful, and swift breed of ancient origins.
history: National dog of Malta and ancient breed believed descended from Egyptian hunting dogs brought by Phoenician traders over 2,000 years ago. Known as 'Kelb tal-Fenek' (rabbit dog) in Malta, used for rabbit hunting. One of oldest domesticated breeds with depictions found in ancient Egyptian tombs. Recognized by AKC in 1983.

name: Philippine Forest Dog
temperament: Alert, intelligent, independent, agile
description: Medium-small native landrace dog adapted to the Philippines' forests, characterized by agility and alertness. This ancient indigenous breed has evolved to thrive in forest environments.
history: Ancient native dog population indigenous to the Philippines, adapted to forest environments and traditionally used for hunting and companionship. Not formally recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Phu Quoc Ridgeback
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, alert, independent, courageous
description: Medium-sized, athletic primitive hunting dog from Vietnam, distinguished by a characteristic ridge of hair running along its back in the opposite direction from the rest of the coat. One of only three ridge-backed breeds in the world, this agile and intelligent dog originated on Phu Quoc Island.
history: Ancient and primitive breed native to Phu Quoc Island in Vietnam's Kien Giang Province. For centuries used by local people as loyal companions for hunting, guarding property, and carting. Not recognized by major international kennel clubs; standards maintained by regional breed organizations.

name: Picardy Spaniel
temperament: Gentle, calm, intelligent, docile, devoted, adaptable
description: Medium-sized, strongly-built French pointing dog with distinctive grey-mottled brown feathered coat, drop ears, and gentle, docile expression. This elegant gundog combines calm adaptability with natural hunting ability.
history: One of the oldest continental spaniel-type pointing breeds, originating in the Picardy region of France. Valued by hunters for its versatility and weather-resistant coat, its numbers declined with imported English breeds but were saved from extinction by dedicated breeders after World War II.

name: Pitsky
temperament: Energetic, loyal, intelligent, confident, playful
description: A powerful and energetic mixed breed combining the athleticism and striking appearance of the Siberian Husky with the strength of the American Pit Bull Terrier, known for its athletic build and energetic temperament
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in recent decades by crossing the American Pit Bull Terrier with the Siberian Husky to create a dog with the Terrier's strength and the Husky's endurance and appearance. No formal breed club or standard exists.

name: Plott Hound
temperament: Loyal, alert, courageous, intelligent, devoted
description: Athletic, streamlined, and powerful scent hound with distinctive brindle coat, known for its intelligence, courage, and exceptional tracking ability. Originally bred for big-game hunting including bear and boar.
history: Developed in the mountains of North Carolina by German immigrant Johannes Georg Plott from German Hanoverian Schweisshunds brought in the 1750s. Perfected over generations for big-game hunting in the Appalachian Mountains. The only coonhound breed not descended from foxhounds and official state dog of North Carolina.

name: Plummer Terrier
temperament: Intelligent, courageous, energetic, alert, work-focused
description: Small, energetic working terrier with a distinctive smooth red and white coat, bred specifically for vermin control and known for its tenacious, fearless nature and athletic build
history: Developed in the 1960s by Dr. David Brian Plummer in the UK by crossing Jack Russell Terriers, Beagles, Fell Terriers, and Bull Terriers to create a hardy, versatile working terrier specialized for fox control and vermin hunting

name: Podenco Andaluz
temperament: Independent, alert, energetic, intelligent, loyal
description: An ancient, agile Spanish sighthound from the Andalusia region with erect ears and athletic build, known for its keen senses and hunting prowess. Exists in three size varieties and three coat types, specialized for rabbit hunting in Andalusian terrain.
history: Ancient primitive hound whose origins trace back to pharaonic dogs brought to the Iberian Peninsula by Phoenician traders. Developed in Andalusia for versatile hunting of rabbits and small game, utilizing sight, sound, and scent. Comes in three size varieties with shared hunting heritage.

name: Podenco Canario
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, alert, independent, courageous, loyal
description: An ancient, athletic sighthound of primitive type from the Canary Islands, characterized by its lean, elegant build, large erect ears, and remarkable agility. This medium-sized hunting dog has a smooth short coat and slender build optimized for speed and hunting rabbits in rocky volcanic terrain.
history: Ancient breed originating in the Canary Islands, believed descended from primitive dogs brought by early Mediterranean settlers like the Phoenicians. Adapted over centuries to the volcanic terrain, becoming a highly specialized rabbit hunter recognized by the FCI as a primitive-type hunting breed.

name: Podenco Valenciano
temperament: Alert, energetic, independent, intelligent, loyal, athletic
description: Medium-sized, primitive-type sighthound from the Valencian region of Spain with lean, athletic build and large ears. Known for its keen hunting senses using sight, smell, and hearing, this agile breed was traditionally used for rabbit hunting across varied terrain.
history: Ancient, native breed from the Valencian region of Spain, traditionally used by hunters for finding and catching rabbits. A rustic, resilient dog that is standardized by the Real Sociedad Canina de España (RSCE) and recognized by FCI as a regional variety related to the Podenco Ibicenco.

name: Pointer
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, affectionate, even-tempered, adaptable
description: An athletic and graceful medium-large sporting dog renowned for its speed, stamina, and keen nose. Known for its characteristic pointing stance and aristocratic demeanor, the Pointer is a hard-driving hunting companion with exceptional field abilities.
history: The Pointer's ancestors are believed to have originated in Spain and were brought to England in the early 1700s. Developed by crossing Spanish Pointers with local scent and setter-type dogs, the breed was refined by British sportsmen into the premier bird-hunting gundog known for its iconic pointing stance.

name: Poitevin
temperament: Intelligent, courageous, independent, gentle, devoted
description: Large, elegant French scent hound with distinctive tricolor coat and lean, muscular build. Bred for endurance and stamina in pack hunting, originally developed for hunting wolves and large game with exceptional scenting ability and melodic voice.
history: Created in 1692 by Marquis François de Larrye from remnants of Haut-Poitou hound packs, developed for hunting wolves and large game. Nearly extinct after WWII but restored using English Foxhound crosses. Recognized by the FCI in the Scenthound Group.

name: Polish Hound
temperament: Loyal, calm, intelligent, alert, gentle
description: Medium-sized, strongly built scent hound with robust build and distinctive black and tan coat, bred for stamina and tracking large game in dense forests. Known for its powerful, melodious voice and calm yet alert temperament.
history: Ancient Polish hunting breed dating back to the Middle Ages, with origins documented in 14th century literature. Developed by crossing local hounds with imported St. Hubert Hounds for tracking large game in forests. Colonel Józef Pawłusiewicz was instrumental in re-establishing the breed after World War II. Recognized by FCI as national breed of Poland.

name: Polish Lowland Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, confident, alert, loyal, energetic
description: Medium-sized, shaggy-coated herding dog known for its lively, intelligent, and confident temperament. Has a long, thick double coat that covers its eyes and is valued for its adaptability and energetic work ethic.
history: Ancient herding breed from Poland descended from herding dogs of the Hungarian plains, used for herding and guarding flocks in lowland regions. Known as Polski Owczarek Nizinny (PON), nearly driven to extinction after WWII but revived by dedicated breeders including a Polish veterinarian.

name: Pomapoo
temperament: Intelligent, affectionate, playful, alert, adaptable
description: A small designer crossbreed between a Pomeranian and Toy or Miniature Poodle, featuring a soft, fluffy coat and expressive face. This intelligent, affectionate companion dog often inherits the Poodle's low-shedding coat with a lively, adaptable personality.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States by crossing Pomeranian with Toy or Miniature Poodle. Primary goal was to create a small, intelligent companion dog with reduced-shedding coat suitable for allergy sufferers.

name: Pomeranian
temperament: Alert, intelligent, playful, confident, outgoing
origin: Pomerania region (now parts of Germany and Poland)
description: Small, compact, active toy spitz breed with abundant fluffy double coat, fox-like expression, and plumed tail carried over the back
history: Descended from larger Arctic spitz-type sled dogs and bred down to current small size in the Pomerania region. Popularity greatly increased by Queen Victoria who became a breeder and established distinct breed standards

name: Pomsky
temperament: Playful, intelligent, energetic, alert, affectionate, loyal
description: Designer crossbreed between Pomeranian and Siberian Husky with striking wolf-like appearance in a compact, manageable size. Features thick plush coat, fox-like face resembling a miniature Husky, with varied appearance and size depending on individual inheritance.
history: Modern designer crossbreed that gained popularity in the early 2010s through internet fame. Breeders aimed to create smaller apartment-friendly dog with Husky appearance by crossing with Pomeranian. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Pont-Audemer Spaniel
temperament: Affectionate, intelligent, gentle, athletic, devoted, playful, loyal
description: Medium-sized, rustic French water spaniel with distinctive curly, water-resistant coat, topknot, and gentle, clownish expression. Specialized for wetland game retrieval with excellent swimming abilities.
history: Developed in 19th century Normandy, France by crossing local water spaniels with Irish Water Spaniels and potentially Poodles for waterfowl hunting in marshlands. Numbers dwindled after World War II and was revived through careful breeding.

name: Poochon
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, playful, affectionate, outgoing, adaptable
description: Small designer crossbreed combining Toy Poodle intelligence with Bichon Frisé cheerfulness, featuring a hypoallergenic curly coat and playful, affectionate personality
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the late 1990s and early 2000s to combine the hypoallergenic qualities and intelligence of the Toy Poodle with the cheerful, friendly temperament of the Bichon Frisé

name: Poodle
temperament: Intelligent, athletic, alert, confident, loyal, eager to please
description: An exceptionally intelligent, proud, and elegant dog with athletic bearing and curly hypoallergenic coat. Originally bred as a water retriever, this athletic breed excels as a companion and in various dog sports.
history: Originated in Germany as a water retrieving dog, with name derived from German 'pudelin' meaning 'to splash in the water.' Later standardized and refined in France where it became a popular companion. One of the oldest hunting dogs recognized globally for intelligence and versatility.

name: Poodle (Miniature)
temperament: Intelligent, alert, playful, confident, eager to please
description: Elegant, athletic, and intelligent smaller version of the Standard Poodle with a curly, hypoallergenic coat and proud carriage
history: Originally developed as water retrievers in Germany, the name 'Poodle' comes from German 'pudel' meaning 'to splash in water'. Miniature variety was bred down from Standard Poodles for companionship, circus performance, and truffle hunting

name: Poodle (Toy)
temperament: Intelligent, alert, confident, playful, affectionate, loyal, outgoing
description: Smallest Poodle variety with dense curly hypoallergenic coat and alert, intelligent demeanor. An elegant, proud companion dog known for exceptional trainability and performing ability.
history: Despite association with France the Poodle originated in Germany as water retrieving dog from German pudel meaning to splash in water. The Toy variety was bred down from Standard and Miniature Poodles for urban companionship and prized by aristocracy for intelligence and performing ability. AKC recognition 1887.

name: Porcelaine
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, energetic, friendly, work-focused
description: Elegant, medium-sized French scenthound with a distinctive glossy white coat that gives the breed its name, resembling porcelain. Known for its refined build, endurance, speed, and exceptional nose for hunting.
history: One of the oldest French scent hounds, descended from white St. Hubert hounds in the Franche-Comté region. Dating back to the 18th century, the breed nearly disappeared after the French Revolution and WWII but was carefully reconstructed by enthusiasts in the mid-19th century using Swiss specimens.

name: Portuguese Podengo
temperament: Alert, intelligent, energetic, loyal, independent
description: Ancient multi-purpose hound from Portugal that comes in three sizes (Pequeno, Medio, Grande) and two coat types (smooth and wire). Medium-sized, agile breed with erect ears, wedge-shaped head, and keen sight and scent abilities developed for hunting rabbits and small game
history: Ancient breed dating back over 2000 years, likely descended from dogs brought by Phoenician and Roman traders. Developed in three distinct sizes to hunt different game across various Portuguese terrains, primarily rabbits and small game

name: Portuguese Pointer
temperament: Affectionate, loyal, intelligent, devoted, energetic, alert
description: A medium-sized, athletic gundog of noble appearance with short dense coat and expressive face, known for its extreme devotion to its master, excellent scenting ability, and dedicated, persistent hunting style
history: Ancient Portuguese hunting breed descended from Iberian hunting dogs, used for centuries by nobility to hunt partridge and game birds. Known locally as Perdigueiro Português, the breed standard was established in early 20th century, reviving it from near extinction.

name: Portuguese Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, alert, devoted
description: Medium-sized herding dog with long, shaggy wavy coat and lively intelligent expression, known for its monkey-like gait and rustic appearance
history: Developed in the Serra de Aires region of Portugal, likely from Briards brought in the early 1900s. Used extensively for herding sheep, goats, and cattle. Known locally as 'cão macaco' (monkey dog) for its lively antics.

name: Portuguese Water Dog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, athletic, eager to please
description: Athletic, medium-sized working dog with a robust build and webbed feet, known for its single coat of curly or wavy waterproof hair. Originally bred as an all-around fisherman's helper.
history: Ancient breed from Portuguese coast, developed as fisherman's helper for herding fish into nets, retrieving tackle, and carrying messages between ships and shore. Nearly extinct in 20th century but saved through dedicated breeding efforts.

name: Posavac Hound
temperament: Calm, devoted, energetic, intelligent, independent
description: Medium-sized Croatian scenthound of robust, noble build with distinctive reddish coat and white markings. Known for exceptional stamina, scenting ability, and sonorous voice when tracking.
history: Ancient Croatian scenthound from the Posavina region with ancestry depicted in 15th-century frescoes. Formally documented in 18th century with first FCI standard published in 1955.

name: Pražský Krysařík
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, affectionate, lively, playful, confident
description: Very small, elegant toy breed with fine bone structure, alert expression, and smooth or long coat varieties. Originally bred in Bohemia as a companion and ratter in royal courts.
history: Ancient Czech breed dating to Middle Ages, developed as companion and ratting dog favored by nobility in Prague Castle and royal courts. Recognized by FCI as Czech national breed and revived from near-extinction in the 1980s.

name: Presa Canario
temperament: Confident, calm, protective, intelligent, loyal, courageous
description: Large, powerfully built mastiff-type dog with robust frame, calm attentive expression, and imposing presence as a confident guardian
history: Ancient breed native to the Canary Islands, originally developed as versatile farm and guard dog for guarding livestock and property, used for catching and holding cattle. Nearly extinct by the 1960s but revived through careful breeding programs in the 1970s

name: Pudelpointer
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, eager to please, work-focused, energetic, playful
description: Medium to large versatile hunting dog combining Poodle intelligence with Pointer field abilities, featuring a distinctive dense wiry coat and keen, energetic temperament bred for exceptional performance in both field and water
history: Developed in Germany in the late 19th century by Baron von Zedlitz, who crossed German Poodles (Pudel) with English Pointers to create the ideal versatile gundog for field and water work

name: Pug
temperament: Affectionate, playful, friendly, charming, adaptable
description: Small, compact, and sturdy toy breed with a distinctive wrinkled, short-muzzled face, curled tail, and charming, mischievous personality
history: Ancient breed originating in China as prized companions for Chinese emperors. Brought to Europe in the 16th century by Dutch traders, became official dog of House of Orange in Holland, gained widespread popularity in Victorian England

name: Pugapoo
temperament: Affectionate, playful, intelligent, outgoing, adaptable
description: Small designer crossbreed combining Pug and Poodle traits, featuring a compact build with wavy or curly coat and lively, affectionate temperament. Appearance varies significantly depending on genetic inheritance from parent breeds.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in late 20th century by crossing Pug with Poodle to combine affectionate comical Pug temperament with Poodle intelligence and potentially lower-shedding coat. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Puggle
temperament: Friendly, playful, affectionate, outgoing, energetic, intelligent
description: Small to medium-sized mixed breed crossbreed between a Pug and Beagle, known for its wrinkled, hound-like face, sturdy body, and energetic, affectionate nature
history: Designer crossbreed first bred in the 1980s in the United States, gaining significant popularity in the early 2000s. Intentionally created by crossing Pug with Beagle to combine the affectionate nature of the Pug with the energetic traits of the Beagle

name: Puli
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, alert, devoted, playful
description: Compact, medium-sized Hungarian herding dog known for its distinctive, naturally corded coat resembling dreadlocks, giving it an agile and athletic build with quick, spirited movement
history: Ancient Hungarian sheepdog breed brought by Magyar tribes over 1000 years ago, traditionally used for herding and guarding livestock on the Hungarian plains. Their unique corded coat provided protection from predators and harsh weather. Nearly decimated by World War II but saved by dedicated breeding programs

name: Pumi
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, alert, playful, loyal, confident
description: Medium-sized, agile Hungarian herding dog known for its whimsical, terrier-like expression, distinctive corkscrew-curled coat, and high-set semi-erect ears with an alert, lively attitude
history: Developed in Hungary during the 17th-18th centuries from crossbreeding the native Puli with German and French herding dogs and terriers. Used as versatile farm dogs for herding cattle, sheep, and swine

name: Pungsan
temperament: Loyal, courageous, intelligent, alert, independent
origin: Pungsan County, North Korea (Kaema Plateau region)
description: Medium to large spitz-type hunting dog from North Korea with thick white double coat and wolf-like appearance, known for strength, stamina, and resilience in harsh mountain climates
history: Developed in the mountainous Kaema Plateau of North Korea as a versatile and resilient hunter of large game including wild boar. Considered a national treasure of North Korea. In 2000, a pair was given as a state gift to South Korea, increasing international recognition.

name: Pyredoodle
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, gentle, protective, calm, affectionate
description: Large mixed breed created by crossing a Great Pyrenees and Standard Poodle, combining the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat with the Great Pyrenees' calm, protective nature. Features a sturdy build with wavy or curly coat, though traits can vary significantly.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States to combine the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat with the Great Pyrenees' calm temperament and protective instincts. Recognized by designer dog registries but not major kennel clubs.

name: Pyrenean Mastiff
temperament: Calm, gentle, courageous, protective, intelligent, loyal
description: A giant, powerful, and noble livestock guardian breed from the Spanish Pyrenees, known for its calm demeanor, thick white double coat with darker patches, and courageous yet gentle protective nature
history: Ancient livestock guardian breed from the Pyrenees mountains of Spain, developed to protect flocks from wolves and bears. The breed faced near extinction after the Spanish Civil War but was revived by dedicated breeders in the 1970s

name: Pyrenean Mountain Dog
temperament: Gentle, confident, protective, independent, calm, loyal
description: Large, majestic, and immensely powerful livestock guardian with thick white double coat and natural guardian instincts developed for mountain work
history: Ancient breed used for centuries by shepherds in the Pyrenees Mountains of France and Spain to guard flocks against predators like wolves and bears. Known as 'Chien de Montagne des Pyrénées' and standardized by the FCI.

name: Pyrenean Sheepdog
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, alert, courageous, playful, devoted, work-focused
description: Small to medium-sized herding dog with long, flowing coat, known for its intelligence, boundless energy, and mischievous nature. Bred for working sheep in the Pyrenees mountains, this lean and agile breed exists in both rough-faced and smooth-faced varieties.
history: Ancient breed developed in the Pyrenees mountains of France for herding sheep, often working in partnership with Great Pyrenees as flock guardians. Recognized by FCI as Chien de Berger des Pyrénées with two coat varieties.

name: Rafeiro do Alentejo
temperament: Calm, confident, protective, loyal, independent, alert
description: Large, powerful livestock guardian dog from the Alentejo region of Portugal with an imposing presence, dense coat, and calm, sober temperament developed for protecting herds against predators
history: Ancient Iberian mastiff-type breed descended from dogs of the Tibetan plateau, used for centuries by Portuguese shepherds to guard flocks against predators in the Alentejo plains; recognized by FCI as breed 96

name: Rajapalayam
temperament: Loyal, protective, independent, alert, courageous, intelligent
description: Large, muscular Indian sighthound with pristine white coat and distinctive pink nose. This powerful breed has a loyal, protective nature and was historically used for hunting wild boar and as a royal companion.
history: Ancient breed named after the town of Rajapalayam in Tamil Nadu, developed as hunting and guard dogs by royalty and aristocracy in Southern India. Traditionally used for hunting wild boar and recognized by the Kennel Club of India.

name: Rampur Greyhound
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, independent, courageous, protective
description: Large, powerfully-built sighthound from India with a lean, athletic build and short coat, known for incredible speed, endurance, and deep loyalty, originally developed for hunting large game in the Indian subcontinent
history: Developed in the princely state of Rampur during the 18th-20th century by royalty including Ahmad Ali Khan, the Nawab of Rampur, by crossing Afghan Hounds, Persian Greyhounds (Salukis), and English Greyhounds for hunting large and dangerous game

name: Rastreador Brasileiro
temperament: Courageous, intelligent, loyal, calm, alert, work-focused
description: Large, athletic Brazilian scenthound with a short, dense coat, developed for tracking jaguar and other large game with exceptional scenting ability, known for perseverance and courage in hunting
history: Developed in Brazil from European scenthounds and local dogs for tracking jaguar and large game in Brazilian terrain. Declared extinct by FCI in 1973 following disease outbreak but successfully reconstituted through dedicated breeding project and granted definitive FCI recognition again in 2019

name: Ratonero Murciano
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, loyal, courageous
description: Small, agile, and athletic Spanish terrier breed from the Murcia region, historically developed for ratting and farm work with a compact build and alert expression
history: Traditional landrace terrier developed in the agricultural region of Murcia in southeastern Spain, essential for controlling rodent populations in farms, wineries, and stables. Refined over centuries from small terriers brought to the area for their specific hunting purpose. Officially recognized by the Real Sociedad Canina de España (RSCE) in 2011.

name: Rat Terrier
temperament: Alert, intelligent, energetic, friendly, playful, loyal
description: Compact, energetic American terrier with smooth coat and athletic build, developed as a versatile all-purpose farm dog and vermin hunter
history: Developed in early 20th century United States from European terriers brought by British migrants, including Fox Terrier and Manchester Terrier, for farm vermin control and companionship. President Theodore Roosevelt was a notable owner and credited with naming the breed. AKC recognition in 2013.

name: Redbone Coonhound
temperament: Friendly, loyal, eager to please, energetic, affectionate
description: Medium to large athletic hound with a striking solid red coat, known for its speed, agility, and powerful scenting ability. Sleek and muscular, this breed was developed specifically for hunting raccoons and other game.
history: Developed in the American South, primarily in Georgia, during the late 18th and early 19th centuries by Scottish immigrants. Created from red-colored Irish Foxhounds and Bloodhounds to produce a fast, hot-nosed hound for trailing and treeing raccoons and larger game. The breed name is attributed to early breeder Peter Redbone. Recognized by the AKC in 2009.

name: Rhodesian Ridgeback
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, confident, independent, affectionate
description: Large, muscular, and athletic hound with a distinctive ridge of hair running along its back, originally bred to hunt lions in Africa. Known for their dignified, even-tempered nature and loyal disposition.
history: Developed in Southern Africa by crossing native ridged Khoikhoi dogs with European breeds like Mastiffs and Greyhounds. Bred by farmers and hunters for tracking and holding big game, notably lions, at bay. Recognized by South African Kennel Union in the 1920s.

name: Ridgeypoo
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, athletic, confident, affectionate, energetic
description: A large, athletic mixed breed created by crossing a Rhodesian Ridgeback with a Standard Poodle, combining the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat with the Ridgeback's strength, loyalty, and distinctive athletic build
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed by combining the Rhodesian Ridgeback and Standard Poodle to create a large, athletic, intelligent companion dog with a lower-shedding coat

name: Rize Koyun
temperament: Protective, independent, courageous, loyal, alert
description: A rare, large and powerful Turkish livestock guardian dog from the Rize province, featuring a thick double coat and protective temperament. This regional landrace is specifically adapted for protecting flocks in the mountainous Black Sea region.
history: A landrace livestock guardian dog developed over centuries by shepherds in Turkey's Rize province in the Black Sea region. Bred to protect sheep and other livestock from predators in rugged, mountainous terrain. Shares ancestry with other Turkish shepherd breeds but specifically adapted for local climate and terrain. Not recognized as a standardized breed by major international kennel clubs.

name: Romanian Mioritic Shepherd Dog
temperament: Calm, protective, independent, loyal, intelligent, courageous, devoted
description: Large, powerful livestock guardian dog native to the Carpathian Mountains of Romania, covered in an abundant, long, shaggy double coat that is typically white or grey. Known for its imposing presence, calm demeanor, and strong protective instincts developed for guarding sheep flocks against predators like bears, wolves, and lynx.
history: Ancient breed developed as a premier livestock guardian dog in the Carpathian Mountains of Romania, selected for courage and ability to protect flocks from large predators. First breed standard written by Romanian Cynological Association in 1981, provisionally recognized by FCI in 2005.

name: Romanian Raven Shepherd Dog
temperament: Protective, loyal, calm, courageous, intelligent, independent, devoted
description: Large, powerful livestock guardian dog from the Carpathian Mountains, distinguished by its impressive size and entirely black coat. Known as 'Corb' (meaning 'Raven' in Romanian), this robust and courageous breed was developed for protecting sheep flocks from wolves, bears, and lynx, valued for its calm demeanor and devotion to its flock and family.
history: Ancient native breed from the Carpathian and Sub-Carpathian regions of Romania, used for centuries as guardian of livestock against large predators. Officially recognized by the FCI on provisional basis in 2019, valued for courage, loyalty and ability to deter wolves, bears and lynx

name: Rottle
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, protective, confident, affectionate, energetic
description: Large, athletic and intelligent mixed breed crossing the Rottweiler and Standard Poodle, combining the Rottweiler's protective nature and strength with the Poodle's intelligence and potentially low-shedding curly or wavy coat
history: Modern designer crossbreed intentionally developed by mixing Rottweiler and Standard Poodle to combine the Rottweiler's loyal guardian nature with the Poodle's high intelligence and low-shedding coat qualities. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Rottsky
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, protective, athletic, confident
description: Large, athletic mixed breed with well-muscled build and thick double coat, combining the strength and loyalty of the Rottweiler with the endurance and independent spirit of the Siberian Husky. Physical appearance and temperament can vary significantly.
history: Modern designer crossbreed created by breeding Rottweiler with Siberian Husky, aiming to blend guarding instincts and loyalty with stamina and intelligence. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Rottweiler
temperament: Confident, loyal, courageous, calm, protective, affectionate
description: Large, robust, muscular working dog with black coat and tan markings, known for great strength, protective instincts, and confident temperament. A loyal and loving guardian breed.
history: Descended from Roman drover dogs, developed in the German town of Rottweil as cattle herders and guard dogs. Known as the 'Butcher's Dog of Rottweil', they protected butchers' money to and from market. Numbers declined with advent of railroads but was revived in early 20th century. Later used for protection and police work

name: Rough Collie
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, devoted, gentle, protective, alert, athletic
description: An elegant and graceful large herding dog with a long, profuse double coat and intelligent expression, known for unwavering loyalty and gentle nature
history: Developed in the Scottish Highlands and Northern England as a versatile sheep-herding dog. The breed's popularity soared after Queen Victoria became an enthusiast in the 19th century. The fictional character 'Lassie' made the Rough Collie a global icon.

name: Russian Spaniel
temperament: Energetic, friendly, intelligent, devoted, loyal
description: Small to medium-sized sporting dog with long, silky coat and feathered ears, known for its energetic, friendly nature. The only gundog breed developed in Russia, this sturdy flushing spaniel was created for hunting game birds in Russian terrain.
history: Developed in the Soviet Union after World War II by crossing English Cocker Spaniels, English Springer Spaniels, and other European spaniels to create a versatile gundog suited to the vast and varied terrain of Russia. The first official breed standard was established in 1951.

name: Russian Toy
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, playful, affectionate, energetic, outgoing
description: Very small, elegant toy breed with fine bone structure and lively expression, available in both smooth and long coat varieties. Known for their cheerful disposition and fragile build.
history: Developed in Russia from English Toy Terriers in the early 20th century. Nearly extinct after the Russian Revolution but rebuilt from remaining dogs post-WWII, leading to standardization of both smooth and long-coated varieties. Recently recognized by AKC in 2022.

name: Russo-European Laika
temperament: Alert, courageous, independent, intelligent, loyal, energetic, confident
description: Medium-sized, strong spitz-type hunting dog of the northern forest zone with erect ears, curled bushy tail, and dense weather-resistant double coat, known for its lively, balanced temperament and versatile hunting abilities
history: Developed from aboriginal hunting dogs (Laikas) from the northern regions of European Russia and northeastern Europe. Officially standardized in the mid-20th century to preserve the working qualities of these versatile hunting spitz for hunting game including small mammals and birds.

name: Ryukyu
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, independent, courageous, alert, energetic
description: Medium-sized, primitive hunting dog from the Ryukyu Islands of Japan with an athletic build, triangular ears, and a distinctive brindle coat. Known for its loyalty to family and traditional use in wild boar hunting. Also, known as 'Ryukyu Inu'.
history: An indigenous primitive dog native to the Ryukyu Islands, traditionally used for hunting wild boar in dense subtropical forests. The breed faced near extinction but was revived through dedicated preservation efforts starting in the 1980s by the Ryukyu Inu Hozonkai (Ryukyu Dog Preservation Society). Rare breed recognized by the Nihon Ken Hozonkai but not by major international kennel clubs.

name: Saarloos Wolfdog
temperament: Independent, alert, loyal, reserved, intelligent
description: Large, strongly built wolf-like dog with reserved temperament and strong pack instincts, originally created by crossing a German Shepherd with a Eurasian wolf
history: Created in the 1930s by Dutch breeder Leendert Saarloos by crossing a German Shepherd Dog with a female Eurasian wolf, aiming to create a more natural, robust working dog. Officially recognized by the Dutch Kennel Club in 1975.

name: Sabueso Español
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, calm, devoted, courageous
description: A medium-sized, ancient Spanish scenthound characterized by its long, pendulous ears, noble expression, and exceptional scenting ability, bred for tracking game across varied terrain of the Iberian Peninsula
history: Believed to be the oldest of the Iberian scenthound breeds, with descriptions dating back to the 14th century in writings by King Alfonso XI. Developed for tracking wild boar and other game across the Iberian Peninsula, recognized by the FCI

name: Saint Berdoodle
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, friendly, loyal, affectionate, calm, protective
description: Large and powerful mixed breed combining the gentle, patient nature of the Saint Bernard with the intelligence and often low-shedding coat of the Standard Poodle. Traits can vary significantly based on genetic inheritance, often featuring wavy or curly coats.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States in the late 20th century as part of the 'designer dog' movement. The goal was to combine Saint Bernard's gentle temperament with Standard Poodle's intelligence and hypoallergenic qualities to create a large, family-friendly companion.

name: Saint Bernard
temperament: Gentle, friendly, calm, patient, loyal, protective
description: A powerful, giant-sized working breed from the Swiss Alps with distinctive markings and massive head, originally bred for mountain rescue. Known for its gentle, patient nature and calm temperament.
history: Developed by monks at the Great St. Bernard Hospice on the border of Switzerland and Italy for rescue work in treacherous Alpine conditions. Used to locate and rescue travelers lost in snow and fog of dangerous Alpine passes

name: Saint Miguel Cattle Dog
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, protective, work-focused, courageous, alert
description: Medium-sized, robust Portuguese cattle herding dog with distinctive brindle coat, known for intelligence, strong work ethic, and guarding instincts. Native to the Azores islands.
history: Developed on São Miguel Island in the Azores, Portugal. Origins linked to extinct Terceira Dog and molosser types brought by early settlers. Served for centuries as exceptional cattle herding and farm guardian dog, with first written standard appearing in 1984. Also known as Cão Fila de São Miguel.

name: Saint-Usuge Spaniel
temperament: Gentle, affectionate, intelligent, eager to please, loyal, devoted, energetic, adaptable
description: A medium-sized, elegant, and robust French spaniel with a brown coat, known for its versatility as a pointing and retrieving gundog with a gentle and affectionate personality
history: An ancient French breed developed in the 19th century in the Bresse region of France as a versatile gundog. It nearly became extinct after World War II, but its survival is credited to the dedicated efforts of Father Robert Billard, who began a careful breeding program in 1950 using remaining stock and regional historical standards. The breed was recognized by the Société Centrale Canine (France) in 1990 and provisionally by the FCI in 2003.

name: Sakhalin Husky
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, independent, work-focused, energetic, courageous
description: Large, powerful spitz-type sled dog originating from Sakhalin Island, also known as the Karafuto Ken. This robust arctic breed with dense double coat was originally bred for endurance and strength in cold climates but is now considered functionally extinct with no active breeding programs.
history: Developed by the indigenous Nivkh people of Sakhalin Island for sledding, hunting and transportation. Gained international fame after the 1958 Japanese Antarctic expedition where dogs Taro and Jiro survived a year abandoned in Antarctica. Despite revival efforts, the breed is considered functionally extinct since the last confirmed purebred died in the 1970s.

name: Saluki
temperament: Gentle, independent, intelligent, devoted, calm, reserved
description: Ancient, elegant sighthound of the Middle East with graceful build, feathered ears and tail, known for extraordinary speed, endurance, and dignified nature. Bred for hunting gazelle across desert terrain.
history: One of the most ancient domesticated dog breeds, treasured by royalty and Bedouin tribes for thousands of years. Considered sacred by Muslims and revered throughout the Middle East for hunting gazelle and swift prey across desert terrain.

name: Samoyed
temperament: Friendly, gentle, adaptable, alert, intelligent, playful
description: A powerful and graceful medium to large Arctic spitz breed with a thick, all-white double coat and distinctive 'Samoyed smile' created by the upturned corners of the mouth
history: Ancient breed developed by the nomadic Samoyede people of Siberia over 3,000 years ago for herding reindeer, pulling sleds, and companionship. Their thick coats protected them from brutal climate, and they often slept with families for warmth. Brought out of Siberia in late 19th and early 20th centuries. Recognized by AKC in 1906.

name: Sapsali
temperament: Loyal, gentle, friendly, protective, intelligent, patient
description: Medium-sized Korean breed with long, shaggy coat covering the eyes and robust build, originally kept as a spiritual guardian believed to dispel evil spirits and bring good fortune
history: Ancient Korean breed traditionally believed to dispel evil spirits and bring good fortune. Nearly extinct during Japanese occupation but saved through dedicated preservation efforts by professors at Kyungpook National University, leading to designation as National Treasure

name: Sarabi
temperament: Protective, independent, calm, loyal, courageous
origin: Sarab County, East Azerbaijan Province, Iran
description: A large, powerful livestock guardian landrace breed from Sarab County in Iran, known for its massive frame, protective instincts, loyalty, and imposing presence
history: An ancient landrace breed from the Sarab region of northwestern Iran, developed over centuries as a powerful livestock guardian dog to protect flocks from large predators such as wolves and bears. Not officially recognized by major international kennel clubs.

name: Sarail Hound
temperament: Loyal, courageous, intelligent, protective, energetic, alert
description: A rare, tall, and athletic sighthound native to Bangladesh, renowned for its speed, endurance, and hunting ability. Known for being courageous, loyal, and protective, this breed was historically prized for hunting and guarding estates.
history: An ancient and rare sighthound breed originating from the Sarail region of Bangladesh. Believed to have been developed by Moghul zamindars by crossing Arabian Salukis with local dog breeds. Historically used by village households and officials, valued for speed, loyalty, and guarding ability in hunting and coursing game.

name: Sardinian Shepherd Dog
temperament: Protective, loyal, independent, intelligent, alert, courageous
description: Medium-sized rustic livestock guardian native to the island of Sardinia, known for its robust build, weather-resistant goat-like coat, and strong protective instincts developed through centuries of guarding livestock in rugged terrain
history: Ancient landrace breed native to the island of Sardinia, traditionally used by local shepherds for guarding livestock against predators for centuries. Developed through natural selection in relative isolation, preserving strong working instincts and hardiness. Also known as Pastore Fonnese or Cane da Pastore Fonnese, officially recognized by Italian Kennel Club (ENCI) in 2013

name: Šarplaninac
temperament: Independent, protective, loyal, calm, courageous, confident
origin: Šar Mountains region (Serbia, North Macedonia, Kosovo, Albania)
description: Large, robust livestock guardian dog from the Balkan region with a dense, weather-resistant double coat and calm, independent protective nature. This ancient molossoid-type breed is known for its powerful build and natural guardian instincts.
history: Ancient molossoid-type livestock guardian breed developed in the Šar Mountains region, used for centuries to protect sheep from wolves and bears. First standardized by the Yugoslavian Kennel Club in 1939 and recognized by the FCI in 1957 as the Yugoslav Shepherd Dog—Sharplanina.

name: Schapendoes
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, energetic, playful, courageous, alert
description: Medium-sized Dutch herding dog with a long, shaggy double coat and lively, cheerful disposition. Known for its springy, effortless movement and lightly built frame, this energetic breed combines intelligence with an independent herding nature.
history: Ancient Dutch herding breed developed to herd sheep in the wetlands of the Netherlands. Nearly extinct after WWII but successfully revived by P.M.C. Toepoel and dedicated fanciers in the 1940s-1950s from remaining dogs.

name: Schillerstövare
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, alert, loyal, courageous, work-focused
description: A medium-sized, noble Swedish scenthound with a distinctive tan coat and black mantle, known for its athletic nature and exceptional ability as a hunter of fox and hare. This energetic Scandinavian breed combines endurance with keen scenting abilities.
history: Developed in the late 19th century by Swedish farmer Per Schiller, who crossed Swiss and German scenthounds with local hounds and English Harrier-type hounds. Created to be an exceptional hunter of fox and hare, first exhibited in 1907.

name: Schipperke
temperament: Alert, confident, energetic, intelligent, independent, curious
description: Small, compact, tailless spitz-type dog with a distinctive fox-like face, thick double coat, and abundant ruff around the neck. Known as the 'little captain', this agile black dog has a confident demeanor and alert expression.
history: Developed in the Flemish regions of Belgium as a canal barge watchdog and ratter. The name means 'little captain' and the breed served as an adept guardian and vermin hunter on Belgian waterways for centuries.

name: Schnoodle
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, playful, alert, affectionate, devoted, energetic
description: A crossbreed between a Poodle and a Schnauzer, combining intelligence, charming personality, and low-shedding coat. This mixed breed companion dog is known for its playful nature, with size and appearance varying significantly depending on the parent breeds used.
history: Developed in the United States starting in the 1980s as a designer crossbreed created to combine the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat with the Schnauzer's sturdiness and alert nature for companion and family purposes

name: Schweizerischer Niederlaufhund
temperament: Intelligent, alert, energetic, friendly, loyal, independent
description: A smaller version of the Swiss Hound, the Schweizerischer Niederlaufhund (Small Swiss Hound) is a sturdy, energetic scenthound with short legs bred for hunting in the mountainous terrain of Switzerland. This medium-small scent hound comes in four distinct coat varieties and possesses keen hunting instincts ideal for tracking game in confined territories.
history: Developed in Switzerland around 1905 when hunting regulations restricted hunting to specific districts, favoring smaller, slower hounds. Created by selectively breeding smaller specimens of existing Swiss Hounds to create dogs well-suited for hunting in confined mountainous territories.

name: Schweizer Laufhund
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, gentle, devoted, alert, independent
description: Medium-sized Swiss scent hound with lean, athletic build and distinctive tricolor or bicolor coat, bred for tracking game in mountainous terrain. Known for noble appearance, endurance, and passionate hunting ability, this ancient breed comes in four distinct varieties: Bernese, Jura, Lucerne, and Schwyz.
history: Ancient breed descending from scenthounds present in Switzerland since Roman times, prized by European gentry in the 15th-18th centuries. The modern standard was established in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, unifying four regional varieties into one breed standard.

name: Scoodle
temperament: Intelligent, confident, alert, playful, loyal
description: Small to medium spirited crossbreed combining Scottish Terrier's bold, confident temperament with Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat. Features a sturdy build with wiry or curly coat and lively expression.
history: Modern designer crossbreed intentionally developed in the United States to combine Scottish Terrier's bold, confident personality with Poodle's intelligence and hypoallergenic coat qualities

name: Scottish Deerhound
temperament: Gentle, dignified, calm, loyal, friendly
description: Large, athletic sighthound with rough, wiry coat and dignified bearing, often called the 'Royal Dog of Scotland', bred for coursing giant red deer in the Scottish Highlands
history: Ancient sighthound breed developed in Scottish Highlands to hunt giant wild red deer. Once so prized that ownership was restricted to nobility, nearly went extinct with decline of clan system but was revived in 19th century. Recognized by AKC in 1886.

name: Scottish Terrier
temperament: Confident, independent, alert, intelligent, loyal
description: Small, compact, and sturdy terrier with a distinctive wiry coat, erect ears, beard and eyebrows, and dignified, confident presence
history: One of Scotland's oldest Highland terrier breeds, developed in the Scottish Highlands for hunting vermin in difficult terrain. Recognized by The Kennel Club (UK) in 1883 and the AKC in 1885.

name: Sealyham Terrier
temperament: Friendly, alert, confident, outgoing, courageous
description: Small, sturdy white terrier with rectangular body shape, long hard wiry coat, and distinctive beard and furnishings on face and legs
history: Developed in the mid-19th century by Captain John Edwardes at his Sealy Ham estate in Wales for hunting badgers foxes and otters. Recognized by The Kennel Club in 1910 and AKC in 1911

name: Segugio dell'Appennino
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, alert, courageous, work-focused, independent
description: A medium-sized, rugged Italian scenthound with a short coat (smooth or wire-haired varieties), specifically developed for hunting hare in the challenging mountainous terrain of the Apennines. This athletic, work-focused breed combines intelligence with the independent determination typical of scenthounds.
history: Descended from ancient hounds of the Italian Apennines, likely influenced by dogs brought by Phoenician merchants. Traditional Italian scenthound developed over centuries specifically for hunting hare in the region's challenging mountainous terrain. Recognized by FCI provisionally in 2019 with both smooth and wire-haired varieties.

name: Segugio Italiano
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, alert, determined, eager to please
description: An ancient Italian scenthound of noble appearance with medium-sized, athletic build and distinctive hound features. Bred for speed and endurance in hunting, the breed comes in two coat varieties: shorthaired (pelo raso) and wirehaired (pelo forte), renowned for exceptional scenting ability.
history: Descended from ancient hounds of Egypt and Phoenicia, the Segugio Italiano has existed in Italy for centuries. Refined during the Renaissance, this ancient breed has long been prized by Italian hunters for stamina, speed, and exceptional scenting ability in hunting hare and wild boar.

name: Segugio Maremmano
temperament: Alert, energetic, intelligent, courageous, independent
description: Medium-sized Italian scent hound with athletic build and strong hunting instincts, developed for wild boar hunting in the Maremma region of Tuscany. Known for its endurance, determination, and sturdy rustic build designed for challenging terrain.
history: Ancient Italian breed developed in the coastal Maremma region of Tuscany for hunting wild boar and hare. Descended from ancient Roman hunting hounds and perfected by local hunters over generations for tenacity and skill in challenging terrain. Recognized by the FCI and Italian Kennel Club.

name: Serbian Hound
temperament: Energetic, loyal, intelligent, courageous, devoted, tenacious
description: Medium-sized, robust scent hound with a distinctive short, dense red coat and black saddle (mantle), known for stamina, determination, and endurance in mountainous hunting terrain
history: Ancient Balkan scenthound believed descended from Phoenician hounds, developed in Serbia for hunting hare, fox, and wild boar in mountainous terrain. First standard dates to 1924, formerly known as the Balkan Hound, recognized by FCI

name: Serbian Tricolour Hound
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, loyal, alert, devoted, gentle
description: Medium-sized, robust scenthound with distinctive tricolor coat and solid construction, known for its tenacity, keen tracking ability, and lively temperament. Bred for hunting hare, fox, and larger game like wild boar in mountainous terrain.
history: Developed in the Balkan region by crossing local Balkan hounds in early 20th century. First official standard drawn up in 1959 as 'Yugoslavian Tricolour Hound', officially recognized by FCI in 1961, renamed Serbian Tricolour Hound in 2006 after Yugoslavia's dissolution.

name: Serrano Bulldog
temperament: Loyal, protective, confident, courageous, calm, alert
description: Medium-sized, powerful, and muscular molosser-type bulldog breed developed in southern Brazil for guarding, property protection, and cattle work
history: Developed in the highlands of Southern Brazil (Serra Gaúcha region) from dogs brought by European immigrants, primarily the old Bullenbeisser, crossed with local working dogs. The breed was adapted for working with cattle (catching and holding), guarding property, and family protection in the local rustic environment. Recognized by the Confederação Brasileira de Cinofilia (CBKC).

name: Sheepadoodle
temperament: Intelligent, playful, gentle, energetic, friendly, loyal
description: Large crossbreed combining the Old English Sheepdog's gentle herding nature with the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat. Known for a shaggy coat and family-friendly disposition, with characteristics varying depending on genetic inheritance from parent breeds
history: Designer crossbreed developed in the United States in the 1980s, gaining popularity in the late 20th century. Created by crossing Old English Sheepdog with Standard Poodle for a large, family-oriented dog with low-shedding coat qualities

name: Shetland Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, alert, playful, gentle, eager to please
description: Small, agile herding dog from the Shetland Islands, resembling a miniature Rough Collie with a dense double coat, intelligent expression, and alert demeanor. A distinct breed known for its bright, playful nature and collie-like appearance.
history: Developed in the rugged Shetland Islands of Scotland as a small, hardy herding dog for sheep and ponies in harsh conditions. Created by crossing native dogs with Border Collies, Scotch Collies and possibly Spitz breeds. Recognized by AKC in 1911.

name: Shiba Inu
temperament: Alert, confident, intelligent, independent, loyal, courageous
description: An ancient Japanese spitz breed with a small, compact, well-muscled frame and distinctive fox-like appearance. Known for its spirited personality, confident bearing, and agile movement.
history: One of Japan's six oldest native breeds, originally developed for hunting small game in mountainous terrain. Nearly extinct after WWII but saved through dedicated breeding programs. First brought to the United States in 1954.

name: Shih Poo
temperament: Affectionate, friendly, playful, intelligent, outgoing
description: Small designer crossbreed between Shih Tzu and Poodle with soft, wavy or curly coat and hypoallergenic potential. Known for friendly temperament and playful nature, though appearance and temperament can vary as a mixed breed.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the late 20th/early 21st century in the United States by crossing Shih Tzu with Toy or Miniature Poodle. Primary goal was to create a small companion dog combining Shih Tzu affectionate nature with Poodle intelligence and low-shedding coat. Not recognized as standardized breed by major kennel clubs.

name: Shih Tzu
temperament: Affectionate, playful, outgoing, loyal
description: Small, sturdy toy breed with a distinctive short snout, large dark eyes, and luxurious long flowing double coat. Known for their charming, affectionate companion temperament
history: Ancient breed developed in Tibetan monasteries and refined by imperial breeders in Chinese palace courts. Believed to descend from Tibetan breeds, possibly crosses between Lhasa Apso and Pekingese, prized as house pets for centuries

name: Shikoku
temperament: Alert, loyal, energetic, intelligent, courageous, independent
description: A medium-sized, primitive spitz-type hunting dog from Japan with a wolf-like appearance, compact build, and dense double coat. Known for its keen senses, endurance, and spirited yet cautious nature.
history: Ancient Japanese breed developed for hunting wild boar and deer in mountainous regions of Kochi Prefecture on Shikoku Island. Declared Japanese Natural Monument in 1937 for preservation, maintained in relative isolation.

name: Shiloh Shepherd
temperament: Calm, gentle, intelligent, loyal, confident, affectionate
description: Large, well-balanced shepherd breed developed from German Shepherds with a plush double coat, bred specifically for a calmer, gentler temperament and improved health compared to traditional German Shepherds
history: Developed in the 1970s in New York by Tina Barber, who crossed larger old-style German Shepherds with Alaskan Malamutes and other breeds to create a larger, calmer shepherd with focus on sound hips, stable temperament, reduced hip dysplasia, and family companionship

name: Shorkie
temperament: Affectionate, playful, intelligent, alert, loyal, confident
description: Small designer crossbreed combining Shih Tzu and Yorkshire Terrier traits, known for its big personality, silky coat, and spirited yet affectionate nature in a compact companion dog
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States during the 21st century by crossing Shih Tzu and Yorkshire Terrier to create a small companion dog with low-shedding coat and lively, affectionate temperament. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Siberian Husky
temperament: Friendly, outgoing, intelligent, alert, gentle, independent, energetic
description: Medium-sized, athletic working sled dog with thick double coat, striking wolf-like appearance, and erect triangular ears, bred for endurance sledding in harsh arctic conditions
history: Developed by the Chukchi people of Siberia as an endurance sled dog for transportation in harsh arctic conditions. Brought to Alaska in 1908 for sled dog racing, gained fame during the 1925 serum run to Nome, and recognized by AKC in 1930.

name: Silken Windhound
temperament: Friendly, gentle, affectionate, intelligent, playful, calm
description: Medium-sized graceful sighthound with a silky, moderately long coat and elegant athletic build, combining the speed of a sighthound with a trainable, affectionate temperament and gentle nature
history: Developed in the 1980s by Francie Stull crossing Borzoi, Whippets, and small amount of Shetland Sheepdog to create a medium-sized sighthound with longer coat and biddable temperament

name: Sinhala Hound
temperament: Alert, independent, loyal, intelligent, energetic
description: An ancient landrace sighthound from Sri Lanka with a lean, athletic build and short coat. This medium-sized primitive dog is known for its keen hunting instincts, alert expression, and reserved yet loyal nature.
history: Ancient pariah-type landrace native to Sri Lanka, traditionally used by indigenous Vedda people for hunting small game, tracking, and village guarding. Efforts are underway by the Kennel Association of Sri Lanka to standardize and preserve this primitive breed with strong survival instincts.

name: Skye Terrier
temperament: Loyal, courageous, intelligent, calm, confident
description: Long, low-built terrier with flowing double coat that parts down the middle and distinctive feathered ears. Despite its elegant, stylish appearance, this is a hardy and courageous working dog with a dignified demeanor, originally bred for hunting vermin on the Isle of Skye.
history: Developed on the Isle of Skye in Scotland for hunting fox, otter, and vermin in rocky terrain. Gained popularity in the 19th century when Queen Victoria became a fancier. Made famous by Greyfriars Bobby in Edinburgh. Recognized by AKC in 1887.

name: Sloughi
temperament: Independent, gentle, loyal, intelligent, reserved
description: Ancient, elegant North African sighthound with lean athletic build and smooth short coat, bred by Berber and Bedouin tribes for hunting in desert terrain. Known for its noble, graceful appearance and reserved temperament.
history: Ancient sighthound breed developed by Berber and Bedouin tribes in North Africa for hunting desert game such as gazelle, hare, fox, and jackal over vast distances. Highly prized for speed, endurance, and graceful gait in harsh desert conditions. Recognized by FCI and AKC.

name: Slovak Cuvac
temperament: Alert, courageous, loyal, protective, independent, confident
description: Large, robust white livestock guardian dog of the mountain type with thick double coat, known for its imposing stature and courageous, protective nature developed for livestock protection in Slovakia
history: Ancient breed developed in Slovak mountainous regions for guarding livestock for centuries. After World War II, the breed was nearly extinct until Dr. Antonin Hruza established a breeding program to restore numbers. Officially standardized in 20th century and recognized by FCI in 1964.

name: Slovak Rough-haired Pointer
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, energetic, work-focused, adaptable
description: A versatile and noble-looking medium-to-large gundog with harsh, wiry gray coat and muscular, athletic build. Distinguished by its endurance in the field and developed for pointing, tracking, and retrieving on land and in water.
history: Developed in Slovakia after World War II in the 1950s by crossing German Wirehaired Pointers, Weimaraners, and Cesky Fousek with local hunting dogs. The goal was to create a versatile, durable gundog capable of pointing, tracking, and retrieving on land and in water. Officially recognized by the FCI in 1983.

name: Slovenský kopov
temperament: Courageous, energetic, intelligent, loyal, alert, work-focused
description: Medium-sized black and tan scent hound of Slovakian origin, known for exceptional endurance and tenacious courage. Bred specifically for tracking and hunting wild boar in mountainous and rugged terrain, this persistent scenthound combines intelligence with a distinctive work-focused temperament.
history: Ancient national breed developed in Slovakia during the 1950s by crossing local hunting dogs with other European scent hounds. Specifically bred as a versatile scenthound for tracking and hunting wild boar and deer in mountainous terrain. The breed standard was first established in 1963.

name: Smaland Hound
temperament: Calm, intelligent, loyal, alert, energetic
description: Medium-sized Swedish scenthound and the oldest native Swedish scent hound, featuring a compact, sturdy build with a tricolor coat and often naturally bobbed tail. Known for keen hunting ability and alert, energetic temperament.
history: Sweden's oldest native scent hound with origins dating back to the 16th century, developed in Småland province for hunting hare and fox. Modern breed established in early 20th century from local scenthounds and imported European hounds, bred for natural bobtail trait and versatility in dense southern Swedish forests.

name: Small Međimurje Dog
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, alert, loyal, affectionate
