description: A small Croatian hunting dog with compact build and keen scenting abilities, historically known as 'Međimurski ftičar' (little bird hunter). This rare, energetic breed combines primitive-type characteristics with scenthound abilities and makes a loyal companion and courageous watchdog.
history: Developed in the Međimurje region of northwestern Croatia as a hunting companion for small game, traditionally known as 'Međimurski ftičar' (little bird hunter). Known locally for generations as both hunter and companion, provisionally recognized by the FCI in 2022.

name: Small Münsterländer
temperament: Intelligent, eager to please, energetic, loyal, adaptable
description: Medium-sized versatile hunting dog of German origin with distinctive brown and white or brown roan feathered coat, known for excellent pointing and retrieving abilities and elegant appearance
history: Developed in the Münsterland region of Germany in the early 1900s from crossing German Longhaired Pointers with Brittany-type dogs and long-haired German Spaniels to create a versatile, all-purpose hunting companion

name: Smithfield
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, work-focused, alert, independent, courageous
description: Large historical herding dog with thick, shaggy coat originally used for droving cattle to London's Smithfield Market. Considered an ancestor of the Old English Sheepdog, this hardy working breed is now extinct in its original form, though similar types persist in Australia as landrace breeds.
history: Historic droving breed originating in England, named after London's Smithfield Market where these dogs drove and managed cattle. Known for hardiness and intelligence, they were brought to Australia and Tasmania by early settlers. The original English Smithfield is now extinct, but similar types persist as landrace breeds in Australia. Considered a key ancestor to modern Old English Sheepdogs and Bearded Collies.

name: Smooth Collie
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, gentle, friendly, devoted
description: Medium to large graceful herding dog with short, dense, smooth coat and elegant build. Distinguished by its intelligent, gentle expression and lithe, active build, the Smooth Collie shares the same chassis and sweet temperament as its rough-coated counterpart but is an easier-to-groom variety
history: Developed in the Scottish Highlands and Northern England as a sheep-herding dog, sharing ancestry with the Rough Collie but selected for shorter coat favored in milder climates. Popularity surged after Queen Victoria became an enthusiast in the 19th century

name: Smooth Fox Terrier
temperament: Energetic, alert, intelligent, friendly, courageous, playful
description: Compact, energetic terrier with a predominantly white, short, smooth coat and keen, alert expression. This sturdy, medium-sized breed combines intelligence with the bold, lively spirit characteristic of terriers.
history: Developed in England in the 18th-19th century for fox hunting, with their primary role being to 'bolt' foxes from dens. Refined from various terrier breeds and hounds, standardized by English foxhunters. Smooth and Wire Fox Terriers were considered one breed until 1985.

name: Soft Coated Wheaten Terrier
temperament: Friendly, happy, intelligent, devoted, energetic
description: Medium-sized, hardy and agile terrier distinguished by its soft, silky, gently waving coat of warm wheaten color and lively expression. Known for their joyful, affectionate nature with a steady, confident temperament.
history: Ancient Irish breed developed over 200 years as versatile farm dog for herding, guarding property, and vermin control. Known as the 'poor man's Wolfhound,' gained official recognition from Irish Kennel Club in 1937 and AKC in 1973.

name: Spanish Greyhound
temperament: Gentle, calm, intelligent, affectionate, independent, loyal
description: An ancient sighthound breed from Spain known for its lean, athletic build, speed, and gentle, calm temperament. Also called Galgo Español, they come in both smooth and wire-coated varieties and are bred for grace and reserved demeanor.
history: Ancient breed with roots tracing back over 2000 years to Celtic hounds in the Iberian Peninsula. Used by Spanish nobility and rural hunters for coursing hare across open plains and fields. Recognized by FCI and national Spanish kennel club.

name: Spanish Mastiff
temperament: Protective, calm, loyal, intelligent, independent, affectionate
description: A massive, powerful livestock guardian dog with loose skin, thick double coat, and noble demeanor. Known for its deep bark and protective nature, this giant breed combines calm temperament with strong guardian instincts.
history: Ancient livestock guardian breed from the Iberian Peninsula, used for centuries to protect migrating Merino sheep flocks from wolves and other predators. Documented since medieval times and recognized by the FCI.

name: Spanish Water Dog
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, work-focused, athletic, energetic, alert
description: Medium-sized rustic water dog with distinctive curly woolly coat, athletic build, and versatile multi-purpose working ability
history: Ancient all-purpose farm dog from the Iberian Peninsula, used for centuries in Spain for herding livestock, retrieving waterfowl for hunters, and assisting fishermen. Nearly extinct but successfully revived in the 1970s

name: Spino degli Iblei
temperament: Courageous, independent, loyal, alert, intelligent
description: Medium-sized rough-coated Italian hunting dog developed in the Iblean Mountains of Sicily for wild boar and game tracking, characterized by a rustic appearance and strong territorial nature
history: Ancient Sicilian hunting breed developed in the Iblean Mountains for wild boar and game hunting, brought back from near extinction through dedicated recovery efforts and recognized by Italian breed registries

name: Spinone Italiano
temperament: Gentle, patient, loyal, intelligent, docile, affectionate
description: Large, robust, and muscular hunting dog with a distinctive dense wiry coat, expressive beard and eyebrows, and patient gentle expression. Built for all-terrain hunting with a rugged yet versatile build and methodical hunting style.
history: Ancient Italian breed from the Piedmont region dating back to 500 BC, developed as a versatile gundog for hunting in dense, thorny terrain. Known since at least the Renaissance and valued for tracking, pointing and retrieving. The breed faced near extinction after World War II but was revived by dedicated enthusiasts.

name: Sporting Lucas Terrier
temperament: Alert, intelligent, friendly, energetic, courageous
description: Small, sturdy, and active working terrier developed for hunting ability with a harsh weatherproof double coat and compact build, known for its friendly and game nature
history: Developed in the late 1940s by Sir Jocelyn Lucas in England, crossing Sealyham Terriers with Norfolk Terriers to create a smaller, more agile working terrier capable of accompanying hounds

name: Springerdoodle
temperament: Intelligent, friendly, energetic, playful, affectionate, eager to please, loyal
description: Medium to large crossbreed combining the English Springer Spaniel's athleticism and friendly sporting nature with the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat. This energetic, versatile designer breed often has a curly coat and makes an excellent family companion.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed primarily in the United States in recent decades to combine the English Springer Spaniel's friendly, athletic sporting nature with the Poodle's intelligence and hypoallergenic coat qualities. Also seen in the UK but not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Sprollie
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, athletic, eager to please, work-focused
description: Medium to large energetic crossbreed between a Border Collie and English Springer Spaniel, known for its high intelligence, athleticism, work drive, and affectionate nature
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed by crossing English Springer Spaniels with Border Collies to combine the intense intelligence and work ethic of the Collie with the friendly, eager-to-please nature of the Spaniel, creating a versatile companion and sporting dog

name: Stabyhoun
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, calm, loyal, friendly, devoted
description: Medium-sized versatile Dutch pointing dog from Friesland with a distinctive sleek, longish black and white coat and gentle, calm temperament. One of the rarest breeds in the world and considered a Dutch national treasure.
history: Developed in the Friesland province of the Netherlands in the 1800s as a versatile farm dog for pointing, retrieving game, and controlling vermin. One of the rarest breeds in the world and considered a Dutch national treasure.

name: Staffordshire Bull Terrier
temperament: Courageous, intelligent, affectionate, loyal, confident, energetic
description: Compact, muscular, and stocky terrier with broad head and strong build, known for great strength, agility, courage, and affectionate nature with family
history: Developed in 19th-century Staffordshire, England by crossing bulldogs with local terriers for bull-baiting and dog fighting. After these sports were banned, breeders refined the breed into the affectionate and reliable family companion known today

name: Standard Schnauzer
temperament: Intelligent, alert, energetic, loyal, protective, devoted
description: Medium-sized, robust dog with distinctive beard and eyebrows, square build and wiry coat. An intelligent, high-spirited breed that serves as both a versatile working dog and loyal companion.
history: The original Schnauzer breed dating back to the Middle Ages in Germany, developed as versatile farm dogs for guarding property, herding livestock, ratting and exterminating vermin. Recognized by AKC since 1904.

name: Stephens Stock
temperament: Courageous, loyal, intelligent, alert, work-focused, energetic
description: Medium-sized American treeing hound with compact, muscular build and agile frame. Known for courage, intelligence, and tenacity in hunting game from squirrels to bears. Features a short, dense coat typically black with white markings or black and tan coloring.
history: Developed in southeastern Kentucky by the Stephens family in the early 20th century from Mountain Cur stock. Selectively bred for specific 'silent on track, tree with a chop' hunting style for small game, notably squirrel and raccoon. Recognized by United Kennel Club in 1995.

name: St. Hubert Jura Hound
temperament: Gentle, intelligent, devoted, calm, work-focused
description: A medium-sized Swiss scent hound of the Jura variety, distinguished by its noble appearance, long ears, and more substantial head structure indicating St. Hubert Hound influence. Known for exceptional stamina, tracking ability, and endurance in mountainous terrain.
history: A variety of the Swiss Hound originating in the Jura Mountains, descended from ancient St. Hubert Hounds. Developed for tracking hare, roe deer, and fox in mountainous terrain. The 'St. Hubert' type shows heavier build and head structure indicating French St. Hubert Hound influence.

name: Styrian Coarse-haired Hound
temperament: Work-focused, loyal, courageous, independent, alert
description: Medium-sized, rugged Austrian scent hound with a distinctive coarse, wiry red coat, known for its perseverance and stamina as a hunter in mountainous terrain. Bred specifically for tracking wounded game over difficult, high-altitude terrain.
history: Developed in the 1870s in the Austrian province of Styria by Carl Peintinger by crossing a Hanoverian Scenthound with an Istrian Coarse-haired Hound to create a resilient hound capable of tracking boar and deer in high-altitude, rugged mountainous terrain

name: Sussex Spaniel
temperament: Calm, gentle, friendly, loyal, alert
description: A long, low, and muscular spaniel with a distinctive rich golden liver coat and rolling gait, bred for hunting in dense undergrowth. Known for its calm and affectionate nature, this massive spaniel moves with deliberate purpose and steady temperament.
history: Developed in Sussex County England in the 18th-19th centuries by landowners specifically for hunting in dense forests and undergrowth. Nearly extinct after WWII but saved by dedicated breeders. Recognized by Kennel Club in 1872 and AKC in 1884.

name: Swedish Lapphund
temperament: Alert, intelligent, affectionate, friendly, energetic, loyal
description: Medium-sized spitz-type herding dog with dense black double coat and curled tail, bred by the Sami people for reindeer herding. Known for its lively expression, intelligence, and versatile working ability as both herder and guardian.
history: Ancient Nordic spitz breed developed by the Sami people for herding and guarding reindeer in Arctic regions of Sweden. One of Sweden's three national breeds and oldest spitz breeds, officially recognized by Swedish Kennel Club in 1944.

name: Swedish Vallhund
temperament: Alert, energetic, friendly, intelligent, courageous, playful, eager to please
description: Small, powerful spitz-type herding dog with short legs, pointed ears, curled tail, and distinctive wolf-like markings. This ancient and fearless breed is often referred to as 'the Viking dog' and features a weather-resistant double coat.
history: Ancient Swedish breed dating back over 1,000 years to the Viking era, used as an all-purpose farm dog for herding cattle, vermin control, and guarding. Also called Västgötaspets, believed to share ancestry with the Welsh Corgi. Recognized by Swedish Kennel Club in 1943, AKC in 2007.

name: Taigan
temperament: Independent, calm, intelligent, loyal, courageous, reserved
description: Ancient, elegant sighthound from the high-altitude Tian Shan mountains of Kyrgyzstan with long, silky coat and feathered ears, adapted for hunting in mountainous terrain
history: Ancient aboriginal sighthound breed from the mountainous Tian Shan region of Kyrgyzstan, developed by nomadic tribes as a versatile hunter capable of working at high altitudes in harsh conditions. Integral part of Kyrgyz national culture and recognized by FCI as breed No. 337

name: Taiwan Dog
temperament: Alert, loyal, courageous, intelligent, independent
description: Medium-sized spitz-type dog with triangular erect ears, wedge-shaped head, and sickle tail. This athletic, ancient hunting breed is characterized by its keen senses, strong hunting instincts, and unwavering loyalty to family.
history: Ancient indigenous breed descended from hunting dogs of Taiwanese aboriginal tribes in the central mountain regions. Originally used for hunting wild boar and as guard dogs. Nearly extinct but revived through dedicated breeding program in the 1970s. Recognized by FCI (Standard No. 348).

name: Tamaskan Dog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, friendly, athletic, alert, energetic
description: Large, athletic wolf-like breed developed to resemble wolves while maintaining a friendly, trainable domestic dog temperament. Features a thick double coat and striking wolf-like appearance created through selective breeding of Nordic breeds.
history: Developed in Finland in the 1980s by crossing Siberian Huskies, Alaskan Malamutes, and German Shepherds to create a dog with distinctive wolf-like appearance combined with gentle, family-oriented temperament. Part of AKC Foundation Stock Service but not fully recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Tang Dog
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, independent, protective, alert
description: A medium-sized, ancient Chinese landrace dog with athletic build, thick double coat, and curled tail. Known for its primitive appearance, loyalty, intelligence, and protective temperament.
history: An ancient, indigenous Chinese landrace dog that has served for centuries as a versatile farm and family dog in southern China, especially in Hong Kong and the Pearl River Delta. Valued for guarding, hunting, and companionship, this breed is closely related to other primitive East Asian breeds and is currently the subject of preservation efforts to standardize and protect it.

name: Tarsus Çatalburun
temperament: Loyal, intelligent, energetic, alert, work-focused
description: A rare Turkish gundog known for its distinctive split nose and exceptional scenting abilities. This athletic hunting breed from the Tarsus region combines keen pointing skills with a unique congenital split nose believed to enhance scenting ability.
history: A rare landrace gundog from the Tarsus region of Turkey, prized by local hunters for centuries. This ancient breed is one of the few with a congenital split nose, believed to enhance scenting ability. Traditionally used for hunting partridge and small game, it is recognized as a national breed by the Turkish Kennel Club (KIF) but not by major international kennel clubs.

name: Tatra Shepherd Dog
temperament: Protective, intelligent, calm, confident, loyal, independent, courageous
description: Large, powerful, and purely white livestock guardian dog from the Tatra Mountains with dense double coat, imposing presence, and noble bearing. Ancient flock guardian bred for autonomous work protecting sheep from wolves and bears.
history: Ancient breed developed for centuries by Goral highlanders in the Polish Tatra Mountains as independent livestock guardian protecting flocks from wolves and bears. White coat helped shepherds distinguish it from predators at night. Recognized by FCI and The Kennel Club (UK).

name: Teddy Roosevelt Terrier
temperament: Alert, loyal, intelligent, courageous, energetic, playful, friendly
description: A low-set, compact, muscular terrier with short legs and sturdy build, slightly longer than tall with a lively, alert expression. Developed as a small-to-medium-sized hunting and farm terrier for ratting and vermin control duties.
history: Developed in the United States from Rat Terriers and other small terriers, bred for farm work, hunting, and vermin control abilities. Named in honor of President Theodore Roosevelt, who was associated with similar terrier types. Recognized by the AKC in 2022.

name: Telomian
temperament: Intelligent, alert, independent, loyal, energetic
description: Medium-sized primitive spitz-type dog from Malaysia with distinctive upright ears, curled tail, and compact athletic build. Known for its unique climbing ability and originally developed by the Orang Asli people for hunting and pest control.
history: Originated among the Orang Asli indigenous people of Malaysia as a vermin hunter and camp watchdog. Their unique climbing ability evolved from entering raised stilt houses. First documented pair brought to United States in 1963 by anthropologist Dr. Orville Elliot.

name: Tenterfield Terrier
temperament: Alert, intelligent, energetic, loyal, confident, affectionate, playful, friendly
description: Small, compact, agile terrier with a smooth, short coat and alert expression. Strong, active, and athletic, slightly longer than high, this breed was developed for farm work and vermin control with an alert and friendly disposition.
history: Developed in Australia from small terriers and miniature Fox Terriers brought by British settlers in the 19th century to the Tenterfield region of New South Wales. Bred as versatile vermin hunters and companions for farm work and ratting, developing as a distinct breed through the 19th and 20th centuries. The breed name references the town of Tenterfield, NSW, where they were prevalent, and was officially adopted in the late 20th century. Recognized by the Australian National Kennel Council.

name: Thai Bangkaew Dog
temperament: Alert, loyal, protective, intelligent, independent
description: Medium-sized spitz-type dog with a fox-like face, thick double coat, pricked ears, and plumed tail, known for its loyalty and protective nature
history: Originated from Wat Bangkaew monastery in early 20th century from a cross between a native domestic dog and wild canid. Developed as guard dog and companion in rural Thailand, traditionally kept as watchdogs. Recognized by FCI in Group 5.

name: Thai Ridgeback
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, independent, protective, athletic, alert
description: An ancient, medium-sized hound known for the distinctive ridge of hair running along its back, with a muscular, athletic build and an independent, protective nature
history: An ancient, primitive breed native to eastern Thailand, developed for hunting small game, vermin control, and serving as a guard dog. Its isolation in the region preserved its unique characteristics, including the dorsal ridge, for centuries. One of only three breeds with hair ridge.

name: Tibetan Kyi Apso
temperament: Protective, independent, loyal, alert, intelligent
description: Large, powerful Tibetan guardian dog with dense, shaggy double coat, developed for guarding livestock in harsh mountain climates. Known as the 'Bearded Lion Dog', this rugged working breed combines protective instincts with independent temperament.
history: Ancient Tibetan guardian breed developed by nomadic herders to protect livestock and property in harsh Himalayan mountain conditions. Known as the 'Bearded Lion Dog' and considered holy bearers of good luck. Related to but distinct from the Tibetan Mastiff.

name: Tibetan Mastiff
temperament: Independent, protective, intelligent, loyal, calm
description: An ancient and imposing guardian breed with massive size, noble bearing, and dense double coat. Known for its independent nature, impressive presence, and deep, powerful bark.
history: Ancient guardian breed developed in the isolated Himalayas, used by Tibetan nomads and monasteries to protect livestock, villages, and property. Considered a progenitor of many modern mastiff breeds.

name: Tibetan Spaniel
temperament: Intelligent, alert, independent, playful, confident, affectionate
description: Small, compact companion breed with a silky double coat and lion-like mane around the face, known as a 'little lion'. Originally bred by Buddhist monks as monastery watchdogs and companions, featuring an alert expression and confident demeanor.
history: Ancient breed prized in Tibetan monasteries for over 2,000 years, developed by Buddhist monks to serve as companions and watchdogs on monastery walls. Often given as treasured gifts to royalty and dignitaries.

name: Tibetan Terrier
temperament: Affectionate, intelligent, loyal, alert, playful
description: Medium-sized, squarely built dog with profuse double coat and snowshoe-like feet, known as the 'Holy Dog of Tibet.' Originally bred as companions, watchdogs, and herders in Tibetan monasteries with expressive eyes and protective long coat.
history: Ancient breed developed over 2,000 years ago by Buddhist monasteries in Tibet as cherished companions, watchdogs, and herders. Known as 'Holy Dogs' and 'luck bringers,' they were never sold but only given as gifts. Introduced to the Western world in the 1920s.

name: Tonya Finosu
temperament: Alert, intelligent, energetic, loyal, friendly, courageous
description: A small, spitz-type landrace dog from the Black Sea region of Turkey with a dense double coat, pricked ears, and curled tail. Known for alert watchdog capabilities, hardiness, and intelligent, friendly temperament.
history: Native landrace from the Tonya district in Trabzon province, Turkey. Used for centuries as a multi-purpose farm dog, primarily for watchdog duties and hunting small vermin. Hardy breed adapted to the Black Sea region climate, recognized by regional Turkish organizations but not major international kennel clubs.

name: Tornjak
temperament: Calm, protective, loyal, intelligent, confident, alert
description: Large, powerful, and well-proportioned livestock guardian dog with a profuse, dense double coat and weather-resistant qualities. Known for its calm demeanor, strong protective instincts, and confident, sturdy build suited for mountainous terrain.
history: Ancient livestock guardian breed descended from dogs of nomadic peoples in the mountainous regions of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Croatia. Traditionally used to protect sheep and cattle from wolves and bears. Nearly extinct by the 1970s when systematic revival program began. Recognized by FCI in 2007.

name: Tosa
temperament: Calm, courageous, loyal, protective, confident
description: Large, powerful mastiff-type breed originally developed for dog fighting, characterized by a calm, dignified demeanor and imposing physical presence. Known for its stately manner, patience, and courage with a naturally quiet temperament.
history: Developed in mid-to-late 19th century Japan by crossing native Shikoku dogs with Western breeds including Mastiffs, Bulldogs, Bull Terriers, and Great Danes to create a formidable fighting dog with increased size and power.

name: Tosa Ken
temperament: Calm, courageous, loyal, protective, confident
description: Large, powerful Japanese mastiff-type breed developed for dog fighting, known for its imposing presence, calm and dignified temperament, and loyalty to family
history: Developed in Japan during the latter half of the 19th century by crossing native Shikoku dogs with Western breeds including Mastiffs, Great Danes, Bulldogs, and German Pointers to create a larger, more powerful fighting dog. Later bred for calmness and self-control.

name: Toy Fox Terrier
temperament: Alert, intelligent, energetic, friendly, playful, loyal, outgoing
description: Small, athletic, elegant terrier with short, fine coat and alert, lively demeanor, combining toy breed size with true terrier temperament and the spirit of a larger dog
history: Developed in the early 20th century in the United States by crossing small Smooth Fox Terriers with toy breeds including Chihuahuas, Manchester Terriers, and Italian Greyhounds to create a smaller, versatile companion and ratter. Recognized by the AKC in 2003.

name: Toy Manchester Terrier
temperament: Alert, intelligent, spirited, devoted, playful
description: Compact, elegant small terrier with distinctive jet-black coat and rich mahogany tan markings, possessing a spirited and alert terrier nature in a sleek frame
history: Developed in 19th-century Manchester, England by crossing Black and Tan Terrier with Whippet to create expert ratter. The Toy variety was selectively bred down from Standard Manchester Terrier for companionship while retaining ratting ability

name: Transylvanian Hound
temperament: Courageous, intelligent, loyal, energetic, independent, calm, adaptable
description: An ancient Hungarian scent hound of medium-to-large size, known for its athletic build, endurance, and distinctive short black-and-tan coat. This courageous and determined hunter was specifically adapted for mountainous terrain and varied landscapes.
history: Ancient Hungarian breed developed in the Carpathian Mountains region of Transylvania for hunting wild boar and other game in mountainous terrain. Prized by nobility for hunting prowess, the breed faced near extinction after World War II but was preserved by dedicated breeders and officially recognized by FCI.

name: Treeing Cur
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, courageous, loyal, protective
description: Medium-sized, athletic, and versatile hunting dog developed in the rural United States, bred specifically for treeing game and working in rural environments. Known for its intelligence, courage, and strong working ability.
history: Developed in the rural Southern United States by pioneers needing an all-purpose dog for hunting, treeing game, and guarding the homestead. Bred from various cur-type and hound crosses, with breeders prioritizing working ability over physical appearance.

name: Treeing Feist
temperament: Energetic, alert, intelligent, eager to please, work-focused
description: Small to medium-sized, athletic hunting dog developed in the Southern United States, known for its tenacity in treeing small game like squirrels and raccoons with high energy and strong prey drive
history: Developed in the rural Southern United States by crossing terriers and hounds brought by early settlers, bred to be a low-maintenance versatile hunting dog excelling at tracking and treeing small game

name: Treeing Tennessee Brindle
temperament: Intelligent, alert, courageous, energetic, friendly
description: Medium-sized, athletic scent hound known for its distinctive brindle coat pattern, intelligence, and persistent treeing ability. Fast and agile with a loud, distinctive bark, this breed excels at tracking and treeing small game.
history: Developed in the mid-20th century (early 1960s) primarily by Rev. Earl Phillips in the Appalachian and Ozark Mountains from brindle-patterned cur dogs and various hounds to create a pure-breeding scent hound with strong treeing instincts for hunting small game

name: Treeing Walker Coonhound
temperament: Intelligent, confident, friendly, energetic, courageous, alert
description: Medium to large athletic scent hound with distinctive tricolor coat, bred for tracking and treeing raccoons and other game in rugged terrain. Known for swift endurance, intelligence, and distinctive baying voice.
history: Developed from English Foxhounds brought to colonial America in 1742, specifically refined by Tennessee breeder Thomas Walker and the Walker family in the 1800s for speed, endurance, and ability to track and tree raccoons in American terrain.

name: Trigg Hound
temperament: Energetic, independent, alert, intelligent, loyal, work-focused
description: Athletic, medium-sized American foxhound strain developed for exceptional speed, stamina, and endurance in fox hunting. Known for its lean build, distinctive running style, and loud, carrying voice with keen scenting ability.
history: Developed in the 1860s by Colonel Haiden C. Trigg in Kentucky. Created by crossing English Foxhounds imported from Ireland with established American lines, notably Birdsong and Walker hounds, to produce a faster, more driven foxhound with enhanced speed, endurance, and trailing ability.

name: Tyrolean Hound
temperament: Intelligent, independent, energetic, alert, work-focused, devoted
description: Medium-sized, athletic scent hound with a strong, sinewy build and dense double coat, developed for endurance and tracking ability in mountainous terrain
history: Descended from Celtic Hounds, systematically bred in the Tyrol region starting around 1860 for hunting hare, fox, and larger game in mountainous terrain. Emperor Maximilian I used these hounds as early as the 1500s. Officially recognized in 1896 by the FCI

name: Valencian Terrier
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, alert, loyal, affectionate
description: A small to medium-sized, agile terrier from the Valencian Community in Spain with a lean, well-proportioned body, smooth coat, and alert, intelligent expression. Known for its history as a proficient hunter of rats and other vermin.
history: Also known as Gos Rater Valencià or Ratonero Valenciano, developed in the Valencian region of Spain for vermin control on farms and stables. Recognized by Real Sociedad Canina de España and provisionally recognized by FCI in 2022 in Group 3.

name: Valley Bulldog
temperament: Friendly, loyal, playful, courageous, intelligent, affectionate
description: Medium-sized, muscular crossbreed with a broad head and compact body, combining the stockiness of the English Bulldog with the athleticism of the Boxer. Features a brachycephalic (short-nosed) face and is known for its loyal, courageous temperament.
history: Developed in the Annapolis Valley of Nova Scotia, Canada, in the mid-20th century by crossing Boxers and English Bulldogs to create a healthier, more athletic companion with the Bulldog's temperament but fewer breathing and health issues

name: Vikhan
temperament: Alert, courageous, independent, protective, loyal
description: A rare, medium to large landrace dog from Pakistan's mountainous regions, combining sighthound speed with guardian instincts. Athletic build with wiry coat, bred for both hunting and livestock protection. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.
history: An ancient landrace dog originating from Pakistan's mountainous regions including Punjab and Chitral areas. Developed by local tribes for hunting, livestock protection, and companionship. Valued for speed, resilience in harsh terrain, and ability to guard against predators like wolves and leopards. Not currently recognized by major international kennel clubs and lacks formal breed standards.

name: Villano de Las Encartaciones
temperament: Loyal, courageous, protective, intelligent, confident, independent
description: Medium-sized, robust Spanish Molossian-type working dog with a short, dense brindle or black coat, traditionally used for cattle herding, property protection, and big game hunting
history: Ancient breed from the Las Encartaciones region of Basque Country, descended from old Alano dogs. Traditionally used for centuries for handling semi-wild Monchina cattle, property protection, and big game hunting particularly wild boar. Recognized by Spanish kennel organizations and valued for working ability.

name: Villanuco de Las Encartaciones
temperament: Intelligent, alert, courageous, energetic, loyal
description: Small, primitive-type Spanish ratter dog from the Basque Country, known for its agile, alert, and rustic nature, traditionally used for vermin control
history: Traditional primitive breed from the Las Encartaciones region of Biscay province, used on farms as guard dog and adept hunter of rats and vermin. The breed faced near-extinction but has been revived through dedicated conservation efforts. Recognized by regional Spanish breed registries but not by major international kennel clubs

name: Vizsla
temperament: Energetic, affectionate, gentle, intelligent, athletic
description: Medium-sized, energetic and graceful gundog of Hungarian origin with a sleek, golden-rust colored coat and athletic build. Known for their affectionate, gentle nature, they are versatile hunters and eager companions with high energy drive.
history: Ancient Hungarian sporting breed descended from hunting dogs of the Magyar tribes who settled in Hungary over a thousand years ago. Developed as a versatile pointer-retriever by Hungarian nobility, the breed nearly faced extinction after World War II but was preserved and re-established by dedicated enthusiasts.

name: Vizsladoodle
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, affectionate, athletic, loyal, playful
description: Medium to large active crossbreed combining the athletic ability and affectionate nature of the Hungarian Vizsla with the intelligence and low-shedding, curly coat of the Standard Poodle
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States by crossing the athletic Hungarian Vizsla with the Standard Poodle to create a versatile, friendly companion with the temperament and athleticism of the Vizsla and the low-shedding, hypoallergenic coat qualities of the Poodle. Not recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Volkosob
temperament: Intelligent, alert, loyal, protective, work-focused, confident
description: A large, powerful wolf-dog hybrid developed in Russia for military and border patrol duties, combining the physical attributes and enhanced senses of a wolf with the working ability of a German Shepherd. This experimental crossbreed features a robust build designed for specialized working applications.
history: Developed in the early 2000s at the Perm Institute of Internal Troops in Russia through experimental crossbreeding program between a female Carpathian grey wolf named Naida and specially selected German Shepherds. Bred for superior sense of smell, stamina, and strength for military and border security roles.

name: Volpino Italiano
temperament: Alert, playful, loyal, intelligent, confident, energetic
description: Small, compact spitz-type dog with a dense, long white double coat, fox-like expression, and lively, bold temperament. An ancient Italian breed traditionally kept as companion and excellent watchdog.
history: Ancient Italian spitz-type breed dating back to Roman times, descended from the same stock as German Spitz. Favored by Italian royalty and nobility as palace companions and watchdogs. Faced near extinction before being revived by dedicated enthusiasts in the 1980s.

name: Weimaraner
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, loyal, friendly, alert, courageous, athletic
description: Large, sleek, athletic sporting dog with distinctive silver-gray coat and striking amber or blue-gray eyes, known for speed, stamina, and intelligence. Originally bred for hunting large game, earning the nickname 'Gray Ghost' for its coat and stealth.
history: Developed at the court of Weimar, Germany, in the early 19th century by German nobility who sought to create an all-around hunting dog for large game. The breed's parentage was a closely guarded secret for many years.

name: Weimardoodle
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, athletic, affectionate, protective
description: Large athletic crossbreed combining the Weimaraner's hunting instincts and noble appearance with the Poodle's intelligence and hypoallergenic coat. Features a wavy or curly coat and energetic, intelligent temperament.
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the late 20th century by combining Weimaraner and Standard Poodle to create an intelligent, athletic companion with reduced shedding coat

name: Welsh Hound
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, independent, alert, loyal, gentle
description: Medium to large-sized, powerful and athletic scent hound with strong pack hunting instincts, built for stamina and endurance over rough Welsh terrain. Known for its distinct, ringing voice and rugged, weatherproof coat.
history: Ancient breed descended from Celtic hounds, developed over centuries in Wales for fox hunting over difficult terrain. A distinct strain of British Foxhound adapted for Welsh conditions, officially recognized by the Kennel Club in 1922.

name: Welsh Sheepdog
temperament: Intelligent, energetic, loyal, work-focused, independent
description: Medium-sized agile herding dog with weather-resistant double coat, bred as a traditional landrace working dog in Wales. Prized for stamina, intelligence and working ability over standardized appearance, this athletic breed was developed for herding sheep in rugged Welsh terrain.
history: Traditional landrace herding breed developed over centuries by Welsh farmers for herding sheep in mountainous terrain. The Welsh Sheepdog Society was formed in 1997 to preserve this working breed valued for natural ability over appearance, maintained in working registries rather than major kennel clubs.

name: Welsh Springer Spaniel
temperament: Friendly, loyal, energetic, intelligent, affectionate, eager to please
description: Medium-sized, compact gundog with a distinctive red and white coat and straight, silky feathering. Known for its happy disposition, boundless energy, and versatility in the field as a sporting spaniel built for endurance.
history: Ancient Welsh breed developed as a versatile gundog to flush and retrieve game birds, mentioned in Welsh laws dating to 300 AD. Officially recognized as distinct from other land spaniels by The Kennel Club in 1902 and AKC in 1906.

name: Welsh Terrier
temperament: Friendly, alert, intelligent, spirited, energetic, confident
description: Compact, sturdy medium-sized terrier with distinctive harsh wiry black and tan coat and lively, spirited expression. One of the oldest terrier breeds from Wales, known for its rugged build and friendly disposition.
history: One of the oldest terrier breeds, originating in Wales where it was developed for hunting fox, badger, otter, and vermin. Originally known as the Old English Terrier or Black-and-Tan Wire Haired Terrier, it was formally recognized by The Kennel Club in 1886 and AKC in 1888.

name: West Country Harrier
temperament: Energetic, friendly, alert, independent, outgoing, work-focused
description: Medium to substantial scent hound with a broad head, dense short weather-resistant coat, and powerful frame developed for pack hunting hare across the varied rough terrain of Southwest England. This rare or extinct regional variant of the Harrier retains stronger Foxhound influence.
history: Traditional regional pack hound developed in Devon, Cornwall and Somerset for hunting hares over rough terrain, combining local harrier bloodlines with stronger Foxhound influence. Now considered extinct by most authorities, having been absorbed into other Harrier and modern English Foxhound lines over the 20th century. No major kennel clubs officially recognize the breed.

name: West Highland White Terrier
temperament: Confident, alert, playful, loyal, independent
description: Small, sturdy, and confident terrier from Scotland, characterized by its distinctive dense white double coat, bright inquisitive expression, and lively personality
history: Developed in the Scottish Highlands to hunt fox, badger, and other vermin. Colonel Edward Donald Malcolm of Poltalloch is credited with developing the modern white breed after one of his reddish terriers was mistaken for a fox and shot, leading him to breed only for white dogs to avoid future hunting accidents

name: Westiepoo
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, playful, alert, confident
description: Small crossbreed combining the spirited West Highland White Terrier with the intelligent Miniature Poodle, featuring a curly or wavy low-shedding coat, compact body, and lively temperament
history: Designer crossbreed that gained popularity in the United States starting in the 1990s, developed by crossing West Highland White Terriers with Miniature Poodles to create a small, intelligent companion with reduced shedding and the sturdy, confident nature of the Westie

name: Westphalian Dachsbracke
temperament: Alert, intelligent, friendly, work-focused, loyal
description: Short-legged, sturdy German scent hound with tricolor coat, developed for hunting in dense terrain and tracking wounded game
history: Developed in Westphalia region of Germany by crossing Deutsche Bracke with Dachshunds to create a short-legged tracking hound for dense terrain hunting and tracking wounded game

name: West Siberian Laika
temperament: Alert, independent, loyal, energetic, intelligent
description: Medium-sized spitz-type hunting dog with wolf-like features, erect ears, curled tail, and dense double coat. Known for its alertness, versatility, and adaptability, this strong and agile breed was developed for hunting in Siberian forests.
history: Ancient spitz breed developed by the Mansi and Khanty indigenous peoples of Russia's Ural Mountains and West Siberia for hunting versatility. First breed standard established in the 1920s, refined and standardized in the Soviet era, and recognized by the FCI.

name: Wetterhoun
temperament: Independent, loyal, intelligent, alert, calm, protective
description: Medium-sized Dutch water dog from Friesland with a distinctive thick, curly, water-resistant coat. Originally bred for hunting otters and waterfowl, this robust breed combines independence with loyalty and serves as both a versatile hunting dog and natural watchdog.
history: Ancient Dutch breed from the province of Friesland, used for centuries to hunt otters and waterfowl. The name translates to 'water dog.' Nearly extinct after WWII but preserved through dedicated breeding programs and recognized by FCI as a rare national breed.

name: Whippet
temperament: Gentle, affectionate, calm, intelligent, playful
description: Medium-sized, sleek sighthound known for its elegant athletic build with distinctive 'inverted S' shape, explosive bursts of speed, and gentle nature. Originally known as the 'poor man's racehorse'.
history: Developed in Northern England during the 18th-19th centuries by working-class families, crossing small Greyhounds with fast terriers for rabbit coursing and racing. Recognized by AKC and The Kennel Club.

name: White Shepherd
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, alert, confident, courageous, gentle
origin: Germany/Switzerland, developed further in North America
description: A direct descendant of the German Shepherd Dog, the White Shepherd is a large, athletic working dog with erect ears, striking white double coat, and confident demeanor. Known for its noble expression and versatile capabilities as an intelligent herding breed.
history: Developed from white-coated German Shepherd Dogs. When white coats were deemed a fault by German breed clubs in the 1930s, fanciers in North America and Europe continued breeding them separately, eventually establishing the White Shepherd as a distinct breed recognized by various registries including UKC and FCI (as Berger Blanc Suisse).

name: White Swiss Shepherd Dog
temperament: Intelligent, loyal, alert, confident, gentle, devoted
description: A powerful, well-muscled, medium to large white shepherd with erect ears, elegant lines, straight back, and an attentive, intelligent expression. A direct descendant of the German Shepherd Dog with a striking white double coat.
history: Developed from white-coated German Shepherds that were disqualified in Germany in the 1930s. Enthusiasts in Switzerland and North America preserved the variety, leading to FCI recognition as the Berger Blanc Suisse in 2002.

name: Wire Fox Terrier
temperament: Alert, confident, energetic, intelligent, playful
description: Small, compact terrier with distinctive dense wiry coat, beard, and alert expression. This bold, energetic breed was developed for fox hunting and combines confidence with an independent spirit.
history: Developed in England in the 18th-19th century for fox hunting, working to bolt foxes above and below ground alongside hounds. One of the oldest terrier breeds recognized by major kennel clubs.

name: Wirehaired Pointing Griffon
temperament: Friendly, intelligent, eager to please, loyal, energetic
description: Medium-sized versatile gundog with distinctive harsh, wiry coat and characteristic beard and eyebrows. Known as the 'supreme gundog' with excellent pointing and retrieving abilities and an intelligent, friendly disposition.
history: Developed in the late 19th century by Dutch breeder Eduard Korthals, who worked in both Germany and France. His goal was to create the ultimate versatile walking-hunter's gun dog by crossing various pointing breeds, spaniels, and retrievers. Often called 'Korthals Griffon' in his honor.

name: Wirehaired Vizsla
temperament: Energetic, loyal, intelligent, affectionate, gentle
description: Medium-sized Hungarian sporting dog with a distinctive dense, wiry coat and golden rust coloring. Features a robust yet lean build and versatile hunting ability, bred for pointing and retrieving in harsh terrain.
history: Developed in Hungary in the 1930s by crossing the smooth-coated Vizsla with German Wirehaired Pointers to create a wire-coated hunting dog with weather-resistant coat suitable for harsh terrain and cold water

name: Xiasi Dog
temperament: Alert, intelligent, loyal, courageous, energetic
description: A lean, muscular, medium-sized hunting dog from the Guizhou province of China, known for its short wiry coat, loyalty, and athletic ability. This agile breed was prized as a versatile hunting and guard dog, believed to bring wealth and good fortune to families.
history: Ancient breed developed for centuries in and around the village of Xiasi in Guizhou province. Prized by the Miao ethnic region as a versatile hunting and guard dog, believed to bring wealth and good fortune to families. Recognized in China and the AKC's Foundation Stock Service.

name: Xigou
temperament: Calm, loyal, intelligent, alert, independent
description: Ancient Chinese sighthound breed, sometimes called the 'Chinese Greyhound', with a slender build, long legs, and elegant appearance. Known for speed, endurance, and calm demeanor, it comes in both smooth and long-haired varieties with several regional variations.
history: An ancient sighthound breed from China, sometimes called the 'Chinese Greyhound', utilized for thousands of years in hunting and coursing wild game including hares. Has several regional varieties and played an important part in Chinese aristocratic and rural life. Its lineage is documented in Chinese art and literature dating back thousands of years and is recognized by the FCI and AKC Foundation Stock Service.

name: Xoloitzcuintli
temperament: Calm, loyal, alert, intelligent, affectionate, protective
description: Ancient hairless breed from Mexico available in three sizes (toy, miniature, and standard) and two coat varieties (hairless and coated), known for its loyal, calm, and alert temperament
history: Ancient breed dating back over 3,000 years with archaeological evidence of existence in Mexico. Highly prized by Aztecs, Maya, and other ancient cultures as companions, healers, guards, and ceremonial dogs. According to myth, believed to guide souls to the afterlife. Nearly extinct but revived in 1950s and recognized by AKC in three sizes.

name: Yakutian Laika
temperament: Energetic, intelligent, loyal, alert, friendly, courageous
description: Medium-sized, strong and compact Spitz-type sled dog from the Arctic region of Siberia with a thick double coat and expressive eyes, known for versatility, resilience, and endurance in extreme cold climates
history: Ancient aboriginal breed developed by indigenous peoples of Yakutia for sled pulling, hunting, herding reindeer, and companionship in extreme Arctic conditions. Recognized by the FCI in 2019.

name: Yorkipoo
temperament: Intelligent, affectionate, playful, energetic, confident
description: Small designer crossbreed between a Yorkshire Terrier and Toy or Miniature Poodle, known for its intelligent, spirited personality and low-shedding, variable coat type that can range from wavy to curly
history: Modern designer crossbreed developed in the United States by crossing Yorkshire Terriers with Toy or Miniature Poodles. The goal was to create a small companion dog combining the Poodle's intelligence and low-shedding coat with the Yorkshire Terrier's affectionate, compact nature. Not recognized by major kennel clubs

name: Yorkshire Terrier
temperament: Confident, courageous, energetic, affectionate, alert, intelligent
description: Small toy terrier with long, silky steel blue and golden tan coat and confident, spirited personality. Despite their tiny size, these feisty dogs possess the bold terrier temperament in a compact package.
history: Developed during the Industrial Revolution in Yorkshire and Lancashire by Scottish weavers and mill workers. Bred to catch rats in textile mills and mines, combining various terrier breeds including the now-extinct Paisley and Clydesdale Terriers.

name: Yorktese
temperament: Affectionate, playful, alert, intelligent, gentle, devoted
description: Small designer crossbreed combining Yorkshire Terrier and Maltese traits, known for its silky, low-shedding coat, spirited personality, and delicate build
history: The Yorktese, also known as Morkie, is a modern designer crossbreed developed by intentionally crossing Yorkshire Terrier and Maltese to combine the low-shedding, silky coat of both breeds with a friendly and spirited temperament. Not recognized by major kennel clubs

name: Zerdava
temperament: Intelligent, courageous, loyal, energetic, alert
origin: Eastern Black Sea region of Turkey
description: A medium-sized, spitz-type hunting dog native to the Black Sea region of Turkey with a wedge-shaped head and prick ears, known for its agility, intelligence, and courageous nature in hunting game such as wild boar
history: Ancient landrace breed from the eastern Black Sea region of Turkey, primarily the Trabzon and Rize provinces. Developed as a versatile and fearless hunter, traditionally used to track and corner prey like wild boar, bear, and fox. Now being preserved and standardized by the Turkish Kennel Club (KIF).
